Everyone wanted to go on that crucial mission," Yevkurov pointed out in an interview with the WarGonzo project. Assessing the operation, Yevkurov stressed that it demonstrated "the highest level" - a small force performed a daring maneuver to determine the outcome of the battle. "Of course, this causes delight and admiration," he said. According to Yevkurov, the soldiers involved, just like other servicemen, underwent combat and psychological training. "Motivation is of the essence. Walking 15 kilometers inside a pipeline requires a pretty serious psychological attitude. Physical endurance is needed and the very motivation to fulfill the task that was set," he pointed out. "Any soldier must be ready to use the weapon he has in his hands," Yevkurov summarized.

title: Taiwan’s independence incompatible with peace in Taiwan Strait — China’s Defense Ministry
text: BEIJING, April 2. /TASS/. Taiwan’s push for independence will bring the island closer to war, Spokesman for the Chinese Ministry of National Defense Zhang Xiaogang said, commenting on the Strait Thunder-2025A large-scale drills of the People’s Liberation Army of China (PLA). "Taiwan’s independence is incompatible with peace in the Taiwan Strait," Zhang said in a statement. "The aspiration for independence will only subject Taiwan to the threat of a war and incur unbearable suffering on our Taiwanese compatriots," he added. The military official noted that the PLA military exercise around the island is "absolutely legitimate, necessary, and reasonable" and serves as a strong containing factor against the supporters of Taiwan’s independence who are "deliberately provoking a war." According to Zhang, the administration of Taiwan's head Lai Ching-te is fiercely striving for independence and provoking confrontation on both sides of the Taiwan Strait. "We are cautioning the Lai Ching-te administration that those who play with fire will bring the fire upon themselves, and the more problems they create, the sooner they die," the military official stated. On April 1 and 2, the PLA held large-scale military drills near Taiwan. The military carried out combat patrols near the island and practiced measures on establishing comprehensive control over it, blocking key routes and areas. Long-range artillery was used to conduct precision strikes on simulated targets, including seaports and energy infrastructure. Additionally, the PLA engaged the Shandong aircraft carrier to the east of the island. Taiwan has been governed by its local administration since 1949 when the remaining Kuomintang forces led by Chiang Kai-shek (1887-1975) fled to the island after being defeated in China’s civil war. Since then, Taiwan has preserved the flag and some other symbols of the Republic of China that had existed in mainland China before the Communists came to power. Beijing regards the island as one of its provinces and this position is supported by most countries, including Russia. Lately, relations between China’s central government and the administration in Taipei have turned particularly tense.

title: Russia, Vietnam interested in strengthening security cooperation — Lavrov
text: MOSCOW, April 2. /TASS/. Moscow and Hanoi are interested in further boosting cooperation in defense and security, Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said at a press conference following talks with Vietnamese Deputy Prime Minister and top diplomat Bui Thanh Son. "We are interested in further strengthening cooperation in defense and security," Lavrov said. Bui Thanh Son is on an official visit to Russia on April 1-4. Diplomatic relations between the two countries were established on January 30, 1950.

title: China's People's Liberation Army completes exercise near Taiwan — statement
text: SHANGHAI, April 2. /TASS/. China's military has successfully completed the Strait Thunder-2025A exercise around Taiwan, according to Shi Yi, the spokesman for the Eastern Theater Command of the People's Liberation Army. "The PLA's Eastern Theater Command has successfully accomplished all tasks in the joint exercise around Taiwan Island and has comprehensively tested the joint combat capabilities of its troops," Shi Yi stated in a message posted on the WeChat social network. He stated that the forces of the Eastern Theater Command are always on high alert and are continuously enhancing their military training. They are "determined to put an end to all separatist activities of Taiwan's independence supporters." On April 1 and 2, the People's Liberation Army (PLA) conducted large-scale military exercises around Taiwan. The military carried out combat patrols near the island and practiced measures to establish comprehensive control over it, blocking key routes and areas. Long-range artillery was used to conduct precision strikes on simulated targets, including seaports and energy infrastructure. Additionally, the PLA engaged the aircraft carrier Shandong to the east of the island. Taiwan has been governed by its own administration since 1949, when remnants of the Kuomintang forces, led by Chiang Kai-shek (1887-1975), fled to the island after their defeat in the civil war. Since then, Taipei has retained the flag and certain other symbols of the former Republic of China that existed on the mainland before the Communists came to power. According to Beijing's official position, which is supported by most countries, including Russia, Taiwan is regarded as one of the regions of the People's Republic of China. Recently, the relationship between the Chinese central government and the administration in Taipei has become particularly strained.

title: Air defenses down 93 UAVs in Kursk, Belgorod, Rostov regions overnight
text: MOSCOW, April 2. /TASS/. Russian air defense systems intercepted and destroyed 93 UAVs this night over the Kursk, Belgorod and Rostov regions, the Defense Ministry said. "During the past night, air defenses on duty destroyed and intercepted 93 Ukrainian drones: 87 over the Kursk Region, four over the Rostov Region and two over the Belgorod Region," the agency said.

title: Pentagon requests funds for system to detect Russian, Chinese subs off US coast
text: WASHINGTON, April 1. /TASS/. The Department of Defense has asked the US Congress to provide funds for a system that would detect Russian and Chinese submarines off the United States coast, Commander of the US Northern Command Gregory Guillot said. "The trend of approaching our coasts from both Russian and Chinese submarines is increasing, and we need an expanded undersea detection capability to ensure that we are aware of these, and can properly posture to defend against submarine-launched cruise missiles or ballistic missiles," he told the Senate Armed Services Committee. Guillot, who is also the commander of the North American Aerospace Defense Command (NORAD), said he was talking about "sensors." In his words, European allies help Washington in tracking Russian and Chinese submarines. According to him, the numbers of Russian and Chinese submarines detected off the US coast, including off Alaska, and of fly-bys of Russian and Chinese warplanes in the NORAD zone "have actually grown over the last several years despite the activities in Ukraine." He once again described the growing cooperation of Russia, China, Iran and North Korea as a threat. The Russian government stressed on numerous occasions that Russian warplanes and submarines conduct their patrol missions in strict compliance with international norms.

title: International Atomic Energy Agency chief to visit Tehran — Iranian foreign ministry
text: DUBAI, April 1. /TASS/. International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Director General Rafael Grossi will pay a visit to Tehran, as was agreed during his phone call with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, the Iranian Foreign Ministry said. During the conversation, Grossi "requested a visit to Tehran," and the top Iranian diplomat granted the request. "It was decided that, in order for the visit to be fruitful, the sides will discuss and adjust details in the near future," the Iranian Foreign Ministry wrote on Telegram.

title: ZNPP denies reports of incident caused by Ukrainian shelling
text: MELITOPOL, April 1. /TASS/. The Zaporozhye NPP denied it experienced a malfunction, reacting to posts of an accident that circulated on the social media platform Telegram. Some Telegram channels earlier carried posts talking about a mishap at Reactor No. 4, which allegedly happened after a Ukrainian shelling attack breached the plant’s safety circuit. "At this time, the Zaporozhye nuclear power plant is operating as usual. All six reactors are in the state of cold shutdown. There have been no violations of safety limits or requirements. Radiation background at the site and in the observation area is normal and does not exceed natural levels. The information of an incident at the plant that is being spread by fake-news accounts is a lame April Fool's prank," the plant said in a statement on Telegram. According to the statement, the operation of the plant is completely safe, and conditions are in place for the staff to work at maximum efficiency. "We believe it is unacceptable to play around with the tranquility of people in this fashion," the statement said.

title: US to keep supporting Taiwan’s ability to defend itself — Pentagon
text: WASHINGTON, April 1. /TASS/. The United States will continue helping Taiwan and supporting its ability to defend itself, said Lt. Gen. Dan Caine, President Donald Trump’s pick to serve as the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff. "If confirmed, I will continue to support our ongoing efforts to enable Taiwan to maintain a sufficient self-defense capability," Caine said in his written response to questions by US senators prior to his nomination hearings at the Senate Armed Services Committee. Washington severed diplomatic relations with Taiwan in 1979 and established ties with China. The US officially recognizes the one-China principle, but continues provoking China by sending delegations to Taiwan and supplying the island with weapons. The Chinese Foreign Ministry estimates that the total value of US military supplies to the island has exceeded $70 billion over several years. Taiwan has been governed by its local administration since 1949, when the remaining Kuomintang forces led by Chiang Kai-shek (1887-1975) fled to the island after suffering a defeat in China’s civil war. Beijing considers Taiwan one of the PRC's provinces.

title: US ready to consider expansion of number of NATO nuclear missions participants — Pentagon
text: WASHINGTON, April 1. /TASS/. The United States is ready to consider expanding the number of countries that host NATO nuclear missions, said Dan Caine, who US President Donald Trump has tapped to be his Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff. " From a military perspective, expanding NATO allies’ participation in the nuclear deterrence mission in some capacity would enhance flexibility, survivability, and military capability. If confirmed, I will work <...> to evaluate the cost/benefit of such a decision," Caine said in written answers to questions from US lawmakers ahead of his confirmation hearing in the US Senate Committee on Armed Services. Russia has consistently said that the continued practice of so-called joint nuclear missions in NATO runs contrary to the provisions of the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons. Caine confirmed that he supports preserving American tactical nuclear weapons in Europe. "As long as NATO remains a nuclear alliance, it is important for the U.S. to maintain nuclear weapons forward deployed in NATO countries. <...> The US nuclear commitment to NATO remains a core element of our deterrence policy and strategy." Caine said that Washington does not support the idea of its allies developing their own nuclear arsenals. "Nuclear proliferation, even among Allies, significantly limits U.S. ability to manage escalation risk. It could trigger further acceleration of adversary efforts to modernize and expand their nuclear arsenals. Additionally, it would irreparably erode the Treaty on the Non-proliferation of Nuclear Weapons and could encourage proliferation around the world," he said. Caine retired from the army last December as lieutenant general. He served in the United States Air Force. Under American law, only a four-star general, which Caine is not, can occupy the position of head of the Joint Chiefs of Staff. However, the US president has the right to revoke this requirement in some cases. If confirmed by the Senate, Caine will be reinstated in military service and automatically become a four-star general.

title: Russian foreign, defense ministers brief Putin on Ukraine’s moratorium violations
text: MOSCOW, April 1. /TASS/. Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov has said that he and Russian Defense Minister Andrey Belousov briefed President Vladimir Putin about Ukraine’s non-compliance with the moratorium on strikes on energy infrastructure. "At the very beginning of today’s Security Council session, the president, before switching to the main issue of fighting terrorist threats, asked Defense Minister Andrey Belousov and myself to talk about the development of our relations with the Americans with regard to the Ukrainian dossier," the top Russian diplomat told reporters. According to him, "Andrey Removich Belousov laid out how the moratorium, coordinated at the proposal of President Trump back during the March 18 phone call between our presidents, of Russia and the US, is being observed." "Belousov said that the moratorium is not being complied with. Russian energy facilities have been subjected to attacks this entire time, maybe, with one-two day interruptions, and listed all the facilities that have been targeted by Ukrainian drones over the past period, including last night. And I continued along the same lines," Lavrov added.

title: Putin asks at Security Council session to talk about US meeting on Black Sea initiative
text: MOSCOW, April 1. /TASS/. Russian President Vladimir Putin asked at a session with permanent members of the Russian Security Council to talk about the meeting with the United States representatives on the resumption of the Black Sea initiative, Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said on Tuesday. "The president asked to talk about other areas of work with Americans, including about what has been discussed since March 24 in Riyadh, where experts from Russia and the United States met to discuss the revitalization, resumption and revival of the so-called Black Sea initiative, the grain initiative," Lavrov told journalists. "At that time, during the talks in Riyadh, we highly evaluated, the president upheld that assessment, the willingness of the United States to help eliminate the obstacles that have been created by illegal unilateral sanctions on the export of Russian grain products and Russian fertilizers, thereby creating tension in the world markets, first of all, creating difficulties for developing countries, for which access to the food products and fertilizers they need has been restricted," the minister said. Russia and the United States held consultations in Riyadh on March 24 that lasted more than 12 hours. The Russian delegation was led by Grigory Karasin, chairman of the Federation Council’s committee on international affairs, and Sergey Beseda, advisor to the Director of the Federal Security Service. The US delegation included Andrew Peek, senior director for Europe at the National Security Council, and Michael Anton, director of Policy Planning Staff at the Department of State, as well as aides to Special Envoy for Ukraine Keith Kellogg and National Security Advisor Mike Waltz.
mil_orgs: Federation Council’s committee on international affairs

title: Russian stand opens at defense exhibition in Brazil first time in six years
text: MOSCOW, April 1. /TASS/. Rosoboronexport's stand has officially opened at the LAAD 2025 international exhibition of aviation and defense systems in Rio de Janeiro. This marks Russia's participation in the event for the first time in six years, according to the weapons exporter. As the special exporter noted, Russia is showcasing armaments that have been tested in operational environments. Visitors will have the opportunity to view the S-350E Vityaz air defense system, the T-90MS tank, the Su-35 fighter jet, and the BK-16E high-speed transport and landing boat. Russia aims to collaborate with Latin American countries on joint development and production projects involving small arms, drones, and precision-guided munitions. The special exporter intends to discuss potential cooperation projects with regional nations during the exhibition. LAAD 2025 will stay open until April 4.

title: Putin asks Belousov for update on energy strike moratorium
text: MOSCOW, April 1. /TASS/. At a meeting with permanent members of the Russian Security Council, President Vladimir Putin requested an update on the situation with the moratorium on strikes on energy facilities. "Before we begin the discussion, I would like to ask Andrey Belousov a question about how we are implementing agreements related to the cessation of mutual attacks on the energy systems between Russia and Ukraine," he said. The meeting continued behind closed doors.

title: Russia practicing 'flexible pragmatism' in relations with Taliban — expert
text: MOSCOW, April 1. /TASS/. The potential lifting of Russia's ban on the Taliban movement - designated as a terrorist organization and outlawed in Russia - could facilitate working together to fight against the Islamic State (IS), another banned terrorist organization in Russia, according to TASS military observer Alexander Hoffmann. Earlier, the press service of the Russian Supreme Court announced that the Prosecutor General's Office had requested to suspend the ban on the Taliban. Hearings on the administrative case are scheduled for April 17. In late December of last year, President Vladimir Putin signed a law permitting the temporary suspension of an organization's status on the unified list of terrorist organizations. "This is being done strictly in accordance with the new law," Hoffmann explained. "The law now allows for such a measure if the organization has ceased engaging in terrorism and posing a threat to Russia. Moreover, the Taliban are currently in control of Afghanistan and have demonstrated a willingness to combat common adversaries, such as the Islamic State." He emphasized that this move acknowledges new realities and creates a legitimate opportunity for direct dialogue on critical issues, though it does not imply that Russia fully recognizes the Taliban as a legitimate regime. Hoffmann also pointed out that, from a geopolitical perspective, this decision reinforces Russia's role as a key mediator in the Afghan settlement, facilitating greater engagement with Kabul. "Importantly, this decision maintains leverage: should extremist activities crop up again, the ban can be reinstated. Russia is demonstrating flexible pragmatism - cooperation with Afghanistan's de facto authorities does not equate to endorsing their ideology, but rather serves to protect its own interests in Central Asia," the TASS observer stated. "It is crucial for Russia and its neighbors in Central Asia to ensure order in Afghanistan - free from terrorism and drug trafficking. To achieve this, effective communication with those currently in charge, the Taliban, is essential," Hoffmann emphasized. "This legal step will facilitate communication aimed at addressing specific security concerns and ensuring that Afghanistan does not pose a threat to us or our allies," he concluded.

title: Russia ready to help Iran, US build dialogue as 'ship hasn't sailed yet' — senior diplomat
text: MOSCOW, April 1. /TASS/. Moscow is ready to offer assistance to Washington and Tehran in building a constructive dialogue as "the ship hasn't sailed yet," Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Sergey Ryabkov said in an interview withthe International Affairs magazine. The senior Russian diplomat stressed that Moscow opposed acts of aggression and the use of force to resolve the conflict, warning Washington against targeting Iran’s nuclear infrastructure, because the consequences "could be devastating for the entire region." "I’m not even talking about the consequences it could have in terms of Tehran’s actions, which is what our Iranian colleagues have made perfectly clear, so efforts need to be doubled before the ship has sailed in order to try to make agreements on a reasonable basis," he pointed out. "Russia is ready to offer its good offices to both Washington and Tehran, as well as to all those interested in it," Ryabkov added. US President Donald Trump said on March 30 that he intended to impose secondary sanctions on Iran in two weeks unless progress was achieved in talks on Tehran’s nuclear program. "If they don't make a deal, there will be bombing," Trump warned, adding: "It will be bombing the likes of which they have never seen before." Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in turn, said in a sermon on Eid Al-Fitr on Monday that he did not believe in a US military intervention but added that Washington would get a tough response if it tried to raise a revolt in Iran.

title: Chinese forces encircle Taiwan on three sides during drills
text: SHANGHAI, April 1. /TASS/. The Chinese People’s Liberation Army (PLA) continues its large-scale military exercise north, south and east of the island of Taiwan, the PLA's Eastern Theater Command said in a statement. "The Eastern Theater Command has organized Air Force and Navy units in coordination with conventional missile forces and long-range artillery units in the waters north, south and east of the island of Taiwan," the statement notes. The Eastern Theater Command also released a poster showing the island surrounded on three sides with a caption reading: "The threat of a blockade: the more noise, the stronger the deterrence." On April 1, the Eastern Theater Command announced the start of comprehensive drills around Taiwan, which involve ground troops, missile forces, the Navy and the Air Force. The PLA is said to be practicing combat patrols, strikes on maritime and ground targets, and blockades of the transport links and key areas of an alleged adversary. Taiwan has been governed by its local administration since 1949 when the remaining Kuomintang forces, led by Chiang Kai-shek (1887-1975), fled to the island after being defeated in China’s civil war. However, Beijing considers the island to be one of its provinces, a position shared by the majority of countries, including Russia.

title: Russia enhances performance of in-demand weapons during special military op — intel chief
text: MOSCOW, April 1. /TASS/. In the three years after facing external aggression from NATO, Russia has been able to largely boost the performance of in-demand weapons, Sergey Naryshkin, director of the Foreign Intelligence Service (SVR) and chairman of the Russian Historical Society, said. "In the past three years, our country has faced external aggression from the members of the infamous NATO bloc and been able not only to expand the production of in-demand weapons but also to significantly improve their efficiency," he pointed out at a roundtable titled "Inventions for Victory," dedicated to the achievements of Soviet science during wartime, as well as to the researchers and inventors that are making significant contributions to Russia’s victory today. According to Naryshkin, it’s clear that "advancements in the development of domestic unmanned aerial vehicles, robotic systems and new materials will be used in Russia’s civilian industries once the special military operation is over and victory is secured."

title: Kiev loses almost 22,000 troops, mercenaries in March in battles near LPR — expert
text: LUGANSK, April 1. /TASS/. Russian servicemen eliminated over 21,900 Ukrainian servicemen and foreign mercenaries in fighting on the borders of the Lugansk People's Republic in March, which is almost 1,800 more than in February, military expert Andrey Marochko told TASS. "In March, the Ukrainian military continued to resist and engage in terrorist activities. The intensity of hostilities slightly increased compared to February. The Russian Armed Forces have been confidently fulfilling the tasks of the special military operation and liberated territories. <...> Enemy losses during the reporting period amounted to about 21,940 Ukrainian troops and mercenaries, which is 1,735 more compared to the previous reporting period. Russia’s battlegroup North has eliminated the largest number of enemy manpower," he said, analyzing the Russian Defense Ministry's data. Marochko pointed out that during March Russian fighters advanced deep into the enemy's defenses and improved their positions along the entire line of contact. The expert added that the Russian army also destroyed 52 enemy tanks, two RAK-SA-12 MLRS launchers, 338 field artillery guns, 63 electronic and counter-battery warfare stations, 94 field ammunition depots, two fuel depots and over 1,100 different enemy combat vehicles.

title: PREVIEW: Russia takes part in LAAD defense exhibition for first time since 2019
text: MOSCOW, April 1. /TASS/. Russia is taking part for the first time since 2019 in the 2025 LAAD International Defense and Security Fair, which kicks off today in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, and will last until April 4. According to Rosoboronexport, the main purpose of Russia's participation in the 2025 LAAD expo is to discuss technological cooperation projects with Latin American countries. "Today Rosoboronexport is considering more than 100 projects of technological cooperation. Their potential exceeds 30 billion dollars. We are considering their implementation with about 20 partners from the Middle East, Africa, the Asia-Pacific region, the CIS and Latin America," the company's CEO Alexander Mikheyev told TASS ahead of the exhibition. Rosoboronexport noted that at the exhibition in Brazil, Russia will present equipment that is of the greatest interest in Latin America. These include tanks, fighter jets, support vehicles and many others. All the equipment has been tested in real combat conditions and finalized taking into account the feedback from the armed forces and other security agencies performing tasks within the framework of the special operation, the agency emphasized.

title: Russian diplomat sees US defense spending as staggering
text: MOSCOW, March 31. /TASS/. The US spends an extraordinary amount of money on security, defense and law enforcement agencies, Deputy Foreign Minister Sergey Ryabkov said in an interview with the International Affairs magazine. "There has been a steady buildup in US military spending and the spending on security, defense and law enforcement agencies in general. It has already exceeded $1 trillion, a staggering figure. I understand that there are certain urges on the part of the US to try to get more money for collective defense from NATO allies. Some particularly zealous figures there, who are aggressive toward Russia, are sometimes willing to get ahead of the Americans and set themselves heightened 'capitalist emulation’ goals by certain dates," he said, according to a fragment of the interview posted online. The diplomat suggested that the alliance could be making these moves ahead of a new meeting with US President Donald Trump. According to Ryabkov, Russia remembers how heightened socialist emulation goals were sometimes not met due to objective reasons. "Let's see whether this capitalist version of the Communist Youth League in Brussels will have enough drive and enthusiasm to come through on everything, as they plow through the budgetary virgin lands in their countries," he said.

title: Belarusian customs detains US national for illegally crossing border by train
text: MINSK, March 31. /TASS/. Minsk customs officers have apprehended a border violator, a US citizen, who was traveling from Lithuania in an empty railroad car, according to a statement from the State Customs Committee (SCC). "During border control procedures at the Molodechno railway station, Minsk customs officers identified a border violator," the message states. "A 27-year-old US citizen was found traveling in one of the empty cars of the Kena-Molodechno train." The border trespasser was promptly detained by the customs alert group in cooperation with the Molodechno police and paramilitary railway guards. The detainee has since been handed over to border control authorities for further investigation.

title: Russia’s General Staff reports on 133,000 conscripts drafted in 2024 fall campaign
text: MOSCOW, March 31. /TASS/. Russia drafted 133,000 conscripts for military service during the 2024 fall campaign, Deputy Chief of the Russian General Staff’s Main Organizational and Mobilization Department Vice Admiral Vladimir Tsimlyansky told a briefing on Monday. "The call-up assignment established by the Russian president’s decree was implemented on time and in full. A total of 133,000 citizens were called up for military service in the Armed Forces, other troops and military formations," the defense official said. The spring military conscription begins in Russia from April 1 and "today we are holding a briefing devoted to its commencement," he pointed out. "I cannot but note that the upcoming conscription campaign will run in the year of the 80th anniversary of the Victory of our people in the [1941-1945] Great Patriotic War. The year that has been declared Defender of the Fatherland Year by the Russian president," he said.

title: A third of Russian spring draft conscripts to be sent to training centers — General Staff
text: MOSCOW, March 31. /TASS/. A third of spring draft conscripts will be sent to training centers to gain special military skills, Deputy Chief of the Russian General Staff’s Main Organizational and Mobilization Department Vice Admiral Vladimir Tsimlyansky said on Monday. "A third of the conscripts will be sent to training military units and formations where within a term of up to five months they will learn to operate modern military hardware and get a military specialty. Upon the completion of the training, they will be sent to troops in compliance with the skills they have gained," the defense official said in the wake of the spring army draft beginning from April 1 in Russia. Russian President Vladimir Putin has signed a decree to call up 160,000 conscripts for military service in the spring campaign, according to the document published on Monday. "To call up 160,000 Russian citizens aged 18-30 for military service who are not in reserve and are subject to army draft from April 1 to July 15, 2025," the decree says. Last year, Russia’s spring military campaign involved 150,000 conscripts compared to 147,000 in 2023 and 134,500 in 2022. The growth is attributed to the implementation of Putin’s decrees on the stage-by-stage increase in the size of the Russian army. The Russian army comprised about 1 million service members in early 2022. During three years, the Russian army’s numerical strength was raised to 1.5 million service members.

title: Russia to get world’s most combat-fit army after Ukraine conflict, says expert
text: SHANGHAI, March 31. /TASS/. Russia will get the world’s most battle-capable army after the Ukraine conflict that has become unprecedented in its scale and new technology use over the past few decades, Director of the Center for Comprehensive European and International Studies at the Higher School of Economics National Research University Vasily Kashin said on Monday. "Now we are stronger than at any other time. The main thing for us will be to maintain this potential. It is perfectly clear that there will be no retreat after the war. We will have no steep declines in military spending. These expenditures will be lower than during the war but still, they will be much higher than in 2021. And this will be a large, about 1.5 million-strong army wholly based on the experience of the first full-scale war over several decades. This will be the only such army in the world," the expert said at a Russian-Chinese international conference. The confrontation in Ukraine was accompanied by "the use of a large amount of technologies that had never been employed before and that began to play a completely critical role in modern warfare," Kashin said. "In this regard, it can be compared to World War I, i.e. when all the principles of the warfare changed at once," the expert went on to say. "Naturally, the armed forces that have had to participate in such a war they always face very big challenges as was the case with all the participants in World War I. You cannot conduct this war without casualties but, as a result, you will get a new army and new experience," he said. The armies that did not take part in this conflict "will not gain this experience," the expert said. "They can look from the outside but it’s something different," the expert added. In this context, some of Russia’s partners also want "to gain such experience because they understand that this provides an edge," he concluded. The conference organized by the Valdai International Discussion Club and the Center for Russian Studies of East China Normal University is running in Shanghai on March 31 - April 1 on the University’s premises. The forum has brought together over 60 leading experts from Russia and China. Specialists are discussing the issues of the global and Eurasian agendas and bilateral Russian-Chinese relations. The conference is entitled: "Space of Joint Development: Global and Eurasian Dimensions."
mil_orgs: School of Economics National Research University

title: Medvedev calls Russia's latest missiles best cure for Russophobia
text: MOSCOW, March 31. /TASS/. The best remedy for a bad form of European politicians’ Russophobia is the latest Russian missiles, said Dmitry Medvedev, Deputy Chairman of the Russian Security Council. "A significant number of European politicians has succumbed to acute Russomania (also known as Russophobia). <...> Treatment is symptomatic. Traditional medications are generally ineffective. The best therapeutic effect has been observed with the combined use of strong sedative drugs such as Kalibr, Onyx, Iskander and the powerful multi-component tranquilizer Oreshnik. In particularly severe cases, nuclear neuroleptics such as Yars and Sarmat must be used," the politician wrote in his Telegram channel. He said that Russophobia stems from "bipolar affective exaggeration of Russia’s influence on the life of Europe and Europeans" and alternates between manic and depressive phases. "The manic stage is characterized by motor agitation, aggressiveness, and a tendency to provoke and attack stronger opponents without assessing one’s actual capabilities against the target of the attack. Sometimes, it ends in uncontrolled urination and defecation. Examples of patients in the manic stage include [France’s President Emmanuel] Macron, [Britain’s Prime Minister Keir] Starmer, [Finland’s President Alexander] Stubb, and several other European politicians," Medvedev said. He added that the depressive phase is marked by sorrow, emotional and physical fatigue, eating disorders, hypochondria, and self-harm. "A patient in the depressive stage of Russomania may harm himself, including self-sterilization (self-castration). At present, this stage is more commonly observed in women ([head of the European Commission] Ursula von der Leyen, [chief of the European diplomacy] Kaja Kallas) or in hermaphrodites suffering from drug addiction (patients [Vladimir] Zelensky, [Mikheil] Saakashvili)."

title: India, Russia kick off second stage of Indra Navy exercise in Bay of Bengal
text: NEW DELHI, March 31. /TASS/. The second stage of the joint Russian-Indian naval exercise Indra Navy 2025 has begun in the Bay of Bengal, Moscow's embassy in New Delhi confirmed to TASS. Russian sailors have successfully completed preparations for departure to sea and are now conducting maneuvers. "Today marks the start of the second stage of the Indra Navy 2025 exercise, involving the Russian and Indian navies. The Russian side is represented by warships from the Pacific Fleet, including the corvettes Rezky, Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhapov, and the medium sea tanker Pechenga. The Indian Navy is participating with the destroyer Rana and the corvette Qatar," the Russian embassy stated. The second stage of Indra Navy 2025 will last for three days and will focus on joint maneuvering, sailing in formation, and exercises to counter air and surface threats. These exercises will involve live firing of AK-630 and A-190 artillery, as well as large-caliber machine guns, the embassy noted. The ships are expected to return to Chennai port on April 3. On April 10, a group of Russian Navy warships will set sail again to continue their combat service tasks. The Pacific Fleet corvettes Rezky and Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhapov, along with the support vessel Pechenga, arrived in the southern Indian port on March 26. The first Indra Navy exercise took place in May 2003 off India's west coast. Since then, the maneuvers have become a regular event, with the two countries alternating hosting duties. The most recent Indra exercises were held in July 2024 in the waters of the Gulf of Finland.
weapons: corvettes Rezky, Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhapov
mil_units: Pacific Fleet corvettes Rezky and Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhapov

title: Russia’s Battlegroup West downs 17 Ukrainian drones, wipes out 23 drone control centers
text: MOSCOW, March 31. /TASS/. Over the past day, Russia’s Battlegroup West shot down 17 fixed-wing Ukrainian drones and destroyed 23 drone control centers, the spokesman for the battlegroup, Ivan Bigma, told reporters. "Air defense crews destroyed 17 fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles <...>. In addition, Nota and Zakhist-AF electronic warfare systems, 23 UAV control centers, an Israeli-made RADA radar station, three satellite communication terminals, and a field ammo depot belonging to the enemy were wiped out," he specified. According to Bigma, the battlegroup hit two mechanized, one assault and one territorial defense brigades in the Kharkov Region, the Lugansk People’s Republic (LPR), the Donetsk People’s Republic (DPR), and in the Serebryanka forestry. Over the past 24 hours, Ukraine lost roughly 240 troops, two US-made M113 APCs, a UK-made Dingo reconnaissance vehicle, and six pickup trucks, he continued. In counterbattery combat, the battlegroup’s artillery units destroyed four field guns, a Verba multiple rocket launcher, and 10 Ukrainian mortar crews, he added.

title: Camera crew attacked by drone in Donetsk People’s Republic
text: DONETSK, March 30. /TASS/. A camera crew of the Donetsk State TV and Radio Broadcasting Company was attacked by a Ukrainian drone in Gorlovka, the Donetsk People's Republic, the television channel said on its Telegram account. "The camera crew of Vesti.Donetsk television was hit by the Ukrainian drone when performing the editorial board’s assignment in Gorlovka. The drone targeted the vehicle but the filming crew of the Donetsk State TV and Radio Broadcasting Company managed to evade the direct strike. The drone exploded nearby, fragmentations partly hit the car. The driver and the camera man were shell-shocked," the TV channel said.

title: Three drones destroyed in Crimea - Russian Defense Ministry
text: MOSCOW, March 30. /TASS/. Russian air defense assets downed three Ukrainian drones in Crimea, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "On March 30, within the period from 04:50 p.m. to 05:20 p.m. Moscow time (01:50 p.m. to 02:20 p.m. GMT), air defense means on duty destroyed three Ukrainian unmanned aerial vehicles over the territory of the Republic of Crimea, the ministry said.

title: Two drones downed over Bryansk Region — Russian Defense Ministry
text: MOSCOW, March 30. /TASS/. Russian air defense assets destroyed two Ukrainian drones over the territory of the Bryansk Region, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "On March 30, within the period from 03:05 p.m. to 03:20 p.m. Moscow time (12:05 p.m. to 12:20 p.m. GMT), air defense systems on duty destroyed two Ukrainian unmanned aerial vehicles over the territory of the Bryansk Region, the ministry said.

title: First German launch vehicle fell soon after launch
text: BERLIN, March 30. /TASS/. Spectrum, the first German launch vehicle of Isar Aerospace startup, fell shortly after the launch, as streamed on the YouTube channel of the company. "Isar Aerospace's Spectrum launch vehicle successfully lifted off from Andoya Spaceport in Norway. The launch vehicle was terminated after approximately 30 seconds in flight, and the vehicle fell directly into the sea. The launch pad seems to be intact," Isar Aerospace said on the X social network. The launch was made from the Norwegian Andoya launch site. The rocket collapsed in several seconds after the start. The test flight goal was to gather data and experience, the company said. The launch vehicle's ascent to the orbit was considered almost impossible.

title: Over 670 Sudzha residents in Kursk Region evacuated to safe areas since March 12
text: MOSCOW, March 30. /TASS/. Russia’s Defense Ministry and other agencies have evacuated more than 670 residents of the liberated settlement of Sudzha in the Kursk Region to safe places since March 12, the Emergencies Ministry reported on Sunday. "The personnel of the Russian Emergencies Ministry, the Defense Ministry and other agencies have evacuated 676 people [from Sudzha] in the Kursk Region to safe areas," the ministry said in a statement. Rescuers escort local residents to temporary shelters, help with their accommodation, the formalization of documents and the search for their relatives, it said. "The searches for relatives begin promptly already at gathering places - Russian Emergencies Ministry personnel and volunteers offer a possibility to get in touch with relatives by mobile phone, find them in social networks and through their groups where people file requests for the search for relatives. Thus, communication with over 1,700 citizens has been restored since August," it said. At temporary accommodation centers, psychologists render all necessary psychological assistance and support to evacuated civilians. Currently, over 20 specialists, including the Emergencies Ministry staff are involved in this effort. All these measures are being carried out under the existing regime of the counter-terror operation, it said.

title: Russian troops liberate Zaporozhye community in Donetsk region over past day
text: MOSCOW, March 30. /TASS/. Russian troops liberated the settlement of Zaporozhye in the Donetsk region over the past day in the special military operation in Ukraine, Russia’s Defense Ministry reported on Sunday. "Battlegroup Center units completed the liberation of the settlement of Zaporozhye in the Donetsk People’s Republic through successful offensive operations," the ministry said in a statement. Russia’s missile strike on Ukraine’s Security Service (SBU) units and formations of the Main Intelligence Directorate’s special operations forces destroyed up to 170 enemy troops and foreign mercenaries over the past day, the ministry reported. "A missile strike on a temporary deployment area of SBU units and formations of special operations forces of Ukraine’s Main Intelligence Directorate destroyed up to 170 Ukrainian militants and foreign mercenaries," the ministry said. Russia’s Battlegroup North inflicted roughly 70 casualties on Ukrainian troops and destroyed two enemy artillery guns in its areas of responsibility in the Sumy and Kharkov Regions over the past day, the ministry reported. "In the Belgorod direction, Battlegroup North units struck massed manpower and equipment of three mechanized brigades, an unmanned systems brigade, two assault regiments of the Ukrainian army and two territorial defense brigades in areas near the settlements of Krasnopolye, Ugroyedy, Petrushevka, Prokhody and Miropolskoye in the Sumy Region and Slatino in the Kharkov Region," the ministry said. In the Kharkov direction, Battlegroup North units inflicted losses on a motorized infantry brigade of the Ukrainian army near the settlement of Volchansk in the Kharkov Region, it said. The Ukrainian army lost an estimated 70 personnel, an armored combat vehicle, five motor vehicles and two field artillery guns in that frontline area over the last 24 hours, it specified. Russia’s Battlegroup West inflicted roughly 240 casualties on Ukrainian troops and destroyed four enemy artillery guns and a multiple launch rocket system in its area of responsibility over the past day, the ministry reported. "Battlegroup West units improved their forward positions and inflicted losses on manpower and equipment of two mechanized brigades, an assault brigade of the Ukrainian army and a territorial defense brigade in areas near the settlements of Kupyansk in the Kharkov Region, Nadiya in the Lugansk People’s Republic, Redkodub in the Donetsk People’s Republic and in the area of the Serebryanka forestry," the ministry said. The Ukrainian army lost an estimated 240 personnel, two US-made M113 armored personnel carriers, a British-made Dingo combat reconnaissance vehicle, six pickup trucks, four field artillery guns, including three Western-made weapons and a Verba multiple rocket launcher, it specified. In addition, Russian forces destroyed Nota and Zakhist-AF electronic warfare stations, an Israeli-made RADA radar and an ammunition depot of the Ukrainian army, it said. Russia’s Battlegroup South inflicted roughly 300 casualties on Ukrainian troops and destroyed two enemy tanks and two infantry fighting vehicles in its area of responsibility over the past day, the ministry reported. "Battlegroup South units improved their tactical position and inflicted losses on formations of a tank brigade, seven mechanized brigades, a motorized infantry brigade, an assault brigade, an airmobile brigade, a mountain assault brigade of the Ukrainian army and a special operations brigade in areas near the settlements of Chasov Yar, Stupochki, Alekseyevo-Druzhkovka, Seversk, Konstantinovka, Nikolayevka, Pleshcheyevka, Ulyanovka, Novoolenovka and Katerinovka in the Donetsk People’s Republic," the ministry said. The Ukrainian army lost an estimated 300 personnel, two tanks, two infantry fighting vehicles, three US-made M113 armored personnel carriers, four armored combat vehicles, eight pickup trucks, two artillery guns and an ammunition depot in that frontline area over the last 24 hours, it specified. Russia’s Battlegroup Center inflicted roughly 510 casualties on Ukrainian troops and destroyed four enemy artillery guns in its area of responsibility over the past day, the ministry reported. During the last 24-hour period, Battlegroup Center units "inflicted losses on formations of four mechanized brigades, a jaeger brigade, two assault brigades, an unmanned systems brigade, an assault regiment and an assault battalion of the Ukrainian army, a marine infantry brigade and a National Guard brigade in areas near the settlements of Zverevo, Udachnoye, Peschanoye, Kotlino, Mirolyubovka, Shevchenko, Uspenovka, Alekseyevka, Novosergeyevka, Petrovskoye, Krasnoarmeisk and Novotoretskoye in the Donetsk People’s Republic," the ministry said. The Ukrainian army lost as many as 510 personnel, seven motor vehicles and four field artillery guns in that frontline area over the last 24 hours, it specified. Russia’s Battlegroup East inflicted more than 140 casualties on Ukrainian troops and destroyed three enemy jamming stations in its area of responsibility over the past day, the ministry reported. "Battlegroup East units continued advancing deep into the enemy’s defenses and inflicted losses on manpower and equipment of two mechanized brigades, an airmobile brigade of the Ukrainian army, a marine infantry brigade and a National Guard brigade in areas near the settlements of Poddubnoye, Karla Marksa, Volnoye Pole, Razliv, Yalta, Vesyoloye and Zelyonoye Pole in the Donetsk People’s Republic and Maliyevka in the Dnepropetrovsk Region," the ministry said. The Ukrainian army lost more than 140 personnel, a motor vehicle and three field artillery guns in that frontline area over the last 24 hours, it specified. In addition, Russian forces destroyed three electronic warfare stations of the Ukrainian army, it said. Russia’s Battlegroup Dnepr destroyed roughly 65 Ukrainian troops and an enemy fuel depot in its area of responsibility over the past day, the ministry reported. "Battlegroup Dnepr units inflicted losses on formations of three mechanized brigades and three coastal defense brigades of the Ukrainian army in areas near the settlements of Novoandreyevka and Stepovoye in the Zaporozhye Region, Dneprovskoye and Sadovoye in the Kherson Region and the city of Kherson," the ministry said. "As many as 65 [Ukrainian] military personnel, a US-made M113 armored personnel carrier, four motor vehicles, a field artillery gun, an electronic warfare station, an ammunition depot and a fuel depot were destroyed," it said. Russian forces struck Ukrainian military airfields and ammunition depots over the past day, the ministry reported. "Operational/tactical aircraft, attack unmanned aerial vehicles, missile troops and artillery of the Russian groups of forces struck military airfield infrastructure facilities, ammunition depots, an enterprise assembling unmanned aerial vehicles and their storage sites and massed enemy manpower and equipment in 140 areas," the ministry said. Russian air defense forces shot down 78 Ukrainian unmanned aerial vehicles and two JDAM smart bombs over the past day, the ministry reported. "Air defense capabilities shot down two US-made JDAM guided aerial bombs and 78 fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles," the ministry said. Overall, the Russian Armed Forces have destroyed 660 Ukrainian warplanes, 283 helicopters, 49,030 unmanned aerial vehicles, 601 surface-to-air missile systems, 22,596 tanks and other armored combat vehicles, 1,533 multiple rocket launchers, 23,109 field artillery guns and mortars and 33,530 special military motor vehicles since the start of the special military operation, the ministry reported.

title: Russian forces engaged in fierce fighting to retake last few communities in Kursk Region
text: KURSK, March 30. /TASS/. Russian forces are engaged in fierce fighting with the Ukrainian military to retake the last few communities in the Kursk Region, a source in Russia’s defense circles told TASS on Sunday. "In the Sumy direction, fierce battles are ongoing in Guyevo, on the outskirts of the farmstead Oleshnya, and in the area of Basovka. Our fighters continue to push Ukrainian troops out of Guyevo. Fierce battles are underway in the settlement itself and on its outskirts," the defense source said. Combat operations continue in the Sumy Region. In particular, in the area of the settlement of Basovka, Russian forces have dislodged Ukrainian troops from a stronghold and are consolidating their gains, he said.

title: Russian investigators find new facts of Ukrainian army’s crimes in Kursk Region
text: MOSCOW, March 30. /TASS/. Russia’s Investigative Committee has established new facts of the Ukrainian army’s crimes in the Sudzha district of the Kursk Region as follows from local residents’ testimony, Investigative Committee Spokeswoman Svetlana Petrenko said on Sunday. "Investigators of the Russian Investigative Committee have found new facts of crimes committed by Ukrainian militants in the Kursk Region," the spokeswoman said. The testimony of Sudzha district residents reveals, in particular, that the Ukrainian militants that had invaded the Kursk Region placed military equipment in private residential sectors, deliberately set homes on fire and engaged in marauding in the village of Martynovka in August 2024, she said. The Investigative Committee has opened a criminal probe into these crimes under part 2, article 205 (‘A Terrorist Act’) and part 3, article 356.1 of Russia’s Criminal Code (‘Marauding’), she said. The Ukrainian military launched a massive attack on the Kursk Region on August 6, 2024. As Russia’s General Staff reported on March 12, Russian forces have liberated over 86% of the territory occupied by Ukrainian troops in the Kursk Region by now. Residents are being evacuated from the liberated town of Sudzha. In some frontline sectors, Russian forces have entered Ukraine’s Sumy Region.

title: Ukraine’s army loses dozens of combat vehicles northwest of Sudzha in Kursk Region
text: KURSK, March 30. /TASS/. Ukrainian troops have lost several dozen combat vehicles northwest of Sudzha in the Kursk Region, a TASS correspondent reported from the scene. In the area from the settlement of Olgovka to the Sudzha-Korenevo road, there is both combat equipment fully destroyed by Russian forces and largely intact military hardware abandoned by Ukrainian troops upon their escape from that territory. The equipment destroyed by Russian forces includes US-made M113 armored personnel carriers and Abrams tanks, German-made Leopard and Soviet-made tanks and various types of armor. The combat vehicles that have remained largely intact reveal white crosses that the Ukrainian military puts on its equipment as identification marks. "Practically all the equipment was destroyed with its assault force," the Russian reconnaissance counter-terror battalion commander with the call sign Valdai told TASS. The largest amount of the destroyed Ukrainian military equipment remains in the settlements of Malaya Loknya, Nikolskoye, Pogrebki, Lebedevka and along the Korenevo-Sudzha road.

title: Russia’s battlegroup West destroys 58 enemy drones in past day
text: MOSCOW, March 30. /TASS/. Air defense units of Russia’s battlegroup West destroyed 58 Ukrainian drones and a RAM II loitering munition in the past 24 hours, the group’s spokesman Ivan Bigma said. "Air defenses shot down 58 fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles and a RAM II loitering munition of the Ukrainian armed forces," he said. Also in the reported period the battlegroup destroyed eight mortar crews and 24 drone control posts.

title: Kiev troops start using FPV drones of longer range
text: LUGANSK, March 30. /TASS/. The armed forces of Ukraine have started to use FPV-drones with a range of up to 40 kilometers in practically all areas of the frontline, military expert Vitaly Kiselev has told TASS. "Information on new FPV-drones used by the Ukrainian military: they have an increased range of up to 40 kilometers," the expert said. The new drones were detected in the Zaporozhye, Orekhov, Dzerzhinsk, Kremennaya and Kharkov directions of the special military operation, as well as in Russia’s borderline areas of Belgorod and Kursk.

title: Over 9,000 consumers in Russia’s Belgorod Region lose power in Ukrainian drone attacks
text: MOSCOW, March 29. /TASS/. Attacks by Ukrainian unmanned aerial vehicles on Russian energy infrastructure have left over 9,000 consumers in the Belgorod Region without electricity, the Russian Defense Ministry said. The agency explained that, as a result of Ukrainian strikes, a number of high-voltage power lines had been shut down and a power converter sustained major damage. "Over 1,100 domestic consumers have lost electric power in the Grayvoronsky District. At 6:07 p.m. Moscow time (3:07 p.m. GMT - TASS), as a result of an attack by a Ukrainian unmanned aerial vehicle, a high-voltage power line was shut down in the Belgorod Region <...> with the subsequent cut-off of energy supplies to more than 8,000 domestic consumers in the Borisovsky District," the ministry noted. According to the military agency, the Kiev regime has just increased the number of attacks on energy infrastructure in Russia’s Kursk and Belgorod regions, despite claims by Vladimir Zelensky about the purported cessation of attacks on Russian energy facilities.

title: IN BRIEF: What is known about Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy facilities
text: MOSCOW, March 29. /TASS/. The Kiev regime has continued attacking Russian energy infrastructure facilities despite Vladimir Zelensky’s claims to the opposite. Earlier, the Ukrainian military carried out five strikes on the energy facilities in Russia's borderline Belgorod Region. TASS has compiled main facts from the statements by the Russian Defense Ministry. Situation in Belgorod Region A high-voltage power line was damaged in an attack by an unmanned aerial vehicle; A power converter sustained major damage in a drone attack; Over 1,100 domestic consumers lost power in the Grayvoronsky District; A Ukrainian drone attack on a high-voltage power line cut off electricity to more than 8,000 domestic consumers in the Borisovsky District. Situation in Kursk Region Additionally, as it has already been reported, on March 28, around 10:20 a.m. Moscow time (7:20 a.m. GMT - TASS), a strike with HIMARS MLRS rockets was carried out on the Sudzha gas-metering station resulting in a major blaze. The Sudzha gas-metering station has been practically eliminated.

title: Ukraine loses more than 190 troops in Kursk Region over day — top brass
text: MOSCOW, March 29. /TASS/. The Ukrainian army has lost more than 190 servicemen in the Kursk Region over the past 24 hours, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "Over the day, the Ukrainian losses amounted to more than 190 servicemen, two infantry fighting vehicles, an armored fighting vehicle, eight vehicles, an artillery gun, three mortars, as well as six drone control posts and an ammunition depot," the statement said. The Defense Ministry also said that Russian servicemen had liberated the Veselovka settlement in the Sumy Region. According to the milirary, the Russian Armed Forces also hit enemy manpower and equipment near the settlements of Gornal, Guyevo and Oleshnya, as well as Basovka, Belovody, Varachino, Velikaya Rybitsa, Veselovka, Vladimirovka, Vodolagi, Grunovka, Zhuravka, Zapselye, Kiyanitsa, Loknya, Mogritsa, Novaya Sech, Novonikolayevka, Obody, Sadki, Khrapovshchina, Yunakovka and Yablonovka in the Sumy Region. In total, during the fighting in the Kursk area, the enemy lost more than 70,890 servicemen, 402 tanks, 329 infantry fighting vehicles, 290 armored personnel carriers, 2,234 armored combat vehicles, 2,569 vehicles, 590 artillery guns, 53 multiple rocket launchers, including 13 HIMARS and seven US-made MLRS, 26 anti-aircraft missile launchers, 56 units of engineering and other equipment, including 23 engineering demolition vehicles, a UR-77 demining unit, as well as 15 armored recovery vehicles and a command and control vehicle.

title: Russian forces liberate Panteleymonovka, Shcherbaki — top brass
text: MOSCOW, March 29. /TASS/. The Russian armed forces have liberated Panteleymonovka in the Donetsk People’s Republic and Shcherbaki in the Zaporozhye Region, the Defense Ministry reported. "The battlegroup South has liberated the settlement of Panteleymonovka in the Donetsk People's Republic. <...> The battlegroup Dnepr has liberated the settlement of Shcherbaki in the Zaporozhye Region," the statement said. Russian air defenses also shot down a JDAM bomb, a US-made HIMARS rocket and 73 Ukrainian drones over the past 24 hours, the ministry added. TASS has compiled the main information about the special operation progress over the past 24 hours. The Ukrainian armed forces lost up to 185 servicemen in the Belgorod area over the day, the ministry reported. "In the Belgorod area, the battlegroup North defeated the manpower and equipment of eight Ukrainian brigades near the settlements of Krasnopolye, Ugroyedy, Prokhody and Miropolskoye in the Kharkov Region. The enemy lost up to 185 servicemen," the statement said. The Ukrainian military also lost two armored combat vehicles, four vehicles, three field artillery guns, an Israel-made Rada radar station and a Nota electronic warfare station, the ministry added. Russia’s battlegroup Center has wiped out more than 480 Ukrainian servicemen over the past 24 hours, the military said. "The enemy lost up over 480 servicemen, two tanks, six armored combat vehicles, five vehicles and three field artillery guns, including a US-made 105 mm M101 howitzer," the statement emphasized. Russia’s battlegroup West has defeated the manpower and equipment of five Ukrainian brigades over the day, eliminating up to 210 enemy servicemen, the ministry said. "The enemy lost up to 210 servicemen, a tank, two armored combat vehicles, six vehicles, four field artillery guns, as well as two electronic warfare stations and an ammunition depot," the statement pointed out. Russia’s battlegroup East has wiped out up to 150 Ukrainian troops over the past 24 hours, the military said. "The enemy lost up to 150 servicemen, three vehicles, three field artillery guns, as well as an ammunition depot," the statement noted. Russia’s battlegroup South has eliminated out up to 300 Ukrainian troops over the past 24 hours, the Defense Ministry reported. "The enemy lost up to 300 servicemen, a tank, a US-made HMMWV truck, five vehicles, as well as an ammunition depot," the statement said. Russian servicemen hit infrastructure facilities of Ukrainian military airfields, a drone assembly shop, storage sites and control posts over the day, the ministry said. "Russian servicemen hit infrastructure facilities of enemy military airfields, a drone assembly shop, storage sites and control posts, ammunition depots, as well as accumulations of enemy manpower and equipment in 148 areas," the statement noted. In total, since the beginning of the special military operation, the Russian Armed Forces destoyed 660 enemy jets, 283 helicopters, 48,952 drones, 601 anti-aircraft missile systems, 22,569 tanks and other armored combat vehicles, 1,532 multiple rocket launchers, 23,064 field artillery guns and mortars, as well as 33,499 special military vehicles.

title: Russian troops wipe out Ukrainian special units in Guyevo — security forces
text: KURSK, March 29. /TASS/. Russian servicemen wipe out Ukrainian special units in the Guyevo settlement of the Kursk Region to ensure that they don't escape to the Sumy Region, security forces told TASS. "The fighters continue the liberation of Guyevo, where the Ukrainian special units are being eliminated. The enemy expects to hold off the advance of the Russian armed forces as long as possible, hoping to escape through the forest to the Sumy Region. But our servicemen are aware of the Ukrainian military plans and are taking retaliatory measures," the source said.

title: Ukrainian troops control only three settlements in Kursk Region — commander
text: KURSK, March 29. /TASS/. The Ukrainian armed forces now control only three settlements of the Kursk Region, Russian troops continue to liberate the seized territories, Deputy Chief of the Russian Armed Forces’ Main Military-Political Department, Akhmat Special Forces Commander Lieutenant General Apty Alaudinov told TASS. "The enemy now controls three settlements," he said. The Russian Defense Ministry said on Friday that the battlegroup North had liberated the village of Gogolevka in the Kursk Region from the Ukrainian military.

title: Russian army advances cautiously in Chasovy Yar — military expert
text: LUGANSK, March 29. /TASS/. Russian servicemen are advancing cautiously and systematically in Chasovy Yar in the Donetsk People's Republic, thus preserving the lives of servicemen, military expert Andrey Marochko told TASS. "Regarding Chasov Yar: there is a planned liberation of this settlement. First of all, our servicemen are advancing very cautiously. Thanks to such decisions of the command, we are preserving the lives of our troops. <...> We have successes in mopping up the areas that we have liberated, and at the moment these processes continue," he said. Marochko noted that due to the ongoing active fighting in Chasovy Yar, it is currently difficult to count the area liberated and mopped up of Ukrainian troops. On March 8, he told TASS that no more than 15% of the town was under Ukrainian control.

title: Russian army in Kursk Region forging ahead to border with Ukraine
text: KURSK, March 29. /TASS/. Russian units in the Kursk Region are forging ahead steadily to the state border between Russia and Ukraine, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "As part of activity to force the Ukrainian army’s units out of border areas of the Kursk Region, the units of the Russian armed forces are forging ahead steadily to the state border between Russia and Ukraine," the report said. The ministry said earlier that assault units of the Don Cossack air assault regiment based in Feodosia had taken an important fortified strongpoint of the Ukrainian army in the borderline Kursk Region.

title: Ukraine loses up to 260 troops, one tank in responsibility area of Battlegroup South
text: MOSCOW, March 29. /TASS/. The Ukrainian army lost up to 260 troops, a tank and two ammunition depots in the responsibility area of Russia’s Battlegroup South in the past 24 hours, battlegroup spokesman Yevgeny Tretyakov said. "In 24 hours, the enemy lost up to 260 troops wounded and killed. A tank, four armored combat vehicles, five cars, two field branch artillery weapons, including a 105mm L119 howitzer, and two ammunition depots were destroyed," he said. Units of Battlegroup South took more advantageous military lines and positions in 24 hours, inflicted damage on troops and equipment of three mechanized brigades of the Ukrainian army, territorial defense and national guard brigades of Ukraine near Katerinovka, Ivanopolye, Konstantinovka, Zarya, and Malinovka, the spokesman added. "Over the past 24 hours the enemy lost up to 230 troops and three pickups. Three Ukrainian troops yield themselves prisoners. In counterbattery warfare, Russian forces destroyed a US-made 105mm M101 weapon and six mortar squads of the Ukrainian army. Air defenses eliminated 24 fix-wing unmanned aerial vehicles of the enemy. Moreover, 23 unmanned aviation control posts, an electronic warfare station, and a field ammunition depot of the enemy were destroyed," battlegroup spokesman Ivan Bigma said. Units of Russia’s Battlegroup West inflicted damage on groups of two mechanized and one air cavalry brigades of the Ukrainian troops, a territorial defense brigade near Shapkovka, Torskoye, Kondrashovka, Grigorovka, and Yampole, the spokesman added. On the previous day the battlegroup’s units liberated the settlement of Krasnoye Pervoye in the Kharkov Region. "In the Belgorod area units of Battlegroup North inflicted damage on troops and equipment of two mechanized brigades, a territorial defense brigade and an assault regiment of the Ukrainian army near settlements of Krasnopolye and Ugroyedy of the Sumy region. The enemy lost up to 60 troops, an armored combat vehicle, six cars, and six field branch artillery weapons, including a Poland-made 155mm Krab self-propelled artillery system," battlegroup spokesman Yaroslav Yakimkin said. "In 24 hours, the enemy lost up to 120 troops, two Kozak armored combat vehicles, and three cars. In counterbattery warfare, Russian forces destroyed four field branch artillery weapons, including two produced in NATO countries, as well as an ammunition depot. Air defenses downed six fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles," battlegroup spokesman Alexander Gordeyev said. Units of Battlegroup East continued advancing deep into the enemy’s defense line, inflicted damage on troops and equipment of groups of a mechanized brigade, brigades of territorial defense and national guard of Ukraine near Shevchenko, Konstantinopol, and Zelenoye Pole, the spokesman added. "Units of the battlegroup inflicted damage on formations of a mechanized brigade, two coastal defense brigades of the Ukrainian army and a territorial defense brigade near Novodanilovka, Lobkovoye, Stepnogorsk, Novopokrovka, and Antonovka. More than 85 troops, a tank, six cars, two artillery weapons, and three ammunition depots were destroyed," battlegroup spokesman Roman Kodryan said. Battlegroup Dnepr continues carrying out military tasks of the special military operation, the spokesman added. "Units of the group improved their tactical position. Damage was inflicted on formations of two mechanized, one assault brigades, and an assault regiment of the Ukrainian army near Novosergeyevka, Kotlino, Krasnoarmeysk, Petrovskoye, and Novoaleksandrovka. The Ukrainian armed forces lost more than 520 troops, two tanks, an armored combat vehicle, six cars, and four field branch artillery weapons, including a 105mm M101 howitzer," spokesman for the group Alexander Savchuk told reporters.

title: Kiev trying to reassemble front, force Russian army to retreat from Volchansk — expert
text: LUGANSK, March 29. /TASS/. The Ukrainian armed forces’ assault teams consisting of ex-prisoners are trying to reassemble the front and force the Russian army to retreat from Volchansk in the Kharkov Region, military expert Andrey Marochko told TASS. "Regarding the settlement of Volchansk, the Ukrainian militants are trying to force our combat power to retreat from this settlement. Development of combat power is registered in this area, including by assault teams, which <…> consist of ex-prisoners," he said. Marochko told TASS earlier this week that the Ukrainian military command was reinforcing troops near Volchansk by mobilized convicts, with around 300 of them having been sent there.

title: American bases to be targeted if US invades Iran, says Iranian parliament speaker
text: DUBAI, March 28. /TASS/. American military bases in the Middle East, as well as those of its allies, will come under attack if the United States invades Iran, said Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf, Iranian parliament speaker. "The Americans are well aware of how vulnerable they are. If they invade Iran, it would be like adding a spark to a powder keg, and it would ignite the entire region. In such a case, their bases and those of their allies won’t be safe anymore," the senior Iranian lawmaker said in an address on International Quds Day. US Presidential National Security Advisor Mike Waltz stated on February 23 that Washington was considering all options regarding Iran’s nuclear program. US President Donald Trump later stated that Iran could be prevented from developing nuclear weapons either through bombardment or through a deal. He added that he would prefer to make a deal with Tehran. International Quds Day (Quds is the Arabic name for Jerusalem) was established in 1979 on Iran’s initiative as a sign of support for the Palestinian people’s fight for their rights. It is observed annually on the last Friday of the Muslim Holy Month of Ramadan. The goal is to attract global attention to the humanitarian issues in Jerusalem and facilitate efforts to resolve the Palestinian-Israeli conflict.

title: Russia’s FSB reports foiling terrorist attack in a Moscow office building
text: MOSCOW, March 28. /TASS/. Russia’s Federal Security Service (FSB) has arrested a previously convicted man, recruited by Ukraine’s special services, for planning a terrorist attack in a Moscow office building. "The FSB’s department in Moscow has arrested a Russian national born in 1972, with prior convictions, who planned to detonate an improvised explosive device in a crowded area in the interests of Ukraine’s special services," the FSB told TASS. The suspect recounted that in February he was recruited by Ukraine’s special services and took from a stash an improvised explosive device with a timer for planting it in a Moscow office building, according to the FSB. His handler had promised him safe exit for Ukraine where he would have joined hostilities against Russian forces. The attacker has confessed and is currently making statements. A criminal case has been launched.

title: Russian, Indian warships kick off Indra Navy joint maneuvers in India’s south
text: NEW DELHI, March 28. /TASS/. A ceremony of opening the Indra Navy joint maneuvers of the Russian and Indian navies took place aboard India’s destroyer Rana with the participation of commanders, representatives of the crews, the local authorities and Russia’s embassy in the port city of Chennai in India’s south, the headquarters of the drills told TASS on Friday. The Russia-India joint maneuvers offer "another opportunity to check the readiness for joint operations and address a broad range of issues," said Captain 1st Rank Aleksey Antsiferov who commands the Russian Pacific Fleet’s warships that have arrived in India for the naval exercise. "Indra Navy is the format of the drills that allow for exchanging experience, sharpening the skills and practicing joint actions, establishing contacts and bolstering strategic partnership between India and Russia for jointly countering military threats at sea," the commander stressed. "Naturally, our maneuvers are not directed against any states and are of a planned nature and are not related to any changes in the military and political situation in the world. Their basic goal is to maintain stability in the World Ocean where our fleets operate," Antsiferov pointed out, noting that the long-standing friendship between Russia and India "helps jointly cope with any threat in the world." The Russian commander said he hoped that the drills would further develop and become more complex in the future. "I am confident that the drills will result in further bolstering cooperation between our navies," he said. Commander of the Tamil Nadu Naval Area Admiral Satish Shenai highlighted that Russia and India were bound by the rich heritage of cooperation, trust and mutual respect. He emphasized that the Indra drills were evidence of the strength of Indian-Russian friendship. A group of the Russian Pacific Fleet’s ships comprising the corvettes Rezky and Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhapov and the support vessel Pechenga arrived at the Indian port of Chennai for the Indra Navy joint maneuvers on March 27. India’s warships participating in the drills include the Project 61-ME destroyer Rana built in the Soviet Union in 1970-1980 on order from the Indian government and transferred to India in 1982 and the corvette Kuthar designed by Indian naval engineers and constructed in Mumbai. The southern Indian port city of Chennai that accommodates the headquarters of the Tamil Nadu Naval Area has been chosen as the venue for the drills. The exercise will run in two stages. The first stage will take place on the shore and involve meetings on practicing interoperability of naval ships from both countries. This stage will also envision official visits, meetings and sports competitions, sightseeing tours and cultural events. During the second phase due to begin next week, the naval ships will deploy to the sea for three-day maneuvers in the Bay of Bengal. During the joint exercise, the Russian and Indian naval sailors will practice maneuvering, helicopter landing on the ship deck and firings at sea and air targets, the headquarters of the drills told TASS. Russia and India have been regularly conducting Indra drills for already more than 20 years. The drills aim to improve operational interoperability and bolster bilateral naval cooperation. In recent years, land and naval forces alternately participate in the maneuvers. The first Russian-Indian naval maneuvers took place off the western coast of India in May 2003. Eventually, the maneuvers became regular and began to be conducted by Russia and India alternately. The latest Indra Russian-Indian drills were conducted in the Gulf of Finland in July 2024.
weapons: corvettes Rezky and Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhapov

title: Putin appoints deputy commander of Russia’s Navy
text: MURMANSK, March 28. /TASS/. Commander of the 155th Marine Brigade of Russia’s Pacific Fleet Mikhail Gudkov has been appointed deputy commander of the Russian Navy, Russian President Vladimir Putin told him during a meeting with the servicemen of the Arkhangelsk nuclear-powered submarine of the Yasen-M class (Project 885M). The Russian leader added that Gudkov’s purview will also involve the command of marines and all artillery and missile coastal troops.

title: Russian forces to overwhelm Ukrainian troops, Putin believes
text: MURMANSK, March 28. /TASS/. The Russian Armed Forces will completely defeat Ukrainian troops, Russian President Vladimir Putin said during a meeting with the servicemen of the Arkhangelsk nuclear-powered submarine of the Yasen-M class (Project 885M). "Just recently, I said that we will push through. There are grounds to think that we will finish them off," the Russian leader said.

title: Russia to do everything possible to bolster its Navy — Putin
text: MURMANSK, March 28. /TASS/. Russian President Vladimir Putin has said that every effort will be made to boost the country’s Navy during a meeting with the servicemen of the Arkhangelsk nuclear-powered submarine of the Yasen-M class (Project 885M). "We think that the Navy is one of the main guarantees of the country’s security and the overall global strategic stability because we have a certain strategic balance with the US. We will treat it most carefully, will do everything in a timely manner," he said, replying to a question about the prospects of the Navy’s development. The Russian leader added that the strategy of developing the Navy and shipbuilding until 2050 is in the works.

title: Russia will do its best to maintain status of powerful seafaring nation — Putin
text: MOSCOW, March 28. /TASS/. Russia has been a powerful seafaring nation for centuries, and will do its best to maintain the status, Russian President Vladimir Putin said during a meeting with the crew of the Arkhangelsk submarine. "The role of the Navy in general is well-known. For centuries, Russia has been a powerful seafaring nation. And we will do our best to maintain this status," the president said. "We will build new surface ships and submarines, and improve their performance. They need to correspond to present-day standards and to be, of course, combat-ready and effective in the near term," he said. "This is a weapon, and it should serve our country for decades, forming the backbone of our strategic security.".

title: Putin describes floating out of Perm nuclear sub as milestone for Russian Navy
text: MURMANSK, March 28. /TASS/. The floating out of the Perm nuclear submarine, armed with Tsirkon hypersonic cruise missiles, is a milestone in the development of the Russian Navy, Russian President Vladimir Putin said during the ceremony. In his address, Putin described the launch of the fifth sub in the Yasen-M project as "a milestone event." "It is supposed to enter service with the Russian Navy and to start performing its combat tasks next year," the president added. "Yasen-M-class submarines are equipped with modern navigation, communication and hydroacoustic systems. They carry high-precision weapons and are fitted with robotic equipment," the Russian supreme commander-in-chief said. "The Perm has become the first multipurpose submarine to carry Tsirkon hypersonic cruise missiles." He thanked those who designed and built the submarine for their "scrupulous and high-quality work.".

title: Putin gives go-ahead to launch Perm nuclear submarine
text: MURMANSK, March 28. /TASS/. Russian President Vladimir Putin has given the signal to float out the Perm nuclear-powered submarine during his visit to the Murmansk Region in Russia’s north, a TASS correspondent reported.
