"Go ahead!" the Russian leader said. The Perm is a Russian fourth-generation multipurpose nuclear-powered submarine. The sub is the sixth in a series of Project 885/885M Yasen-class submarines being constructed at the Sevmash Shipyard for the Russian Navy. The sub’s keel was laid on July 29, 2016. As follows from the materials for the trip by Russian President Vladimir Putin to Murmansk, "it is the Perm that will become the first regular carrier of Tsirkon hypersonic cruise missiles among Project 885M nuclear-powered submarines and, in this regard, it will differ slightly in its design from its predecessors.".

title: Putin calls Northern Fleet most powerful in Russian Navy
text: MURMANSK, March 27. /TASS/. The Northern Fleet is the most powerful in Russia and reliably ensures the country’s security, Russian President Vladimir Putin said at a meeting on the development of the Arctic on Thursday. "The Kola Peninsula is home to our most powerful Northern Fleet that ensures security of Russia’s Arctic frontiers," Putin said, speaking about the role of the Murmansk Region among other Arctic territories. The Northern Fleet is based in Severomorsk in the Murmansk Region. The Northern Fleet also has its bases in other ports of the region and in the Arkhangelsk Region. The Project 1144 Orlan heavy nuclear-powered missile cruiser Pyotr Veliky is the Northern Fleet’s flagship. The Northern Fleet’s objectives are to protect the integrity and inviolability of Russian territory and keep strategic nuclear forces constantly ready. The Northern Fleet comprises the naval, land and air components and integrates coastal defense troops and also the Air Force and Air Defense Army set up in December 2015.

title: US plans to increase its Arctic military presence — Russian presidential adviser
text: MURMANSK, March 27. /TASS/. The United States intends to significantly expand its military presence in the Arctic, a Russian presidential adviser said on Thursday. "Today, airfield infrastructure for aircraft capable of carrying nuclear and high-precision weapons is under construction in the [Arctic] region, in addition to currently active ballistic missile warning systems and reconnaissance satellite control stations," said Anton Kobyakov, a Russian presidential adviser and the executive secretary of the Organizing Committee of the International Arctic Forum ‘Arctic - the Territory of Dialogue.’ "It clearly points to the US intentions to significantly strengthen its military presence in the region and turn the Arctic into a hub for its strategic operations," he added. Kobyakov also mentioned the situation regarding Greenland, with US ambitions to seize the territory, viewing it as a possible region for geopolitical conflict. The Russian presidential adviser added that the US desire "to control Greenland" was nothing new, and all previous attempts had failed. However, he continued, US President Donald Trump’s recently made statements demonstrate that the United States still sought a possible ownership of Greenland, which is under the authority of Denmark. Trump said on January 7 that Greenland should become part of the United States and warned Denmark about high tariffs if it refuses to relinquish the island. During his first term in office, Trump suggested that the US purchase the island, but both Denmark and Greenland rejected the idea back then, just as they do now. The sixth International Arctic Forum, titled The Arctic - the Territory of Dialogue (IAF), is being held in the Murmansk Region for the first time, running on March 26-27. The forum serves as a key platform for discussing the socio-economic development of Arctic territories, establishing multilateral cooperation mechanisms, and exploring the vast resource potential of the region. One of the central topics is the development of the Northern Sea Route, marking 500 years since its exploration. Russian News Agency TASS is the official media partner of the International Arctic Forum.

title: G7 countries have no right to criticize China on nuclear weapons — defense ministry
text: BEIJING, March 27. /TASS/. Countries of the G7 group of nations are disregarding facts pertaining to China’s nuclear arsenal, and have no authority to criticize Beijing on the subject, Chinese Defense Ministry spokesman, Senior Colonel Wu Qian said. "The G7 statement overlooks facts. It contains deliberate defamation regarding China, and presents an outrageous act of interference in China’s sovereign affairs. We strongly condemn it, and oppose it. China adheres to the policy of no-first-use, and its nuclear strategy is defensive in nature. We maintain our nuclear arsenal at the minimum level necessary to ensure our national security. As far as the subject of nuclear weapons is concerned, G7 should reflect on its own actions, and has no right to criticize China," he said. The spokesman also called upon the G7 nations to move away from the Cold War mentality and ideological biases, and to stop dictating to others. "This approach will not work with regard to the Chinese armed forces," he said. Foreign Ministers of the G7 group of nations previously adopted a joint statement, expressing concern over China’s nuclear expansion.

title: Kiev making black market arms deals with Mexican cartel — Russian diplomat
text: MOSCOW, March 27. /TASS/. Ukraine's black market arms deals extend to Latin America, where Kiev is reportedly bartering with the Mexican cartel, Russian Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova said. The Russian diplomat pointed to reports in Mexican media saying that militants from the Golfo cartel had obtained Javelins, US-made anti-tank guided missile systems the West has previously supplied to Ukraine. "It has turned out that drug dealers in Latin America, with whom Bankovaya street (Zelensky’s office - TASS) apparently conducts barter transactions, are now among the clients of [Ukraine’s Vladimir] Zelensky," Zakharova said. Sending weapons to the Kiev regime actually means "not only wasting money or fighting till the last Ukrainian but also spawning a threat in the backyard, in the West’s backyard," she maintained. The diplomat added that the terrorists who killed more than 40 people in southwestern Niger on March 21 used Ukrainian M120 mortars for attacks against the army.

title: NATO's March 2024 exercises demonstratively anti-Russian — advisor
text: MURMANSK, March 27. /TASS/. The NATO Nordic Response 24 exercises, which took place last year from March 4 to March 15 in the northern regions of Norway, Sweden and Finland, were clearly provocative and anti-Russian, said Anton Kobyakov, Advisor to the president, executive secretary of the organizing committee for preparation and holding of the International Arctic Forum. "The March 2024 exercises were the largest since the end of the Cold War and were demonstratively provocative and anti-Russian in nature. As we can see now, the EU is planning to jumpstart its economy through the military-industrial complex - it is necessary to take this threat seriously," he said. According to Kobyakov, the Arctic Strategy adopted by the US Department of Defense in 2024 describes the Arctic as a zone of strategic competition. "Under the slogans of the Russian threat in the Arctic, the NATO countries are openly increasing their military activity. So, in 2024, about 20,000 NATO members from 13 countries took part in the exercises of the Nordic Response alliance, and they are rapidly bringing their military infrastructure closer to our borders. Deep militarization of Northern Europe, in particular Norway, has been initiated under the slogans of alleged security threats from Russia. <...> Currently, a network of eight radar stations is being deployed throughout Norway, which has been dubbed the ‘eyes of NATO in the North’, and which should expand the alliance's capabilities in continuous surveillance of airspace," he added. The sixth International Arctic Forum titled The Arctic - the Territory of Dialogue (IAF) is being held in the Murmansk Region for the first time. It lasts from March 26-27. The forum serves as a key platform for discussing the socio-economic development of Arctic territories, establishing multilateral cooperation mechanisms, and exploring the vast resource potential of the region. One of the central topics is the development of the Northern Sea Route, marking 500 years since its exploration.

title: Russia displays counter-UAV laser rifle
text: ST. PETERSBURG, March 27. /TASS/. A laser rifle that downs drones at a distance of 500 meters was displayed at the Pan-Russian conference on the protection of civilian facilities against unmanned aerial systems in St. Petersburg. "According to our data, such systems are at the design stage in Russia and are not engaged in the zone of the special military operation. We for the first time display the prototype of a laser rifle that destroys unmanned aerial vehicles. It was designed against FPV drones and operates at a distance of 500 meters. The laser rifle physically affects a drone by the technology of focused ytterbium laser emission. The laser beam destroys the drone body, propeller, engines, battery, etc.," the designer company said. The Ukrainian army already uses devices with laser emission in the zone of military operation, it added. The Russian invention belongs to the Posokh project aimed at countering drones by focused laser emission. The conference is held in St. Petersburg on March 27-28.

title: Kiev’s special services recruiting Russians under cover of RAND Corporation — FSB
text: MOSCOW, March 27. /TASS/. Ukraine’s secret services have been recruiting Russian nationals under the guise of the US-based RAND Corporation, Russia’s Federal Security Service (FSB) revealed. "The FSB has obtained information that representatives of the Ukrainian special services work under cover of a powerful US organization, RAND Corporation, to recruit agents on Russian soil and collect sensitive data about how Russia conducts its special military operation in Ukraine," the FSB specified. According to the FSB, Ukrainian spies approach Russian nationals, preferably those with links to the military, through popular messaging apps in Russia with a proposal to collaborate with the US non-profit organization for a decent remuneration. The Ukrainian recruiters pose as employees of RAND Europe, the US firm’s European arm, the FSB shared. "These contacts pursue the goal of getting access to classified information about the tactical use of strategic aircraft of the Russian Aerospace Forces, as well as the number and deployment of Russian troops taking part in the special military operation," the FSB concluded. The Research and Development Corporation, or RAND, is an American nonprofit think tank that engages in research and provides consulting services to government agencies in the United States, Europe, the Middle East and Asia. The bulk of RAND research on US security has been classified. Its funding sources include US federal and state governments as well as foreign governments, agencies and ministries as well as international organizations, colleges, universities, foundations, lobby unions and other nonprofits.

title: Russia offer joint arms production in Latin America
text: MOSCOW, March 27. /TASS/. Russia plans to develop projects with Latin American countries in joint design and production of firearms, unmanned aerial vehicles and precision weapons. Rosoboronexport said it plans to discuss the cooperation projects at LAAD 2025 show in Rio de Janeiro. "The main aim of Rosoboronexport participation in LAAD 2025 is the discussion of technological cooperation with Latin American countries. The main trend in arms and hardware production has shaped out today - strategic autonomy though the development of national defense industry. Due to it, the share of projects in technology transfer on the global arms market will grow by 2030 95% to 40% of the total volume. In Latin America, we plan to develop joint design and production of firearms, drones, precision weapons, armor, aircraft and naval hardware," Rosoboronexport quoted its CEO Alexander Mikheyev as saying. Russia is already building a plant to produce Kalashnikov rifles in Venezuela. LAAD 2025 will be held on April 1-4.

title: Russia to display battle-tested weapons at LAAD show in Brazil
text: MOSCOW, March 27. /TASS/. Rosoboronexport said it would display battle-tested weapons at LAAD 2025 arms show to be held in Rio de Janeiro in Brazil on April 1-4. The arms trader has not participated in the show since 2019. "Rosoboronexport will present in Rio de Janeiro the latest Russian military products for all arms of forces that trigger most interest in the region. All the exhibits were engaged in real combat. They were upgraded according to the feedback from the military and other law enforcement agencies of the Russian Federation engaged in the special military operation," it said. The arms trader will present Su-57E and Su-35 fighter jets, Il-76MD-90A(E) airlifter, Mi-171Sh and Ka-52E helicopters. Ground forces can see T-90MS tank in a new option and a tank support combat vehicle (BMPT). The air defense display will feature S-400 Triumph, S-350 Vityaz, Tor-M2K launchers and Igla-S manpad. The Navy will be offered BK-16E landing speedboat and BK-10 assault boat, patrol ship of project 22160, and light floating Sprut tank for marines. Rosoboronexport said it is ready to discuss new industrial cooperation projects regarding the displayed weapons in full compliance with modern market trends. It will discuss expanded cooperation and new projects with Latin American government and private companies.

title: Russian, Indian warships to train engaging weapons
text: CHENNAI /India/, March 26. /TASS/. Russian and Indian warships will train weapons engagement and joint maneuvering at Indra Navy exercise, Commander of the Russian group of Pacific fleet warships Captain First Rank Alexey Antsiferov said. The drills will "strengthen interaction between the fleets and the historically solid friendship between India and Russia," he said. They will include "joint maneuvering, various missions, including weapons engagement," he added. "It is not aimed at intimidating or threatening anyone, but at the development of interaction," the commander said. "We constantly train on the background of the changing situation. Warships, weapons and tactics change. Combat training of our fleets is developing and the navymen exchange experience and communicate," he said. The exercise is always important. "No matter what political conditions exist, our friendship has always been at a very high level," he added. Russia is represented by the Rezky and the Aldar Tsydenzhapov corvettes and the Pechenga tanker at the exercise. India’s warships include the Project 61-ME destroyer Rana built in the Soviet Union in 1970-1980 on order from the Indian government and transferred to India in 1982 and the corvette Kuthar designed by Indian naval engineers and constructed in Mumbai. Russia and India have been regularly conducting Indra drills for already more than 20 years. The drills aim to improve operational interoperability and bolster bilateral naval cooperation. In recent years, land and naval forces alternately participate in the maneuvers. In 2024, the exercise was held in the Gulf of Finland.

title: Strategic Yars launchers go on new patrol routes in Bologoye formation
text: MOSCOW, March 26. /TASS/. Strategic mobile Yars missile launchers of the Bologoye formation went on new combat patrol routes, the Defense Ministry said. "Autonomous Yars launchers went on combat patrol routes in the Bologoye missile formation. They intensively maneuver in patrol routes at night," it said. Yars drive a hundred kilometers, build up field positions, organize camouflage and security. They counter hostile subversion groups and cross conditionally contaminated areas. The training prepares Yars crews for long combat patrols in field conditions, it added. Yars launchers operate in new combat patrol routes. "During the scheduled command-staff exercise of the Bologoye strategic missile formation, the regiments armed with Yars ground mobile launchers operate in new combat patrol routes," the ministry said. The launchers train operating in a rapidly changing situation and fulfilling missions in various variants. It guarantees the delivery of orders to all command posts and launchers in conditions of complicated jamming situation, it added. The exercise engaged over 3,000 men and close to 300 hardware units. The mobile Yars RS-24 (SS-27 mod.2 Sickle-B by NATO classification) carries RS-24 intercontinental ballistic missile with multiple warheads designed by the Moscow Institute of Heat Technology (MIT). RS-24 was for the first time launched in 2007. Yars has been supplied to the strategic forces since 2009. RS-24 ICBM was created with technological solutions implemented in Topol-M RS-12M2 missile. RS-24 has better capabilities to break through missile defense and thus strengthened the Russian strategic deterrence.

title: Russian Pacific Fleet’s warships arrive at India’s Chennai port for joint naval drills
text: CHENNAI /INDIA/, March 26. /TASS/. A group of the Russian Pacific Fleet’s ships comprising the corvettes Rezky and Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhapov and the support vessel Pechenga has arrived at the Indian port of Chennai for the Indra Navy joint maneuvers, a TASS correspondent reported from the scene. Captain 1st Rank Aleksey Antsiferov commands the Russian naval group. Indian Navy officers greeted the Russian naval sailors on the shore. The southern Indian port city of Chennai that accommodates the headquarters of the Tamil Nadu naval area has been chosen as the venue for the upcoming drills. The exercise will run in two stages. The first stage will take place on the shore and involve meetings on practicing interoperability of naval ships from both countries. This stage will also envision official visits, meetings and sports competitions, sightseeing tours and cultural events. During the second phase due to begin next week, the naval ships will deploy to the sea for three-day maneuvers in the Bay of Bengal. India’s warships participating in the drills include the Project 61-ME destroyer Rana built in the Soviet Union in 1970-1980 on order from the Indian government and transferred to India in 1982 and the corvette Kuthar designed by Indian naval engineers and constructed in Mumbai. Russia and India have been regularly conducting Indra drills for already more than 20 years. The drills aim to improve operational interoperability and bolster bilateral naval cooperation. In recent years, land and naval forces alternately participate in the maneuvers.
weapons: corvettes Rezky and Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhapov

title: Russia’s Ufa submarine fires Kalibr cruise missiles during drills in Sea of Japan
text: MOSCOW, March 26. /TASS/. Russia’s diesel-electric Kilo-class Ufa submarine of the Pacific Fleet fired Kalibr cruise missiles at maritime and coastal targets during exercises in the Sea of Japan, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "During a scheduled exercise, the Ufa diesel-electric submarine of the Pacific Fleet fired Kalibr cruise missiles at maritime and coastal targets from the Sea of Japan. Before the drills, the submarine covertly approached the designated area," the ministry said. The first missile was fired at a surface target, while the second one found its target at a distance of around 1,000 kilometers, at the Syurkum training range in the Far Eastern region of Khabarovsk. The Ufa was accepted for service with the Russian Navy on November 16, 2022. The sub is the fourth in a series of six Project 636.3 submarines being built for Russia’s Pacific Fleet. The submarines of this Project optimally combine acoustic stealth, target detection range and powerful torpedo and missile armament. They carry the Kalibr high-precision missile system as their basic strike weapon.

title: Nukes stationed in Belarus can be used if necessary — Russian ambassador
text: MINSK, March 25. /TASS/. Tactical nuclear weapons deployed on Belarusian soil could be used if the need arises, Russian Ambassador to Belarus Boris Gryzlov said in a comment for RT released via Telegram. "Tactical nuclear weapons, too, have been stationed in Belarus, and they can be used, if need be. So, I think there is top-level security on the border of the Union State," the Russian diplomat said ahead of the inauguration of Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko. Russian President Vladimir Putin announced his decision exactly on this date two years ago to deploy tactical nuclear weapons in Belarus at Minsk’s request, similar to what the United States had long done on the territory of its allies. On April 25, 2024, Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko confirmed that several dozen nuclear warheads had been stationed in Belarus. Under the latest agreement on security guarantees that took effect in the Union State earlier this month, the two countries view Russian nukes as a key factor in preventing nuclear conflicts or military conflicts involving conventional arms, as well as a means of containment that can only be used as an absolute last resort. Besides, Russian nuclear weapons can be used in response to the use of nukes or other weapons of mass destruction against either of the two Union countries. Also, they can be used in the event of aggression against either Russia or Belarus, with conventional weapons, that may pose a critical threat to their sovereignty or territorial integrity. The decision to use Russian nuclear weapons stationed in Belarus for the latter’s protection should be made according to procedures established by the two countries.

title: NATO’s barbaric bombing of Belgrade must not be forgotten — SVR chief
text: MOSCOW, March 25. /TASS/. Russia, together with Serbia, remembers the inhumane bombing of Belgrade by the North Atlantic Treaty Organization and this atrocity must never be forgotten, Sergey Naryshkin, director of the Russian Foreign Intelligence Service (SVR) and chairman of the Russian Historical Society (RHS), said. "Yesterday and today, Russia and Serbia were recalling these tragic events from 26 years ago. The barbaric bombing of Belgrade, one of the most beautiful cities in Europe, by NATO aviation and the bombing attacks on other towns and villages resulted in numerous casualties and destroyed infrastructure. Certainly, this atrocity must not be forgotten and will not be forgotten," he said at a roundtable on the history of fighting against colonialism. NATO launched a military operation against the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia on March 24, 1999. The bloc’s leadership claimed that the main goal of Operation Allied Force was to prevent genocide against the Albanian population of Kosovo. According to NATO’s data, aircraft from the alliance’s member states conducted 38,000 sorties during the 78-day operation, with more than 10,000 of them carrying out bombing attacks. According to Serbian data, the bombardments killed 3,500 to 4,000 people and injured about 10,000, two-thirds of whom were civilians. Financial damage amounted to $100 billion. Over the three months of bombing, NATO forces dropped 15 tons of depleted uranium in munitions on Serbian soil. Since then, the country has been ranked first in Europe in terms of oncological diseases. During the first decade after the bombings, about 30,000 people developed cancer in the republic, and 10,000-18,000 of them died.
mil_orgs: Federal Republic of Yugoslavia on March 24

title: Over 130 Russians wounded over week due to Ukrainian military attacks, 16 killed
text: MOSCOW, March 25. /TASS/. More than 130 civilians, including four children, were wounded, while 16 people were killed in a week as a result of attacks by the Ukrainian Armed Forces. The highest number of casualties occurred in the Donetsk People’s Republic, Belgorod, Kursk, and Kherson regions, as well as the Lugansk People’s Republic, Ambassador-at-Large of the Russian Ministry of Foreign Affairs Rodion Miroshnik said. "Over the past week, 150 civilians suffered from shelling: 134 people were wounded, including 4 minors, 16 people were killed. <…> Against the backdrop of discussions about striving for a peaceful settlement, Kiev has, for the second week in a row, shown an increase in the number of shellings of civilian infrastructure in Russian territories. <…> The highest number of casualties has been recorded in the Donetsk People’s Republic, Belgorod, Kursk, Kherson regions, and the Lugansk People’s Republic," Miroshnik stated. He added that in total, Ukrainian forces fired over 1,900 various munitions at Russian territory in the past week. Mass drone attacks by the Ukrainian Armed Forces targeted civilian infrastructure in the Saratov and Rostov regions. "Residential homes, energy infrastructure, emergency service transportation, medical and educational institutions, commercial facilities, and parking areas were hit," Miroshnik noted.
mil_orgs: Russian Ministry of Foreign Affairs Rodion Miroshnik

title: Russia’s West battlegroup eliminates 10 Starlink stations, 25 UAV command posts
text: MOSCOW, March 25. /TASS/. Units of Russia’s West battlegroup have destroyed 10 Starlink satellite communication stations and 25 unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) command posts of the Ukrainian Armed Forces within the past 24 hours, while two Ukrainian servicemen have surrendered, Head of the battlegroup’s press center Ivan Bigma told TASS. "Over the past 24 hours, two Ukrainian Armed Forces servicemen have surrendered. A total of 10 Starlink satellite communication stations, 25 UAV command posts, and three enemy field ammunition depots have been destroyed," Bigma stated. According to him, air defense crews of the West battlegroup have also downed 51 fixed-wing UAVs. Additionally, the Ukrainian Armed Forces have lost a robotic platform and 13 mortar teams.

title: Russia’s Yars missile formations to be taken to field positions
text: MOSCOW, March 24. /TASS/. Russia’s Yars mobile land-based nuclear missile launchers will set off on field positions as part of a scheduled check of Russia’s Strategic Missile Troops, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "Scheduled complex readiness checks in the follow-up of the winter 2025 training season began in formations of Russia’s Strategic Missile Troops. As part of the check, the Tagil (Sverdlovsk Region) and the Barnaul (Altai Region) formations are planned to hold command-and-staff exercises during which Yars missile regiments will be taken to field positions," the ministry said. Military units will also practice engineering support and radiological, chemical and biological protection tasks during the maneuvers.

title: NATO atrocities against Serbs remain unpunished, but Belgrade never forgets them — PM
text: BELGRADE, March 24. /TASS/. The North Atlantic Alliance has not yet been held accountable for its assault against Yugoslavia, but Serbs will never forget the atrocities against their homeland, Serbian Prime Minister Milos Vucevic said. "On this day, March 24, 1999, Nineteen big ones attacked Serbia, violating international law and the decisions of the United Nations Security Council. For the next 78 days they ravaged our homeland, killed our children, damaged our bridges, factories, schools and hospitals, ruined our media... We still remember the sound of air raid sirens and unceasing explosions. NATO still enjoys impunity for this offense. We are a nation that always remembers its victims and its history. We keep the memory of all those who bravely defended our country and sacrificed their lives for freedom and for the future of our children," Vucevic said on his X social media page. "Serbia is a free and proud country! We will forget NATO's crime only if we are dead! Glory and eternity to innocent victims! Serbia remembers the tragedy and mourns!" the prime minister pointed out. NATO's aggression against the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia began on March 24, 1999, and lasted 78 days. The main reason for the NATO offensive campaign, Operation Allied Force, was to prevent the genocide of the Albanian population in Kosovo. According to NATO, during the operation, the aviation of NATO countries carried out 38,000 military flights, with over 10,000 of them aimed at delivering bombs. According to Serbian information, 3,500 to 4,000 people were killed and about 10,000 others were injured as a result of the bombing. Two-thirds of these people were civilians. Material damage from strikes amounted to 100 billion dollars. During the three months of bombing of Yugoslavia by NATO, 15 tons of depleted uranium had been dropped in shells on the territory of Serbia. After that, the country took the first place in the number of cancer cases in Europe. During the first ten years after the bombings, 30,000 people contracted cancer, 10,000 to 18,000 of whom died.

title: Russia may become channel for Iran-US communication on nuclear program — expert
text: MOSCOW, March 24. /TASS/. Russia can serve as an intermediary between Iran and the United States regarding discussions about Iran's nuclear program, Yury Lyamin, a senior researcher at the Center for Analysis of Strategies and Technologies, said in an interview with TASS. "The situation surrounding the Iranian nuclear program is currently deteriorating, which is understandably worrisome. Nevertheless, Russia can act as a communication channel between Tehran and Washington. The approach taken by US President Donald Trump's administration does not align well with the expectations of the Iranian leadership. Consequently, Moscow could facilitate communication through which some agreements might be reached," he stated during a discussion at the Valdai International Club on the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership Treaty between Russia and Iran. Lyamin pointed out that the ongoing pressure on Iran from the United States and Israel, including threats to target Iran's nuclear facilities, "could lead to a significant escalation in the region." He added, "Thus, the possibility of a serious conflict in the near future cannot be dismissed, and this is something that should be avoided at all costs." He expressed skepticism about Russia's ability to effectively mediate on the Iranian nuclear issue unless the United States moves away from its previously maximalist stance. "This includes demands such as Iran's complete abandonment of its nuclear program, the withdrawal of support for regional allies, and stringent restrictions on Iran's missile program," Lyamin explained. "In such a situation, it would be extremely challenging for Russia to take any action, as these demands are entirely unacceptable to Iran." Earlier, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated that the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA) regarding Iran's nuclear program can no longer be restored to its original form; however, its framework may serve as the basis for a new agreement between Iran and the West. On March 7, US President Donald Trump posted on his Truth Social media platform that he had sent a message to Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei proposing talks on Tehran's nuclear program. In response, Khamenei remarked that Iran would not negotiate with the US since Washington prioritized its own interests. The Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA) was signed in 2015 by the permanent members of the UN Security Council (P5) and Germany with Iran to address the crisis surrounding its nuclear research. In 2018, during Trump's first term, the US withdrew from the agreement and reinstated all anti-Iran sanctions that had been lifted under the JCPOA. In retaliation, the Iranian parliament enacted a law in 2020 known as the Strategic Action Plan to Lift Sanctions and Protect the Interests of the Iranian Nation. This law reduced several commitments under the nuclear deal, including the cessation of IAEA inspections beyond the parameters of the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty Safeguards Agreement between the IAEA and Iran, as well as the prohibition of stringent monitoring measures.
mil_orgs: Center for Analysis of Strategies and Technologies

title: Belarusian armed forces kick off combat readiness check — Defense Ministry
text: MINSK, March 24. /TASS/. The Belarusian armed forces have kicked off a combat readiness check, as ordered by President Alexander Lukashenko, the country’s Defense Ministry said in a statement. "The state secretariat of the Belarusian Security Council has launched a combat readiness check of the national armed forces in accordance with orders from the president," the statement reads. According to the ministry, the personnel of a military unit will perform a range of activities aimed at preparing weapons and hardware for combat training missions, including deploying them to a designated army training ground. "The troops will then carry out activities involving engineering equipment, performing camouflage, security and defensive missions in assigned areas, as well as conducting live-fire exercises using standard weapons," the ministry specified. In order to simulate a combat situation, attack and reconnaissance drones, as well as planes, are planned to be used, meant to recreate a contemporary military conflict. The Belarusian Defense Ministry pointed out that during the combat readiness check military equipment will be moved, so temporary restrictions may be introduced for civilian traffic along public roads and in certain areas.

title: Black Sea navigation security to be discussed in Riyadh — Kremlin
text: MOSCOW, March 24. /TASS/. The discussions in Riyadh will focus primarily on the security of navigation in the Black Sea, Russian Presidential Spokesman Dmitry Peskov told reporters. "We proceed from the fact that the issue of the Black Sea initiative and all topics related to the resumption of this initiative are on the agenda today, it was a proposal by the [US] President [Donald] Trump, and [Russian] President [Vladimir] Putin has agreed to this. That is exactly the mandate with which our delegation went to Riyadh," the Kremlin spokesman said. He said the Black Sea initiative is primarily aimed at the safety of navigation. "If you remember it in its previous form <...>, there was quite a large part of the obligations to our side, which, by the way, were not fulfilled last time. This is why it will also be on the agenda today," he said. Russian-US consultations on restarting the Black Sea grain initiative are taking place in Riyadh (Saudi Arabia) today. Russia is represented by Grigory Karasin, Chairman of the Federation Council Committee on International Affairs, and Sergey Beseda, Advisor to the Director of the FSB. The Black Sea Initiative, also known as the Grain Deal, involved the export of Ukrainian grain from the Black Sea ports and the normalization of exports of Russian agricultural products and fertilizers to global markets. Contrary to the agreements, the West exported most of the Ukrainian grain to its own countries, while the main objective of the deal - the supply of grain to nations in need - was never achieved. At the same time, obligations to Russia were not fulfilled, so in July 2023, Moscow pulled out of the deal.
mil_orgs: Federation Council Committee on International Affairs

title: Russian troops liberated settlement of Sribnoye in DPR — Defense Ministry
text: MOSCOW, March 23. /TASS/. Russian troops liberated the settlement of Sribnoye in the Donetsk People's Republic, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "As a result of active and decisive actions by units of the Battlegroup Center, the settlement of Sribnoye in the Donetsk People's Republic was liberated," the ministry said. In the course of the special military operation in Ukraine, Russian troops hit military airfield infrastructure, unmanned aerial vehicle operator training centers, their production workshops and storage sites, as well as concentrations of manpower and equipment of the Ukrainian armed forces and foreign mercenaries in 138 districts over the past 24 hours, the ministry added. "Tactical aviation, unmanned aerial vehicles, missile troops and artillery of the Russian armed forces hit military airfield infrastructure facilities, unmanned aerial vehicle operator training centers, their production workshops and storage sites, as well as concentrations of manpower and equipment of the Ukrainian armed forces and foreign mercenaries in 138 districts," the report says. The Ukrainian armed forces have lost up to 60 servicemen in the area of responsibility of the Battlegroup North (Belgorod direction) over the past day, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "Units of the Battlegroup North have inflicted losses on formations of the Ukrainian armed forces' unmanned systems brigade and the territorial defense brigade in the areas of the settlements of Maryino, Novodmitrovka and Ugroedy in the Sumy Region. The Ukrainian armed forces have lost up to 60 servicemen, a tank, two combat armored vehicles, four cars, three artillery pieces and an electronic warfare station," the ministry said. Ukrainian armed forces' losses in the zone of responsibility of the Battlegroup Center in one day amounted to more than 500 servicemen and 4 combat armored vehicles, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "The formations of three mechanized, a Jaeger, four assault brigades, an assault regiment of the Ukrainian armed forces, the East center of special operations forces and two brigades of the National Guard were defeated in the areas of the settlements of Mirolyubovka, Zverevo, Troitskoye, Krasnoarmeysk, Kotlino, Novopavlovka, Udachnoye and Novosergeevka of the Donetsk People's Republic. The enemy lost over 500 servicemen, four combat armored vehicles, seven cars and two artillery pieces, including a 155-mm self-propelled artillery unit Caesar made in France," the ministry said. Over the past 24 hours, the Battlegroup West forces inflicted losses on the manpower and equipment of six brigades of the Ukrainian armed forces, the enemy lost up to 230 servicemen, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "The enemy's losses amounted to 230 servicemen, a tank, an M577 armored personnel carrier and a US-made HMMWV armored vehicle, 7 vehicles and 6 field artillery guns," the ministry said. Also, an electronic warfare station, an AN/TPQ-48 counter-battery radar station and two ammunition depots were destroyed. The Ukrainian armed forces lost up to 160 servicemen in the responsibility zone of the Battlegroup East, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. “The units of the Battlegroup East continued to advance deep into the enemy’s defense, inflicted losses on the formations of the airmobile, mountain assault brigades of the Ukrainian armed forces, the marine brigade and three defense brigades in the areas of the settlements of Komar, Otradnoye, Razliv Novopol, Shevchenko of the Donetsk People’s Republic and Gulyay Pole and Chervone of the Zaporozhye region,” the ministry said. Ukrainian armed forces lost up to 160 servicemen, nine vehicles and three field artillery pieces, including a Polish-made a 155-mm Polish-made Krab self-propelled howitzer. An ammunition depot was destroyed. Ukrainian armed forces units lost up to 70 servicemen and two electronic warfare stations in 24 hours as a result of actions by the Battlegroup Dnepr forces, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "Units of the Battlegroup Dnepr improved the situation along the forward edge, inflicted losses on the manpower and equipment of the mechanized, mountain assault brigades, three coastal defense brigades of the Ukrainian armed forces and a defense brigade in the areas of the settlements of Malaya Tokmachka, Kamenskoye in the Zaporozhye region, Tokarevka, Ponyatovka, Sadovoye and Antonovka in the Kherson region. Up to 70 servicemen, 3 vehicles, 2 foreign-made artillery pieces, 2 ammunition depots and 2 electronic warfare stations were destroyed," the ministry said. The Ukrainian armed forces lost over 360 servicemen and a tank in the zone of responsibility of the Battlegroup South, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "Units of the Battlegroup South took more advantageous lines and positions. They inflicted losses on formations of six mechanized, motorized infantry, airmobile brigades of the Ukrainian armed forces, a special forces brigade and a territorial defense brigade in the areas of the settlements of Tarasovka, Markovo, Novaya Poltavka, Aleksandropol, Shcherbinovka, Druzhba, Vasyukovka, Minkovka, Grigorovka and Chasov Yar of the Donetsk People's Republic," the ministry said. According to the Defense Ministry, the Ukrainian armed forces lost over 360 servicemen, a tank, two combat armored vehicles, nine cars and a UK-made 155-mm FH-70 howitzer and an electronic warfare station. Also an electronic intelligence station and two ammunition depots were destroyed. Russian air defense systems shot down 10 JDAM guided aerial bombs, three HIMARS multiple launch rockets and 224 fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. “Air defense and electronic warfare systems shot down 10 JDAM guided aerial bombs and three US-made HIMARS multiple launch rockets, as well as 224 aircraft-type unmanned aerial vehicles,” the report says.

title: Ukrainian armed forces lost over 190 soldiers in Kursk direction in 24 hours
text: MOSCOW, March 23. /TASS/. Over the past 24 hours, the Ukrainian armed forces have lost over 190 soldiers, three combat armored vehicles, and a multiple launch rocket system launcher in the Kursk area, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "Over the past 24 hours, the Ukrainian armed forces have lost over 190 soldiers, three combat armored vehicles, 16 vehicles, five artillery pieces, six mortars, a multiple launch rocket system launcher, and two ammunition depots were destroyed," the ministry reported. The Russian Defense Ministry added that units of the Battlegroup North inflicted losses on formations of a mechanized, airborne assault brigade, a marine brigade and three territorial defense brigades of the Ukrainian armed forces in the areas of the settlements of Gogolevka, Gornal, Guevo and Oleshnya during offensive operations. Four enemy counterattacks were repelled.

title: Air defense systems downed 59 drones at night
text: MOSCOW, March 23. /TASS/. Russian air defense means destroyed fifty-nine drones at night, including nineteen ones over the Lipetsk Region, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "During the past night, air defense systems on duty intercepted and destroyed 59 Ukrainian drones: 29 drones over the territory of the Rostov Region, 20 drones over the Astrakhan Region, three drones over the Voronezh Region, three drones over the territory of the Volgograd Region, two drones over the territory of the Republic of Crimea, and one drone in each of the Kursk and Saratov Regions," the ministry said. As Voronezh Region Governor Alexander Gusev wrote on hisTelegram channel, according to preliminary data, there are no casualties or damage as a result of the Ukrainian UAV attack in the region. "The danger of a UAV attack in the region remains," he added. Volgograd Region Governor Andrey Bocharov said there were no consequences of the Ukrainian drone attack. "Several unmanned aerial vehicles were intercepted and neutralized while flying over the territory of the Volgograd Region and Volgograd. At the moment, there are no casualties or damage to facilities, and the assessment of the consequences of the falling debris is ongoing," he said as quoted by the regional administration's press service.

title: Russian army hit Ukraine’s Azov brigade location
text: MOSCOW, March 23. /TASS/. Rocket troops of the Russian Battlegroup West delivered a strike against a temporary location of the Ukrainian Azov brigade (recognized as a terrorist organization and banned in Russia), battlegroup spokesman Ivan Bigma told TASS. "Rocket troops of the battlegroup hit the temporary location of a unit from the Ukrainian Azov 3rd assault brigade by the Tornado-S MLRS. Enemy losses were more than ten fighters. Air defense crews destroyed 24 fixed-wing type unmanned aerial vehicles," Bigma said. Seven Starlink terminals, 21 drone control points and a field ammunition depot of the Ukrainian army were destroyed, he added.

title: Russia’s Operation Pipeline triggers panic among Ukrainian troops — newspaper
text: NEW YORK, March 22. /TASS/. Operation Pipeline, which Russia carried out in the Kursk Region, with troops creeping through a gas pipeline deep into enemy defenses, triggered panic among Ukrainian forces, the Wall Street Journal writes. Despite official statements about the situation being under control, Ukrainian Senior Sergeant Zenon Dashak and other soldiers reported panic among Ukrainian forces. According to Dashak, the situation had already been critical when he and his fellow soldiers heard that Russian forces had broken through Ukrainian lines. Some Ukrainian commanders told their men to withdraw before receiving orders from senior officers. Operation Pipeline involved a joint assault unit comprising soldiers from the 11th Separate Guards Air Assault Brigade, the 30th Regiment of the 72nd Motorized Rifle Division, the Akhmat special forces unit, and volunteers from the Veterans and Vostok V assault brigades. The troops moved stealthily through a gas pipeline, about 15 kilometers, and emerged deep within Ukrainian defenses near the town of Sudzha, catching enemy forces off guard. On March 12, Army General Valery Gerasimov, chief of the General Staff of the Russian Armed Forces and first deputy defense minister, stated that over 600 soldiers participated in the operation, with the Veterans and Akhmat units, along with the 11th Air Assault Brigade and the 30th Motorized Rifle Regiment, standing out. Gerasimov commended "the heroic actions of the combined assault unit," which startled the enemy, caused them to abandon their defenses, and thereby aided the Russian army’s progress in the Kursk Region.

title: Russian forces hit Ukrainian military airfields, ammunition depots in 135 districts
text: MOSCOW, March 22. /TASS/. Russian forces have hit the infrastructure of military airfields and ammunition depots of the Ukrainian Armed Forces in 135 districts over the past 24 hours, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "Operational-tactical aviation, attack unmanned aerial vehicles, missile troops, and artillery groups of the Russian Armed Forces hit the infrastructure of military airfields, ammunition depots, as well as concentrations of enemy personnel and equipment in 135 districts," the ministry said. At the same time, Russia’s air defense systems have shot down a MiG-29 aircraft of the Ukrainian Air Force over the past 24 hours, the Russian Defense Ministry reported.

title: Russian forces shot down MiG-29 aircraft of Ukrainian Air Force
text: MOSCOW, March 22. /TASS/. Russia’s air defense systems have shot down a MiG-29 aircraft of the Ukrainian Air Force over the past 24 hours, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "The air defense systems destroyed one MiG-29 aircraft of the Ukrainian Air Force, shot down five JDAM air-launched bombs, and five HIMARS multiple launch rockets, as well as 142 fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles," the report said. The Ukrainian Armed Forces have also lost more than 500 servicemen per day as a result of the actions of Russia’s Center battlegroup. "The units of Russia’s Center battlegroup improved the situation along the front line. They inflicted losses on formations of two mechanized, one ranger, and one assault brigade of the Ukrainian Armed Forces, one marine brigade and one brigade of the National Guard in the areas of the settlements of Novopavlovka, Krasnoarmeysk, Udachnoye, Dimitrov, Troitskoye, and Alekseyevka in the Donetsk People's Republic. The losses of the Ukrainian armed formations amounted to more than 500 servicemen, six armored combat vehicles, including a US-made M113 infantry fighting vehicle, 14 vehicles, and five field artillery pieces," the ministry said. At the same time, the Ukrainian forces have lost over 220 servicemen in the past 24 hours as a result of the actions of Russia’s West battlegroup. "The units of Russia’s West battlegroup improved their tactical situation. They inflicted losses on the personnel and equipment of two mechanized assault brigades of the Ukrainian Armed Forces and a territorial defense brigade in the areas of the settlements of Cherneschina, Andreyevka in the Kharkov region, Novomikhaylovka and Novoye in the Donetsk People's Republic. The enemy's losses amounted to over 220 servicemen, two armored combat vehicles, including a US-made M113 infantry fighting vehicle, four vehicles and three field artillery guns. An ammunition depot was destroyed," the Defense Ministry said.

title: Ukrainian forces lose up to 315 servicemen in Russia’s South battlegroup zone
text: MOSCOW, March 22. /TASS/. The Ukrainian Armed Forces lost up to 315 servicemen and a camp in one day as a result of the actions of Russia’s South battlegroup, the Russian Defense Ministry report. "The units of Russia’s South battlegroup occupied more advantageous positions. They defeated the formations of a heavy mechanized brigade, two mechanized brigades of the Ukrainian Armed Forces, a territorial defense brigade, and two brigades of the National Guard in the areas of the settlements of Alexandropol, Novaya Poltavka, Seversk, Katerinovka, and Ivanopolye of the Donetsk People's Republic. The enemy lost up to 315 servicemen, five armored combat vehicles, six cars, four artillery pieces, and one ammunition depot," the Defense Ministry said. At the same time, the Ukrainian Armed Forces have lost up to 85 servicemen in the area of responsibility of Russia’s Dnepr battlegroup over the past 24 hours. "The units of Russia’s Dnepr battlegroup have improved their tactical situation. They inflicted losses on the personnel and equipment of the mountain assault brigade and two coastal defense brigades of the Ukrainian Armed Forces in the areas of the settlements of Pridneprovskoye, Antonovka in the Kherson region and Kamenskoye in the Zaporozhye region. Up to 85 servicemen, 12 vehicles, two field artillery guns, and an ammunition depot were destroyed," the ministry said.

title: Putin orders establishment of unmanned aviation development center in Samara Region
text: MOSCOW, March 21. /TASS/. Russian President Vladimir Putin has given instructions to establish an unmanned aviation development center in the Samara Region, which will include a drone institute based at Togliatti State University under its umbrella, with the list of instructions published on the Kremlin website. "The Russian government, in conjunction with the Samara Region’s government, are tasked with issues around the establishment of the center on the development of unmanned aviation in the Samara Region, including the creation of an unmanned aviation institute as part of the federally funded higher education institute, the Togliatti State University," the list of instructions stated. A report on the implementation of the order must be submitted to the head of state by June 15. The officials in charge are Prime Minister Mikhail Mishustin and Samara Region Governor Vyacheslav Fedorishchev. The regional authorities' intentions to create an institute on unmanned aerial systems within the Togliatti State University were made public during the Russian president's working trip to the Samara region on January 28, 2025. The university intends to use the method of training qualified drone specialists from secondary professional education to higher education. Initially, the project can be implemented in the format of network programs involving leading universities.

title: Navy development strategy to account for Ukraine operation experience — Maritime Board
text: MOSCOW, March 21. /TASS/. Nikolay Patrushev, Russian presidential aide and chairman of the Maritime Board, has held a meeting of the Council for Strategic Development of the Navy, addressing among other things the Russian Navy Development Strategy, the board’s press service said. "The draft of the Russian Navy Development Strategy has been considered," the press service noted. "The strategy will be grounded in the fundamental principles of fleet construction, will take into account the experience of the special military operation, as well as the current state and anticipated developments of the political-military situation in the world." The document is expected to contribute to the defense of Russia and the protection of its national interests in the World Ocean. Moreover, the participants of the meeting examined the draft of the Strategy of the Federal Security Service's (FSB) ship composition modernization. "The document will reflect tasks in the field of coastal guard and ensuring security in maritime border areas," a statement by the Maritime Board noted. It is stated that the members of the Council for Strategic Development of the Navy also discussed issues of federal and other structures’ coordination with regards to the naval sphere. The meeting was attended by representatives of federal agencies. The Council for Strategic Development of the Navy was created within the Maritime Board by the decree of the Russian president. It is tasked with drafting measures for the strategic development of the Russian Navy.

title: Germany adopts sweeping debt reform, paving way for increased defense spending
text: BERLIN, March 21. /TASS/. The Bundesrat, Germany's House of Representatives for the federal states, has approved amendments to the Constitution that will facilitate the easing of the so-called "debt brake" - a regulation that prohibits spending more money than the government takes in. This change is related to plans to increase defense spending and establish a special infrastructure fund of 500 billion euros. The Bundesrat passed the amendments with a two-thirds majority, securing 53 out of 69 votes, as reported by Ntv TV. Experts are calling the current financial package historic, as it may pave the way for investments totaling between 1 and 1.5 trillion euros. The amendments specify that defense spending exceeding 1% of GDP will be exempt from debt restrictions. Additionally, the new infrastructure fund will not be subject to the ‘debt brake." The German parliament approved the amendments on March 18, and they are expected to take effect next week after the president signs them. The conservative bloc, consisting of the Christian Democratic Union (CDU), the Christian Social Union (CSU), and the Social Democratic Party (SPD), spearheaded the initiative to lift restrictions on financing defense and infrastructure spending. However, obtaining a two-thirds majority in the new Bundestag is likely to be impossible, as both the Alternative for Germany and the Left Party have indicated they will not support the easing of the "debt brake." This was feasible in the previous Bundestag, requiring consensus between the CDU/CSU, SPD, and the Greens. A compromise with the Greens was reached on March 14, which includes allocating 100 billion euros from the new fund for climate protection initiatives. Furthermore, the fund's name illustrates its dual purpose: to attract additional investment in infrastructure and to achieve climate neutrality by 2045. Additionally, the federal states will now have the ability to increase their debt, permitted to borrow up to 0.35% of their total nominal GDP. The new fund will allocate 100 billion euros to the regions.Previously, the Federal Constitutional Court dismissed complaints filed by several Bundestag members aimed at preventing a vote on the amendments.

title: Putin calls for technological leadership in unmanned aerial systems within five years
text: MOSCOW, March 21. /TASS/. Russia must establish itself as a technological leader in the field of unmanned aviation by the year 2030. This objective has been set by the Russian President Vladimir Putin. "The Russian Government, taking into account previously issued directives, is to ensure the accelerated development of the unmanned aviation industry to achieve technological leadership in this field by 2030. It must define specific target indicators that characterize such leadership and allocate funding for the corresponding initiatives," states the list of presidential instructions published on the Kremlin’s official website. The president expects an initial report by June 1, followed by subsequent updates every six months. Putin also ordered to take measures providing for the use of solutions based on artificial intelligence in manufacturing and operation of drones. The list of assignments in conclusion of the relevant meeting was posted on the Kremlin’s website. The Russian government is tasked to perform this work together with the National Technological Initiative Platform and Sber. The assignment was given "for purposes of increasing self-dependence of unmanned aerial vehicles."

title: Putin orders to establish separate airspace class for drones
text: MOSCOW, March 21. /TASS/. Russian President Vladimir Putin tasked the government to ensure establishment of a new aerospace class with application of a simplified procedure of its use by drones. The list of presidential assignments was posted on the Kremlin’s website. "To provide for establishment of a new class of the air space of the Russian Federation, stipulating the application of the simplified procedure of air space use for flights of unmanned aerial vehicles," the document indicates. The head of state also tasked to ensure creation of an efficient state governance system in the unmanned aviation sphere.

title: Ukraine's military intelligence chief identified as organizer of drone attacks on Russia
text: MOSCOW, March 21. /TASS/. Russia's Investigative Committee has identified Kirill Budanov, the head of Ukraine's military intelligence, as one of the organizers of Ukrainian drone attacks on the Russian regions, the agency's press service told TASS. "According to investigators, among the organizers of Ukrainian drone attacks on the Kursk Region and other regions of Russia were Head of the Ukrainian Defense Ministry's Main Intelligence Directorate Kirill Budanov, Commander of the Ukrainian Air Force Nikolay Oleshchuk, and Commander of the Ukrainian Naval Forces Alexey Neizhpapa," the press service said following an operational meeting held by Investigative Committee Head Alexander Bastrykin in Kursk. They have been charged in absentia and placed on the wanted list. The agency added that Russian investigators are conducting a legal evaluation of actions of those involved in strikes on civilian infrastructure using Western operational-tactical missile systems. The Investigative Committee reported that Rostislav Karpusha, commander of the 19th Missile Brigade of the Armed Forces of Ukraine, was charged in absentia in one of these criminal cases.
mil_orgs: Ukrainian Defense Ministry's Main Intelligence Directorate Kirill Budanov

title: Battlegroup West destroys 3 Starlink stations, 25 UAV control points
text: MOSCOW, March 21. /TASS/. Over the past 24 hours, the Battlegroup West destroyed 11 Ukrainian armed forces drones, 25 control points for them, and three Starlink satellite communication stations, head of the group's press center, Ivan Bigma, told TASS. "Air defense crews destroyed 11 fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles. <…> In addition, three Starlink satellite communication stations and 25 UAV control points were destroyed," he said. Over the past 24 hours the group's units improved their tactical position, defeated the manpower and equipment of three mechanized brigades, an assault brigade of the Ukrainian armed forces, a territorial defense brigade, and a national guard brigade, he added. Bigma noted that the Ukrainian armed forces lost more than 240 servicemen, six vehicles and five field artillery guns. Also, an electronic warfare station and two ammunition depots were destroyed.

title: Kiev forces lost 4 UAV control points in 24 hours due to actions of Battlegroup East
text: MOSCOW, March 21. /TASS/. The Ukrainian armed forces lost four unmanned aircraft control points and three fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles in 24 hours due to the actions of the Battlegroup East, head of the group's press center, Alexander Gordeyev, told TASS. "Over the course of 24 hours, the enemy lost a MaxxPro combat armored vehicle, three fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles and four unmanned aircraft control points," Gordeyev said. According to him, during the active operations, units of the Battlegroup East continued to advance into the depths of the enemy's defense, inflicted losses on the manpower and equipment of the mechanized brigade, the marine brigade and the assault regiment of the Ukrainian armed forces in the areas of Veseloye, Shevchenko, Novodarovka, Razliv, Komar and Volnoye Pole.

title: Tanks of Battlegroup Dnepr destroy strongholds of Kiev forces in Zaporozhye region
text: MELITOPOL, March 21. /TASS/. Crews of tank units of the Battlegroup Dnepr constantly suppress firing points of the Ukrainian armed forces in the Zaporozhye region, a tank commander with the call sign Veter (the Wind) told reporters. "There are no enemy strongholds on the front section that are not zeroed in and not dismantled on a regular basis. That is, two, three, four trips per day," he said. According to him, the Battlegroup Dnepr continues to carry out combat missions in the Zaporozhye direction, showing high efficiency and professionalism.

title: Russia to boost arms exports after special military operation — Security Council
text: MOSCOW, March 20. /TASS/. The demand for Russian weapons will grow after the special military operation in Ukraine, Secretary of the Russian Security Council Sergey Shoigu told reporters. "Believe me, a large queue will line up for our arms after the end of the special military operation," Shoigu said. It proved itself in combat operations and was improved to perfection, he added. This refers to air defense systems, strike drones and upgraded armored vehicles. Shoigu highlighted improved T-90 and T-80 tanks. "It pertains to S-400, S-500 [air defense systems] and Pantsir surface-to-air missile system," the official said.

title: Russia developing Caspian flotilla infrastructure — top brass
text: MOSCOW, March 20. /TASS/. Coastal infrastructure for the docking and maintenance of the Caspian flotilla of warships, speedboats and vessels is being constructed in Kaspiysk in the Republic of Dagestan, the Russian Defense Ministry said in a report timed to coincide with Defense Minister Andrey Belousov’s working trip to Russia’s North Caucasus republic. "At present, work to construct a complex of hydrotechnical facilities, including the northern and southern piers spanning more than 2 kilometers, as well as berths and coastal infrastructure for effective docking and servicing of vessels and speedboats is ongoing in Kaspiysk," the ministry specified.

title: Package attached to car bottom found near Legislative Assembly of Sevastopol
text: SEVASTOPOL, March 20. /TASS/. A package was discovered on the bottom of a car during an inspection of a parking lot near the Legislative Assembly of Sevastopol, and the area was cordoned off, the Department of Public Security of the city said. "This morning, during the inspection of cars at the parking lot on Lenin St., house 3 (the building of the Legislative Assembly), according to a preliminary assessment, a package was attached to the underside of one of the cars. Checks are ongoing. The scene of the incident has been cordoned off," the report states. According to the Directorate for the Development of the City's Road Transport Infrastructure, some buses and trolleybuses are altering their routes due to the temporary closure of Lenin Street.
mil_orgs: Directorate for the Development of the City's Road Transport Infrastructure

title: Russian combat ships hold PASSEX joint exercise with Pakistani navy
text: MOSCOW, March 20. /TASS/. A group of Russian combat ships held a joint exercise with the Pakistani naval forces in the Arabian Sea, Russia’s Defense Ministry reported on Thursday. "A group of Russian naval ships comprising the corvettes Rezky and Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhapov and the medium sea tanker Pechenga from the Pacific Fleet has conducted a PASSEX joint naval exercise codenamed Arabian Monsoon with the Navy of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan," the ministry said in a statement. After departing from the port of Karachi, the Pacific Fleet’s ships formed a naval group with the Pakistani frigate Shah Jahan and corvette Tabuk, it said. During the naval exercise, the combat ships from both navies practiced tactical maneuvering and repulsed notional attacks by uncrewed boats. The crews of the combat ships also conducted an air defense exercise, in which Pakistani fighter jets and patrol aircraft simulated launches of anti-ship missiles, the ministry said. The joint naval exercise ended with an operation to free a vessel notionally seized by terrorists simulated by the sea tanker Pechenga, it added. Last time, the Russian and Pakistani navies held the PASSEX Arabian Monsoon exercise in 2021.
weapons: corvettes Rezky and Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhapov

title: Russian Battlegroup East eliminates 5 Ukrainian drone control centers
text: MOSCOW, March 20. /TASS/. Russia’s Battlegroup East destroyed five Ukrainian drone control centers during the past day, the battlegroup’s spokesman Alexander Gordeyev told TASS. "During the day, the enemy lost a MaxxPro armored combat vehicle and five drone control centers," he said.

title: India, Russia to conduct Indra Navy joint military exercises
text: NEW DELHI, March 19. /TASS/. India and Russia will conduct the Indra Navy joint naval drills in late March - early April, the Russian Embassy in New Delhi told TASS. "We will conduct Russian-Indian Indra Navy drills, during which objectives on joint maneuvering, assisting a vessel in distress, and inspection operations will be carried out," the Russian Embassy said. Conducting the maneuvers "will serve the purpose of strengthening the friendship between Russia’s Navy and the Navy of the Republic of India within the special exclusive strategic partnership between our countries," the embassy emphasized. Following the fourth session of the working group on military cooperation within the India-Russia Inter-Governmental Commission on Military & Military Technical Cooperation (IRIGC-M&MTC) in November last year, the Indian Defense Ministry said that the two countries would expand cooperation in defense, including conducting joint exercises. The defense ministry of the South Asian republic highlighted the "importance of the continuous exchange of expertise and cooperation in fields of strategic interest." The Indian defense agency also noted that the two nations had already previously conducted numerous joint ground, air, and naval drills, and exercises like Indra, Avia Indra, and Indra Navy have served as important platforms for exchanging advanced expertise, improving joint operational tactical maneuvers and procedures, as well as deepening mutual understanding.
mil_orgs: India-Russia Inter-Governmental Commission on Military & Military Technical Cooperation

title: US suspends some counterintelligence operations against Russia — agency
text: NEW YORK, March 19. /TASS/. US agencies have suspended coordinated counterintelligence operations against Russia as part of the Trump administration’s efforts to resolve the Ukraine conflict,Reutersreports, citing former US security officials. According to the news agency, the initiative to establish working groups to track the hybrid operations that the West ascribes to Russia, including sabotage, disinformation and cyberattacks, was developed under ex-US President Joe Biden. The project involved at least seven security agencies working with Washington’s European partners to disrupt plots allegedly targeting the US and Europe. However, according to current and former US officials, most of this work has been suspended, meetings with European partners have become irregular and the White House National Security Council has stopped coordinating efforts with other agencies, including the Federal Bureau of Investigation, the Department of Homeland Security and the Department of State. According to the agency’s sources, the White House did not specify whether it will recreate the cross-agency working groups. When commenting on the issue, Russian Presidential Press Secretary Dmitry Peskov told Reuters that the Trump administration was trying to get rid of "everything ineffective, corrupt and implausible," which was "understandable." According to the Kremlin spokesman, Western accusations that Russia commits sabotage against the US and Europe are "empty and ephemeral" and have not been proven.

title: Belarus, Russia talk transparency measures for West-2025 drills — senior defense official
text: MINSK, March 18. /TASS/. The defense officials of Belarus and Russia have discussed issues of transparency for September's West-2025 joint strategic drills, Belarusian Defense Ministry’s International Military Cooperation Department head Major General Valery Revenko said. The ministry’s press service noted that the 63rd meeting of the Joint Consultative Group on Arms Control is taking place in Minsk, bringing together both defense officials and diplomats. "The meeting participants focused primarily on transparency regarding the West-2025 drills: whom to invite to the territories of both Belarus and Russia," the press service quoted Revenko as saying on its Telegram channel. The senior defense official emphasized that another key issue was monitoring Ukrainian territory in the post-conflict period. "We found constructive approaches for joint activities between Russia and Belarus on this issue," Revenko pointed out. The sides also discussed the implementation of trust and security measures within the framework of the 2011 Vienna Document. "We compared approaches and opinions on this issue. We also discussed the regional confidence and security measures with our neighbors: Poland, Lithuania, Latvia," the Belarusian defense official said. "We will continue working to align our positions and de-escalating tensions near the Belarusian border. Our proposal to Poland regarding verification activities up to 80 kilometers inside their territory remains on the table," he stressed. Revenko expressed confidence that, after a period of crisis and stagnation, arms control in Europe will once again become a key issue on the international agenda and a subject of legal regulation. Earlier, Revenko stated that Belarus had informed international partners about the upcoming West-2025 joint strategic exercises with Russia and intends to invite several EU and NATO observers.
mil_units: 63rd meeting of the Joint Consultative Group on Arms Control

title: Baltic, Polish defense chiefs call for withdrawal from anti-personnel mine ban convention
text: VILNIUS, March 18. /TASS/. The defense ministers of Latvia, Lithuania, Poland and Estonia have recommended the government of their countries to leave the Ottawa Convention, or the Anti-Personnel Mine Ban Convention. "Since the ratification of the Anti-Personnel Mine Ban Convention (Ottawa Convention), the security situation in our region has fundamentally deteriorated. Military threats to NATO Member States bordering Russia and Belarus have significantly increased," says the joint statement quoted by the Estonian Defense Ministry. "In light of this unstable security environment marked by Russia’s aggression and its ongoing threat to the Euro-Atlantic community, it is essential to evaluate all measures to strengthen our deterrence and defense capabilities. We believe that in the current security environment it is of paramount importance to provide our defense forces with flexibility and freedom of choice of potential use of new weapon systems and solutions to bolster the defense of the Alliance’s vulnerable Eastern Flank. In light of these considerations, we - the Ministers of Defense of Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, and Poland - unanimously recommend withdrawing from the Ottawa Convention," the statement reads. It adds that despite launching the withdrawal process, the Baltics and Poland will "remain committed to international humanitarian law, including the protection of civilians during an armed conflict." The Ottawa Convention, which bans anti-personnel mines, entered into force in 1999 and has been ratified by 164 states, including Ukraine. The International Committee of the Red Cross estimates that these weapons cause numerous civilian casualties and continue to pose a danger long after hostilities have ended.

title: Russia to deliver 2,000 jam-resistant kamikaze drones to Ukraine operation zone
text: MOSCOW, March 18. /TASS/. Krasnodar designers and the Kulibin Club will hand over to Russian troops in Ukraine 2,000 UDK kamikaze drones that deceive electronic warfare by switching video and control channels, the People’s Front told TASS. "UDK are kamikaze drones with a control system that automatically changes frequencies in flight. It is also possible to manually change the frequency of video transmission. It is a method to bypass hostile electronic warfare and win time for the mission. Our drones have destroyed over 50 US-made Bradley vehicles and over 30 M777 artillery guns," it said. UDK can carry a 4-kg payload. The first US-made MaxxPro armored vehicle was destroyed by the drone. The front said over 10 thousand drones had been delivered to Ukraine operation zone and another batch of 2,000 units is prepared. The Kulibin Club of the People’s Front selects, tests and supports serial production of the best innovations by Russian engineers. This initiative has helped deliver thousands of hi-tech electronic warfare systems to the frontline to counter enemy drones. Russian troops have also received quadcopters, self-propelled robotic carts and other innovative equipment.

title: Almost whole Kursk Region has been liberated — Russian commander
text: KURSK, March 18. /TASS/. Almost the whole territory of the Kursk Region has been liberated, Deputy Chief of the Russian Armed Forces’ Main Military-Political Department, Akhmat Special Forces Commander Lieutenant General Apty Alaudinov said in an interview with TASS. "We can state the fact now that almost the whole territory of the Kursk Region has been liberated," he said. Russia’s military advance gave strong results, with almost all settlements taken at once, and the enemy’s personnel destroyed, Alaudinov added.

title: Nuclear submarine supplies to Australia are challenge to IAEA safeguards system — diplomat
text: MOSCOW, March 17. /TASS/. Supplies of highly-enriched uranium to Australia to fuel US-and UK-made nuclear-powered submarines pose a challenge to the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) safeguards system, Russia’s Permanent Representative to the Vienna-based international organizations Mikhail Ulyanov said. "This is a challenge for the IAEA safeguards system," he said. "The basic safeguards agreement’s article 14 says that guarantees do not apply to nuclear materials that are used for non-peaceful purposes. In this case, the uses are obviously not peaceful as we are talking about military nuclear submarines." According to the Russian diplomat, the problem is that "Australia, a non-nuclear state under the Non-Proliferation Treaty, will receive around two, or even more, tons of highly-enriched uranium." "The IAEA has never before seen a case like this in its history," he stated. "No one knows how article 14 is going to work. The IAEA secretariat and Australia are discussing this problem, involving, as far as I know, the Americans and the British, but it is not known what exactly they are negotiating. They are not being transparent about this process." The Russian diplomat also drew attention to the fact that three members of the AUKUS bloc (the United States, the United Kingdom, and Australia) have already "agreed on a foundational document saying that certain information will not be reported to the agency." "This only adds to the suspicion that something is amiss here," Ulyanov stressed. "The AUKUS members are unwilling to engage in a serious interstate-level discussion. Given the lack of progress in this case, it is expedient to keep the topic on the IAEA agenda and so far, it is there." Ulyanov questioned the feasibility of providing non-nuclear Australia with a fleet of combat nuclear-powered submarines. "The rationale behind this project is difficult to grasp because neither the Americans nor the British have the capability to manufacture that many submarines," he explained. "This is a very costly undertaking, even if Australia pays for it. I saw this in the Australian media: the Americans can manufacture 1.2 nuclear submarines a year, so it will take quite a long time to make several such submarines."

title: IAEA keeps mum about social media intimidation against ZNPP staff — Russian diplomat
text: MOSCOW, March 17. /TASS/. The Secretariat of the International Atomic Agency (IAEA) is turning a blind eye to attempts to intimidate staff at the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant (ZNPP) on social media, Mikhail Ulyanov, Russia’s Permanent Representative to international organizations in Vienna, said. "This is a highly concerning issue, one that the Agency’s Secretariat seems to overlook, and that is the psychological terror campaign that has been launched against ZNPP employees. Photos of staff members, their home addresses, and even warnings to flee ‘before it’s too late’ are being circulated," the Russian diplomat shared. Meanwhile, he said, the IAEA Secretariat has categorically refused to react to these attempts to scare staff. "Of course, the Secretariat itself is to a large extent intimidated, so no one should expect an objective assessment from it," Ulyanov noted. For this reason, the diplomat continued, the IAEA Secretariat refrains from speaking out in any way about this issue.

title: Russian annual arms export may grow to $17 bln after Ukraine conflict ends
text: MOSCOW, March 17. /TASS/. The annual portfolio of contracts for arms exports may grow from $13.7 to $17 billion after the end of the Ukraine operation, the Center for Analysis of Global Arms Trade (TsAMTO) estimated. "TsAMTO estimates that in four years after the end of the special military operation the Russian military exports may reach $17-19 a year and maybe more. The forecast considers that all Russian weapons have been tested in battle and are adapted to the realities of modern highly technological war, have been modified and upgraded according to real combat experience of fighting modern arms supplied to Ukraine," it said. The center estimated the current arms export portfolio at $13.75 billion and it tends to grow. In February, Rostec CEO Sergey Chemezov said the total portfolio of Rosboronexport exceeded $60 billion. TsAMTO estimated the global arms trade to be the biggest in 2024 after the end of the Cold War. It comprised $111.615 billion by the exchange rate at the day of contract signing. The total arms trade comprised $82.942 billion in 2021, $106.185 billion in 2022 and $96.889 billion in 2023, the center estimated. It attributed the current growth to the conflict in Ukraine, where Soviet-made arms were exhausted and the West launched large-scale purchases of expensive modern arms for Ukraine.
mil_orgs: Center for Analysis of Global Arms Trade
