Extremist movements, by attempting to drive a wedge between ethnicities and religious denominations, are one of the lines of attack on Russia, Russian President Vladimir Putin highlighted at a board meeting of the Federal Security Service (FSB). "It is necessary to fight extremism and terrorism resolutely and assertively, thwart any attempts to divide and split our society, pit various ethnicities and religious denominations against each other," the head of state said. "We fully realize that this is one of the lines of attack on Russia, on our multinational and multidenominational country," Putin emphasized.

title: FSB is strong, reliable component of national security — Putin
text: MOSCOW, February 27. /TASS/. Russia’s Federal Security Service (FSB) is a strong, reliable and effective component of national security, President Vladimir Putin stated during a meeting of the FSB board. "The concrete results of your activities demonstrate that the FSB is a strong, reliable, and effective link in the system of national security. Your specialized units have acted promptly and competently, defending the sovereignty and national interests of Russia, as well as the rights of our citizens. They have reliably protected the constitutional order, fought against terrorism and extremism, tackled crime, and conducted complex counterintelligence operations," Putin said. He added that this year the FSB coped with all of its tasks "at a high level by and large."

title: Putin points to growing number of terrorist attacks, says over half planned by Kiev
text: MOSCOW, February 27. /TASS/. The Ukrainian special services planned more than a half of the terrorist crimes in Russia, President Vladimir Putin said at the meeting of the Russian Federal Security Service's (FSB) board. "Over the past year, the number of terrorist crimes has increased, more than half of which were planned and organized by Ukrainian special services and their handlers," the president pointed out. "The FSB must continue its systematic work in all specialized areas, and first of all, of course, in the fight against international terrorism," Putin emphasized.

title: Putin instructed to strengthen FSB potential in Donbass, Novorossiya
text: MOSCOW, February 27. /TASS/. The Federal Security Service (FSB) forces in Donbass and Novorossiya are doing a lot to neutralize terrorists and saboteurs and their potential must be reinforced, Russian President Vladimir Putin told a meeting of the FSB board. "Work should also continue to fortify the anti-terrorist potential of the FSB forces in the regions of Donbass and Novorossiya," the head of state instructed. "I would like to emphasize that these units are doing a lot today to neutralize terrorist and sabotage groups and to gather intelligence about the enemy."

title: Malaysian PM says he discussed defense issues with top Russian security official
text: TOKYO, February 27. /TASS/. Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim has said that he discussed security, defense and educational issues with Russian Security Council Secretary Sergey Shoigu during the latter’s visit to Kuala Lumpur. "Today, I received <...> Sergey Shoigu, Russian Security Council secretary, who is on an official visit to Malaysia. In our conversation, we touched upon several important topics pertaining to relations between our countries, including educational, security and defense issues," Ibrahim wrote on Facebook (prohibited in Russia due to its ownership by Meta, which is designated as extremist). According tothe Bernama news agency, Ibrahim and Shoigu also discussed ties in the fields of science, technology and banking. The Malaysian prime minister shared his impressions from a meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin on the sidelines of the Eastern Economic Forum (EEF) in September 2023. He highlighted the EEF’s importance in expanding and strengthening economic cooperation between Malaysia and other participants. In addition, the Malaysian premier also expressed hope that the Russian Security Council secretary's visit would lay the foundation for fruitful cooperation between the two countries. Shoigu said earlier on Wednesday that the Malaysian prime minister and he had discussed all issues on the agenda, addressing cooperation in the fields of finances and energy, including the nuclear energy sector. Sergey Shoigu is currently on a working visit to the Malaysian capital of Kuala Lumpur. During his trip, he held a series of talks with the country’s top officials, discussing pressing issues. In particular, he met with Nushirwan Zainal Abidin, director general of the National Security Council under the Malaysian prime minister’s office.

title: France may employ nuclear deterrence to protect others in the EU, minister says
text: PARIS, February 27. /TASS/. French Minister of the Armed Forces Sebastien Lecornu said his country could employ nuclear deterrence to protect the other members of the European Union. Commenting on reports that France and Britain could use their nuclear deterrence forces to protect Germany should the United States move to withdraw its troops from there, Lecornu told Franceinfo Radio that only Paris controls French nukes and that the decision to use them can be made by the head of state to protect "essential interests." "Are French essential interests strictly confined to our borders? <...> Since the General [Charles] de Gaulle era, several heads of state, including Emmanuel Macron, have stated that our vital interests are of a European dimension," the French minister said. "General de Gaulle was even more precise as he wrote in his instructions to the armed forces in 1964 that France’s essential interests will be jeopardized should western Germany or the Benelux countries be attacked," he explained. On February 25, The Daily Telegraph reported, citing sources, that France might deploy its nuclear forces to Germany if the US decides to reduce its military presence in Europe. German diplomats, in turn, told the British newspaper that such a French move could compel Britain to follow suit. Friedrich Merz, Germany’s likely next chancellor, has urged the UK and France to extend their nuclear protection to his country. On February 21, Merz told ZDF television in an interview that he questioned whether the Donald Trump-led United States would adhere to the collective defense clause of the North Atlantic Alliance. He said the Europeans must "take every step to at least be able to defend their region." Earlier, NBC quoted sources as saying that the United States is considering reducing its military presence in Europe due to its priorities in the Asia-Pacific region and on the border with Mexico. According to the TV channel, US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told Vladimir Zelensky "in a closed-door meeting that the United States may withdraw a significant number of its troops from Europe."

title: Ukrainian forces strike house in Energodar with loitering munition
text: MELITOPOL, February 27. /TASS/. The Ukrainian armed forces have struck the city of Energodar near the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant (ZNPP), the city’s acting mayor, Maxim Pukhov, said on his Telegram channel. "The Kiev regime used a kamikaze drone to once again attempt to disrupt life in Energodar. The windows of one house were damaged in the attack," he wrote. The head of the city administration added that the threat of the attack repeating is still present in the city, urging its residents to avoid open areas.

title: Chinese Defense Ministry urges US to meet disarmament obligations
text: BEIJING, February 27. /TASS/. China is calling on the United States to responsibly fulfill its disarmament obligations and significantly reduce its nuclear arsenal, Spokesman for China's Ministry of National Defense Wu Qian said at a briefing. "As the nation with the largest nuclear stockpile, the US must genuinely meet its special obligations in nuclear disarmament and greatly cut down its nuclear capabilities," he emphasized. The spokesperson reaffirmed that "China follows a policy that prohibits it from being the first to use nuclear weapons, adheres to a nuclear defense strategy, keeps its nuclear arsenal at the minimum required for national security, and actively supports international peace and security." Earlier, US President Donald Trump expressed his intention to engage in talks with Russia and China on reducing nuclear arsenals.

title: Russian troops developing an offensive in the south of the DPR — Defense Ministry
text: DONETSK, February 27. /TASS/. Units of the Battlegroup East are developing an offensive in the southern Donetsk direction, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "In the southern Donetsk direction, the pace of our troops' advance is steadily increasing. Motorized rifle and tank units are leveling the front in the section of Novoocheretovatoye - Novosyolka and are developing an offensive to the north towards Dneproenergiya," the defense ministry reported. On February 24, Vladimir Rogov, Chairman of the Commission on Sovereignty, Patriotic Projects and Support for Veterans of the Public Chamber of the Russian Federation, told TASS that Russian forces had completed clearing Novoocheretovaty near the border of the DPR and Zaporozhye Region of Ukrainian armed forces servicemen and had advanced toward the settlements of Skudnoye and Dneproenergiya.
mil_orgs: Commission on Sovereignty, Patriotic Projects and Support for Veterans of the Public Chamber of the Russian Federation

title: Battlegroup West destroys 4 Starlink stations, 18 UAV control points of Ukrainian forces
text: MOSCOW, February 27. /TASS/. The Battlegroup West forces destroyed two robotic platforms, four Starlink satellite communication stations and 18 unmanned aerial vehicle control points of the Ukrainian armed forces in one day, head of the group's press center, Ivan Bigma, told TASS. "In one day, two robotic platforms, 18 UAV control points, four Starlink satellite communication stations, a field artillery ammunition depot and eight mortar crews were destroyed," Bigma said. In addition, according to him, the air defense crews of the group destroyed a Hammer guided aerial bomb and 11 aircraft-type UAVs.

title: Ukrainian forces lost 2 UAV control points in Battlegroup East responsibility zone
text: MOSCOW, February 27. /TASS/. The Ukrainian armed forces lost two unmanned aircraft control points and three heavy Baba Yaga-type copters in the area of responsibility of Russia’s Battlegroup East, head of the group's press center, Alexander Gordeyev, told TASS. "The enemy lost two aircraft-type unmanned aerial vehicles, three heavy Baba Yaga-type copters, and two unmanned aircraft control points," Gordeyev said. Earlier, the Defense Ministry reported that on February 25, the Ukrainian armed forces lost up to 175 servicemen as a result of the actions of the Battlegroup East.

title: Risk of direct military clash between nuclear powers extremely high — Russian MFA
text: GENEVA, February 26. /TASS/. The risk of a head-on military clash between nuclear powers is alarmingly high, given the rising tensions and conflicts across the globe, Deputy Foreign Minister Sergey Vershinin stated at a high-level disarmament conference. "As we approach the 80th anniversary of the UN, we find ourselves in the midst of a severe crisis in international security. The situation is deteriorating at an alarming pace, and the risk of a direct military confrontation between nuclear powers is extremely high. Tensions and conflicts are escalating almost everywhere," he remarked. Vershinin attributed the current state of affairs to the relentless struggle over the global order: "The hegemonic ambitions of some nations clash with the aspirations of others who seek a more equitable, polycentric framework for international relations." He noted, "Those pursuing global dominance are committing gross violations of the principle of equal and indivisible security, neglecting the fundamental interests of other states, undermining previous agreements, and attempting to achieve unquestionable military superiority. This provokes other countries to adopt compensatory measures." He emphasized that the crises in Ukraine and the Middle East were direct consequences of this situation. "The conflict in Ukraine is a result of the aggressive and unrelenting policies of Western countries, which have sought to erase all aspects of Russian presence from public, cultural, and religious life, while simultaneously increasing supplies of long-range weapons to the Kiev regime. Russia, for its part, has always advocated for a negotiated settlement to the conflict. However, the dialogue must be founded on an equal and mutually respectful basis, aiming not for a fleeting truce, but for a lasting peace grounded in respect for the legitimate interests of all states and peoples in the region," Vershinin concluded.

title: Belarusian parliament’s lower house ratifies security pact with Russia
text: MINSK, February 26. /TASS/. The House of Representatives (the lower house) of the National Assembly of Belarus ratified on Wednesday the Russia-Belarus Union State Treaty on Security Guarantees. Specifically, the document stipulates that "military and other facilities of the Russian Federation may be set up and Russian military formations may be deployed on Belarusian territory for the purpose of preventing and repelling acts of aggression against the Republic of Belarus." The draft law on the ratification of the agreement is still have to be approved by the Council of the Republic (the upper house) of the National Assembly of Belarus and then sent for the signing by the country’s president. The State Duma and the Federation Council (Russian parliament’s lower and upper houses) ratified the document on February 18 and 26 correspondingly. The document was signed in Minsk on December 6, 2024. Under the Treaty, the parties undertake to support each other with all methods and means agreed upon and permitted by international law and take corresponding actions in the political, military and other spheres in case of threats to the security of ether party and the Union State as a whole. Pursuant to the Treaty, Russia and Belarus will deem an armed attack on a member of the Union State as an act of aggression against the Union State as a whole and will take appropriate retaliatory measures using all forces and capabilities at their disposal. In addition, the parties regard Russian nuclear weapons as an important factor of preventing the emergence of nuclear military conflicts or military conflicts with the use of conventional weapons, as well as a deterrent that can be used only as an extreme and last-resort measure. In accordance with the document, Russia’s nuclear weapons may be employed in response to the use of nuclear weapons or other weapons of mass destruction against any of the parties to the Treaty. They can also be used in case of aggression against either party with the use of conventional weapons that creates a critical threat to its sovereignty or territorial integrity. A decision on using Russian nuclear weapons deployed on the territory of Belarus for the purpose of its protection will be taken in a procedure established by the parties.

title: Russia guarantees security of Belarus in case of external aggression — MFA
text: MOSCOW, February 26. /TASS/. The Russia-Belarus Union State Treaty on Mutual Security Guarantees holds historic significance for safeguarding Russian foreign policy interests and strengthening the defense of both countries, Russian State Secretary and Deputy Foreign Minister Yevgeny Ivanov stated at the session of the Federation Council during the ratification of the agreement. "The treaty holds historic significance. The signing of it demonstrates the unprecedented level and nature of the bilateral relations," he said. "In the current geopolitical environment, Russia could not have chosen not to provide additional security guarantees to its closest strategic ally - Belarus. This is exactly what has been achieved through the signing of the treaty." "We guarantee such a level of security for a foreign state for the first time, up to the use of nuclear weapons in the event of aggression against the brotherly Belarusian people," the senior diplomat emphasized. "The treaty culminated the results of the careful work conducted by our two countries over many years, including the deployment of Russia’s tactical nuclear weapons in Belarus." According to him, the document is of great importance for securing Russian foreign policy interests, as well as the robust defense and security of the Union State.

title: Nominee for US deputy secretary of defense says US’ nuclear weapons need modernization
text: WASHINGTON, February 25. /TASS/. The United States’ nuclear weapons have become outdates and need to be upgraded, Stephen A. Feinberg, the nominee for Deputy Secretary of Defense, said. "Our nuclear capabilities are old. We have to upgrade them, and we have to develop hypersonics," he said during US Senate Committee on Armed Services hearings on his candidature. "There's nothing that could be more important than our nuclear modernization." "It is critical to maintain and modernize all three legs of the triad - ICBMs, submarines, and bombers," he emphasized in written answers to questions from the committee members. He shared the assessments that China, Russia, and North Korea "have significantly expanded and modernized their nuclear force capabilities." "These improvements, which include advances in warheads, delivery systems, and command and control systems, pose an increasing threat to the United States and its Allies," he noted. "Russia has developed new advanced nuclear weapons like the Sarmat ICBM, Avangard hypersonic glide vehicle, and Kinzhal hypersonic missile," he added.

title: Sanctions fail to curb strengthening of Russian army — nominee to be deputy Pentagon chief
text: WASHINGTON, February 25. /TASS/. The sanctions imposed by Western countries and repeatedly tightened against Russia have failed to restrain the strengthening of the combat potential of the Russian armed forces, Stephen A. Feinberg, the nominee to be Deputy Secretary of Defense said in replies to questions from members of the US Congress Senate Committee on Armed Services. "Despite facing international sanctions since 2014, which were further strengthened during the first Trump Administration, Russia has maintained a capable military, developed strategic capabilities, and increased production of crucial military equipment," Feinberg pointed out. "Of particular concern is Russia's expansion of its nuclear arsenal, posing a threat to the US homeland, as well as its tactical nuclear weapons, which threaten its European and Asian neighbors. Russia also presents challenges in cyberspace, information warfare, undersea warfare, space, and the Arctic." Trump nominated investor and billionaire Feinberg to be the Pentagon's top deputy chief. During Trump's first term, Feinberg chaired the US president's Intelligence Advisory Board.

title: Up to 20,000 militants join Syria's police, security forces — Kyrgyz Security Council
text: BISHKEK, February 25. /TASS/. Militants from various terrorist groups are actively joining Syria's police and security agencies, according to Marat Imankulov, the secretary of the Kyrgyz Security Council. He shared his estimates at the roundtable conference entitled CSTO 2025: Kyrgyzstan's Role in Reducing Regional Threats Amid Growing Geopolitical Tensions, organized by the Oi Ordo Center for Expert Initiatives in Bishkek. "According to some estimates, up to 20,000 foreign militants are joining the Syrian security forces. Some of them are affiliated with the Islamic Movement of Eastern Turkestan. Experts believe that their ranks include up to 6,000 militants focused on destabilizing China, as well as individuals from Central Asia and the North Caucasus. About 5,000 militants, not including their family members, have Central Asian roots," he stated. Imankulov emphasized that this situation was a cause for concern among many states, including Syria's neighbors. He warned of the potential for a "multinational terrorist threat" to spill over beyond Syria's borders. "There is evidence that Syrian terrorist resources can be exploited by certain forces, both from within the country and from other regions, to change the political regime in Iran - a member of the SCO and a partner of Kyrgyzstan and our Central Asian neighbors," he added. He also highlighted another negative security factor: "The appointment of individuals who are wanted in their home countries for terrorist and extremist crimes to key government positions in Syria." "In addition to the propaganda effect, such individuals gain the ability to leave Syria without hindrance, visit other countries, and engage in destructive activities using their official status, which poses a significant threat," he warned. According to the Secretary of the Kyrgyz Security Council, the political statements of the new Syrian authorities currently "mainly concern Syria itself," while "foreign groups" are involved in establishing a new political system and security structures. "For example, the Islamic Movement of East Turkestan has received significant preferential treatment." For these reasons, a mass migration of militants is not anticipated at this time. However, he speculated that in the event of a serious conflict with the Syrian authorities or their ousting from the country's political landscape, such a possibility could emerge.

title: Belarus to consider ratifying security pact with Russia on February 26
text: MINSK, February 25. /TASS/. The House of Representatives (the lower house) of the National Assembly of Belarus will consider ratifying the Russia-Belarus Union State security pact on February 26, the press service of the republic’s lower house reported on its Telegram channel. "After a joint meeting of the chambers of [the Belarusian] parliament, the House of Representatives of the National Assembly of Belarus will hold its next meeting to consider ratifying the ‘Treaty between Belarus and Russia on Mutual Security Guarantees within the Union State,’" the press service specified. On February 18, the State Duma (the lower house of the Russian parliament) ratified the agreement with Belarus on mutual security guarantees within the Union State. The document was signed in Minsk on December 6, 2024. Under the security pact, Russia and Belarus undertake to support each other by all methods and means agreed upon and permitted by international law and take appropriate actions in the political, military and other spheres in cases of threats to the security of either member and the Union State as a whole. Under the treaty, Russia and Belarus will deem an armed attack on a member of the Union State as an act of aggression against the Union State as a whole and will take appropriate retaliatory measures using all forces and means at their disposal. In addition, the parties regard Russian nuclear weapons as an important factor of preventing the emergence of nuclear military conflicts or military conflicts with the use of conventional weapons, as well as a deterrent that can be used only as an extreme and last-resort measure. Russian nuclear weapons can also be used in case of aggression against either party with the use of conventional weapons that creates a critical threat to its sovereignty or territorial integrity. A decision on using Russian nuclear weapons deployed on the territory of Belarus for the purpose of its protection shall be taken in a procedure established by the parties.

title: UK to increase defense spending to 2.5% of GDP by 2027, PM says
text: LONDON, February 25. /TASS/. The United Kingdom will increase its defense spending from 2.3% to 2.5% of GDP, Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced, addressing the House of Commons. "Starting today, I can announce this Government will begin the biggest sustained increase in defence spending since the end of the Cold War. We will deliver our commitment to spend 2.5% of GDP on defense but we will bring it forward so that we reach that level in 2027," he stated. "Let me spell that out, that means spending 13.4 billion pounds more on defense every year from 2027," he added. Starmer noted that the goal was to raise defense spending to 3% of GDP after the next parliamentary election, which is expected to be held no later than in August 2029. The prime minister said that London would fund the increase in defense spending by cutting funding for overseas aid from 0.5% of GDP to 0.3% by 2027. "That is not an announcement I am happy to make," Starmer admitted. Speaking about the prospects for resolving the conflict in Ukraine, Starmer said that London believed in the power of democracy, adding that no one wanted bloodshed to continue. Still, he highlighted the need to achieve peace through strength and continue supporting Ukraine. Starmer also claimed that "Russia is a menace in our waters, in our airspace and on our streets." He reiterated that NATO would remain the "bedrock" of European security, and called for rejecting "any false choice between our allies, between one side of the Atlantic or the other." Still, Starmer said that the UK would boost bilateral partnership with the US. He also said that European countries needed a new defense alliance.

title: Shoigu calls talks in Indonesia detailed, thorough
text: JAKARTA, February 25. /TASS/. The Secretary of the Russian Security Council, Sergey Shoigu, has summarized the results of his recent working trip to Indonesia, describing the negotiations as thorough and detailed. Shoigu arrived in Indonesia on Monday for talks with the country's top leadership on key issues regarding bilateral relations, including security and defense, as well as cooperation in other areas of mutual interest. "With regard to the discussions with the president and the Defense Ministry, they were indeed quite detailed and comprehensive. Most of the substantive conversations were distributed among various agencies and ministries. Our delegation is quite large, encompassing nearly all agencies involved in security, as well as those focused on the economy, agriculture, energy, banking, finance, and military cooperation," Shoigu stated. He emphasized that the discussions on most issues led to concrete outcomes, complete with figures, dates, and contracts specifying when and what agreements would be concluded and signed. Shoigu noted that investment activity was a separate focus of discussion, particularly in the areas of energy and the experience of Indonesian colleagues in protecting bioresources.

title: Russian Navy demonstrates readiness to respond to any challenges — chief commander
text: MOSCOW, February 25. /TASS/. The Russian Navy is ready to respond to any challenges in operationally significant areas of the World Ocean, Navy Commander-in-Chief Admiral Alexander Moiseyev said on Tuesday. "The Russian Navy continues its planned presence in operationally significant and important areas of the World Ocean and demonstrates its readiness to respond to tasks of any complexity and address them both on its own and jointly with the naval forces of other states. This is what we have witnessed during these maneuvers," the Russian Defense Ministry quoted the admiral as saying. The Navy chief expressed his gratitude to the crews of the Russian combat ships that had participated in the Komodo 2025 international drills off the coast of Indonesia. The final stage of the Komodo 2025 international naval exercise took place off the Island of Bali of the Republic of Indonesia on February 24. The exercise brought together over 20 combat ships of various states. Russia was represented in the drills by the Pacific Fleet’s corvettes Rezky and Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhapov and medium sea tanker Pechenga. Currently, the Russian naval task force continues accomplishing long-distance deployment missions in the Indian Ocean.
weapons: corvettes Rezky and Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhapov

title: Top Russian security official holds talks with Indonesian defense chief
text: JAKARTA, February 25. /TASS/. Russian Security Council Secretary Sergey Shoigu has held talks with Indonesian Defense Minister Sjafrie Sjamsoeddin, a TASS correspondent reported. Earlier on Tuesday, the top Russian security official, who is currently on a working visit to Indonesia, arrived at the country's Defense Ministry, where a welcoming ceremony took place. After the meeting, the parties exchanged souvenirs. Shoigu arrived in Indonesia on Monday. During his trip, he is expected to hold talks with the country’s top officials, discussing pressing bilateral issues, including those related to security and defense, as well as the development of cooperation in other areas of mutual interest.

title: Iran calls on world to scrutinize Israel's nuclear activities
text: DUBAI, February 24. /TASS/. The world should scrutinize Israel's nuclear activities and take them under control, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said in a speech at the Conference on Disarmament in Geneva. "The international community must hold Israel accountable in this area. While Iran is developing its peaceful nuclear program under international supervision and in accordance with the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons <...>, Israel is neither a party to any convention nor a member of an international nuclear arms control organization and continues its activities without any control or restrictions," Araghchi was quoted as saying by SNN television. According to the Iranian diplomat, Israel possesses weapons of mass destruction that "pose a serious threat to security in the region and the world." He called for a new comprehensive nuclear weapons convention. Concluded in 1968, the Non-Proliferation Treaty legitimized the nuclear arsenals of Russia, the UK, China, France, the US and Russia. Other countries, if they sign the accord, forfeit the right to build or acquire weapons of mass destruction. More than 190 countries are now party to the agreement. Israel, India and Pakistan never joined the treaty, while North Korea withdrew in January 2003.

title: Moscow City Court upholds three-year sentence for France’s Vinatier
text: MOSCOW, February 24. /TASS/. The Moscow City Court has upheld the conviction of French citizen Laurent Vinatier (recognized in Russia as a foreign agent), sentencing him to three years in a penal colony for failing to adhere to the conditions of his foreign agent status and for gathering classified information, a TASS correspondent reports from the courtroom. "The verdict of the Zamoskvoretsky District Court of Moscow dated October 14, 2024, shall remain unchanged, and the defense’s appeal shall be dismissed," the judge announced the decision. According to the TASS reporter, representatives of the Consulate General of France attended the hearing. In his appeal, Vinatier challenged the verdict by saying that the court of first instance had not properly taken into account all the mitigating circumstances: no criminal record, a doctorate in political science, two dependent children, as well as complete remorse for what he had done. The defense asked the court of appeal to throw out the sentence, or at least reduce it. In his final statement, the convict reiterated his full remorse, expressed "his love for Russia and the Russian people," and apologized. The Justice Ministry previously listed Vinatier as a foreign agent. He is an employee of the Swiss non-governmental non-profit organization Center for Humanitarian Dialogue, and was detained in Moscow in early June last year. On October 14, the Zamoskvoretsky Court of Moscow found him guilty under Part 3 of Article 330.1 of the Criminal Code of the Russian Federation (evading duties provided for by the legislation of the Russian Federation on foreign agents). The case was considered under special procedure. According to investigators, from April 23, 2021 to August 26, 2022, Vinatier collected military and military-technical information, as well as information on mobilization, of which he had had to notify the relevant Russian agency. The French citizen received information about the deployment of the Russian army on the territory of Ukraine, as well as information about forecasts of the course of military operations. According to the investigation, the information he received could be used against Russia.

title: Russian Security Council Secretary Sergey Shoigu arrives in Indonesia on working visit
text: JAKARTA, February 24. /TASS/. Russian Security Council Secretary Sergey Shoigu arrived in the Indonesian capital Jakarta, leading a delegation from a variety of agencies on a working visit, according to a TASS reporter. The delegation was met at the airport by Russian Ambassador to Indonesia Sergey Tolchyonov and the director of the Americas and Europe Department at Indonesia’s Coordinating Ministry for Political and Security Affairs, Vitto Tahar. The Russian Security Council said that on Monday Shoigu left for a working visit to Indonesia and Malaysia. The visit’s schedule includes talks with the country's top leadership on pressing issues of bilateral relations, including security, defense and the development of cooperation in other areas of mutual interest.

title: Komodo Exercise 2025 ended in Bali — Russian Defense Ministry
text: MOSCOW, February 24. /TASS/. The final stage of the Komodo Exercise 2025 with participation of more than twenty warships of different countries, including Russian warships, ended near the Indonesian coast, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "The final stage of the Komodo 2025 multilateral international naval exercise took place near the coast of the Island of Bali, the Republic of Indonesia. More than twenty warships from various countries participated in it. The Russian Navy was represented by Rezky and Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhalov corvettes, and by the medium-size marine tanker Pechenga of the Pacific Fleet," the ministry said. Warships participated during the exercise in the search of a civilian vessel in distress with a rescue operation on water. The search was performed in designated areas with the use of naval helicopters.

title: Russian Battlegroup West destroy 19 Ukrainian drone control centers
text: MOSCOW, February 24. /TASS/. Russia’s Battlegroup West units destroyed nineteen Ukrainian drone control centers and three Starlink satellite communication terminals over the past day, Battlegroup Spokesperson Ivan Bigma told TASS. "Nineteen drone control stations, three Starlink terminals, a Nota electronic warfare station and a field ammunition depot were destroyed," Bigma said. Air defense crews destroyed nine Ukrainian drones over the past day, he added. The Battlegroup East destroyed a M198 howitzer, two ammunition depots and three drone control centers of the enemy, Battlegroup Spokesperson Alexander Gordeev told TASS in his turn.

title: AI already being integrated into Russian equipment — First Deputy PM
text: MOSCOW, February 23. /TASS/. Neural networks are already being integrated into Russian military equipment to accelerate the destruction of its targets, Russian First Deputy Prime Minister Denis Manturov toldChannel One. "Primarily in data-processing and, of course, in the prompt destruction of the target," he said when asked in what way artificial intelligence technologies can assist the military. "This is already being integrated [into Russian equipment] today," he emphasized.

title: Russian troops liberate two communities in Donetsk region over past day
text: MOSCOW, February 23. /TASS/. Russian troops liberated two communities in the Donetsk region over the past day in the special military operation in Ukraine, Russia’s Defense Ministry reported on Sunday. "Battlegroup South units liberated the settlement of Ulakly in the Donetsk People’s Republic through decisive offensive operations… Battlegroup Center units completed the liberation of the settlement of Novoandreyevka in the Donetsk People’s Republic," the ministry said in a statement. Russia’s Battlegroup North inflicted roughly 30 casualties on Ukrainian troops and destroyed six enemy armored combat vehicles in its area of responsibility in the Kharkov Region over the past day, the ministry reported. "Battlegroup North units operating in the Kharkov direction inflicted losses on formations of a motorized infantry brigade of the Ukrainian army and a territorial defense brigade in areas near the settlements of Volchansk and Zolochev in the Kharkov Region," the ministry said. The Ukrainian army lost an estimated 30 personnel, six armored combat vehicles and two artillery guns, including a US-made 155mm M777 howitzer in that frontline area over the past 24 hours, it specified. Russia’s Battlegroup West inflicted roughly 190 casualties on Ukrainian troops and destroyed three enemy armored personnel carriers in its area of responsibility over the past day, the ministry reported. "Battlegroup West units improved their tactical position and inflicted damage on manpower and equipment of five mechanized brigades and an assault brigade of the Ukrainian army, a territorial defense brigade and a National Guard brigade in areas near the settlements of Kupyansk, Dvurechnaya, Peschanoye, Shiykovka, Zagoruikovka, Novaya Kruglyakovka, Staroverovka and Zelyony Gai in the Kharkov Region, Yampolovka and Yampol in the Donetsk People’s Republic," the ministry said. The Ukrainian army’s losses in that frontline area over the past 24 hours amounted to 190 personnel, two US-made M113 armored personnel carriers, a French-made VAB armored personnel carrier, five motor vehicles and four field artillery guns, including two NATO weapons, it specified. In addition, Russian forces destroyed a Nota electronic warfare station and an ammunition depot of the Ukrainian army, it said. Russia’s Battlegroup South inflicted roughly 165 casualties on Ukrainian troops and destroyed four enemy artillery weapons in its area of responsibility over the past day, the ministry reported. During the last 24-hour period, Battlegroup South "inflicted losses on formations of six mechanized brigades and an airmobile brigade of the Ukrainian army in areas near the settlements of Seversk, Dronovka, Verkhnekamenskoye, Novomarkovo, Belogorovka, Chasov Yar, Stupochki and Konstantinovka in the Donetsk People’s Republic," the ministry said. The Ukrainian army lost an estimated 165 personnel, a tank, an armored combat vehicle, four motor vehicles and four field artillery guns in that frontline area over the past 24 hours, it specified. In addition, Russian forces destroyed an electronic reconnaissance station and two ammunition depots of the Ukrainian army, it said. Russia’s Battlegroup Center inflicted roughly 365 casualties on Ukrainian troops and destroyed three enemy artillery guns in its area of responsibility over the past day, the ministry reported. During the last 24-hour period, Battlegroup Center units "inflicted losses on manpower and equipment of seven mechanized brigades, a jaeger brigade, an assault brigade and an assault regiment of the Ukrainian army and two National Guard brigades in areas near the settlements of Kotlino, Zverevo, Sergeyevka, Lenina, Beryozovka, Shevchenko, Udachnoye, Uspenovka and Andreyevka in the Donetsk People’s Republic," the ministry said. The Ukrainian army’s losses in that frontline area over the past 24 hours amounted to 365 personnel, an infantry fighting vehicle, two Kozak armored combat vehicles, five motor vehicles and three artillery guns, it specified. Russia’s Battlegroup East inflicted more than 150 casualties on Ukrainian troops and destroyed two enemy ammunition depots in its area of responsibility over the past day, the ministry reported. "Battlegroup East units continued advancing deep into the enemy’s defenses and inflicted losses on formations of a tank brigade, two mechanized brigades, an air assault brigade of the Ukrainian army, a marine infantry brigade and two territorial defense brigades in areas near the settlements of Konstantinopol, Razliv, Bogatyr, Komar and Burlatskoye in the Donetsk People’s Republic and Gulyaipole in the Zaporozhye Region," the ministry said. The Ukrainian army’s losses in that frontline area over the past 24 hours totaled more than 150 personnel, a Novator armored combat vehicle, five motor vehicles and four field artillery guns, it specified. In addition, Russian forces destroyed two ammunition depots of the Ukrainian army, it said. Russia’s Battlegroup Dnepr inflicted roughly 85 casualties on Ukrainian troops and destroyed two enemy ammunition depots in its area of responsibility over the past day, the ministry reported. "Battlegroup Dnepr units inflicted damage on manpower and equipment of four mechanized brigades, a mountain assault brigade and three coastal defense brigades of the Ukrainian army in areas near the settlements of Respublikanets, Vysokoye, Sadovoye and Dneprovskoye in the Kherson Region, Lobkovoye and Novodanilovka in the Zaporozhye Region," the ministry said. The Ukrainian army lost an estimated 85 personnel, seven motor vehicles, two artillery guns and two electronic warfare stations in that frontline area over the past 24 hours, it specified. In addition, Russian forces destroyed two ammunition depots of the Ukrainian army, it said. Russian forces struck Ukrainian military commanders’ gathering places over the past day, the ministry reported. "Operational/tactical aircraft, attack unmanned aerial vehicles, missile troops and artillery of the Russian groups of forces struck the infrastructure of military airfields, gathering places of Ukrainian military commanders and the sites of training operators of unmanned aerial vehicles, fuel depots and temporary deployment areas of Ukrainian armed formations in 142 locations," the ministry said. Russian air defense forces shot down 73 Ukrainian unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) and four Hammer smart bombs over the past day, the ministry reported. "Air defense capabilities shot down four French-made Hammer guided aerial bombs, a rocket of the US-made HIMARS multiple launch rocket system and 73 fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles," the ministry said. Overall, the Russian Armed Forces have destroyed 656 Ukrainian warplanes, 283 helicopters, 44,263 unmanned aerial vehicles, 596 surface-to-air missile systems, 21,713 tanks and other armored combat vehicles, 1,521 multiple rocket launchers, 21,980 field artillery guns and mortars and 32,059 special military motor vehicles since the start of the special military operation, the ministry reported.
weapons: special military motor vehicles since the start of the special military operation

title: Russia says its Ka-52M helicopter destroyed Ukrainian troops, vehicles in Kursk Region
text: MOSCOW, February 23. /TASS/. A Ka-52M helicopter struck Ukrainian troops and armored vehicles with missiles near the border in the Kursk Region, the Russian Defense Ministry said in a statement. "While performing a combat mission to support units of the battlegroup North, the crew of an army aviation Ka-52M helicopter, acting as part of a combined-forces tactical group, fired air-launched missiles at Ukrainian personnel and armored vehicles near the border area in the Kursk region," the statement said. Following the strike, the crews returned to the airport of departure. "A forward spotter confirmed the destruction of all Ukrainian targets," the ministry said.

title: Russian forces repel three Ukrainian counterattacks over day — top brass
text: MOSCOW, February 22. /TASS/. The Russian Armed Forces repelled three Ukrainian counterattacks in the Kursk Region over the past 24 hours, the Defense Ministry reported. "The battlegroup North defeated 16 Ukrainian brigades near Gogolevka, Goncharovka, Guyevo, Zaoleshenka, Kazachya Loknya, Kurilovka, Lebedevka, Loknya, Makhnovka, Novaya Sorochyna, Nikolsky, Oleshnya, Rubanshchyna and Sudzha. The battlegroup’s servicemen repelled three enemy counterattacks," the statement said. The Ukrainian losses in Russia’s Kursk Region amounted to more than 190 servicemen over the past 24 hours, the ministry added. "Over the day, Ukraine lost over 190 servicemen, three tanks, six infantry fighting vehicles, four armored personnel carriers, 15 armored fighting vehicles, 17 vehicles, an artillery gun, three mortars, two electronic warfare stations, as well as three drone control posts and an ammunition depot," the statement said. In total, during the hostilities in the Kursk area, Kiev lost more than 62,650 troops, 376 tanks, 286 infantry fighting vehicles, 237 armored personnel carriers, 1,977 armored combat vehicles, 2,129 vehicles, 465 artillery systems, 51 multiple rocket launchers, including 13 HIMARS and six US-made MLRS, 22 anti-aircraft missile systems, nine transport and load vehicles, 113 electronic warfare stations, 15 counter-battery radars, seven air defense radars, 49 units of engineering and other equipment, including 19 demolition vehicles, one UR-77 mine-clearing unit, as well as 14 armored maintenance and recovery vehicles, and a command vehicle.

title: Senior defense official inspects battlegroup South — top brass
text: MOSCOW, February 22. /TASS/. Chief of the General Staff of the Russian Armed Forces General Valery Gerasimov has inspected the battlegroup South in the special military operation zone and gave instructions for further actions, the Defense Ministry reported. "Army General Valery Gerasimov visited the command post of the battlegroup South in the zone of the special military operation. At the command post, the Chief of the General Staff heard a report from the battlegroup’s commander, Colonel General Alexander Sanchik, as well as unit commanders on the current situation in the area of responsibility and the progress of combat missions," the statement said. According to the ministry, when summarizing the results of the meeting, "the Chief of the General Staff praised the successes of the battlegroup's military units in carrying out combat tasks and gave instructions for further actions.".

title: Russian forces hit Ukrainian attack drone warehouses, drone operator sites — top brass
text: MOSCOW, February 22. /TASS/. The Russian military struck Ukrainian infrastructure over the past day, the Defense Ministry reported. "The Russian Armed Forces hit the infrastructure of Ukrainian military airfields, attack drone warehouses and drone operator sites, ammunition and fuel depots, as well as enemy temporary deployment sites in 157 areas," the ministry said. TASS has compiled the main information on the special military operation progress over the past 24 hours. The Russian Armed Forces have liberated the Novolubovka settlement in the Lugansk People's Republic, the Defense Ministry reported. "As a result of decisive actions of the battlegroup West, the Novolubovka settlement in the Lugansk People's Republic has been liberated," it said. According to the ministry, over the past 24 hours, the battlegroup defeated the manpower and equipment of six Ukrainian brigades, eliminating over 200 enemy servicemen and destroying a tank and a US-made M113 armored personnel carrier. The servicemen on the battlegroup South have defeated Ukraine’s Foreign Legion units in the Donetsk People's Republic (DPR), the Russian military reported. "The battlegroup South has taken more favorable lines and positions, defeating the manpower and equipment of six Ukrainian brigades and the Foreign Legion units. The battlegroup also eliminated up to 220 Ukrainian servicemen," the statement said. The ministry added that the Ukrainian military also lost an infantry fighting vehicle, nine vehicles and six field artillery guns, including a US-made 155 mm M777 howitzer, an electronic warfare station and an ammunition depot. Russia’s battlegroup Center has improved its tactical positions, eliminating over 375 Ukrainian servicemen, the Defense Ministry said. "The battlegroup Center defeated the manpower and equipment of 17 Ukrainian brigades. The enemy lost over 375 servicemen," the statement emphasized. The Ukrainian military also lost four armored combat vehicles, including a US-made MaxxPro armored vehicle, five vehicles and seven field artillery guns, including a US-made Paladin 155 mm howitzer, the ministry added. The battlegroup East eliminated up to 160 servicemen and destroyed a German-made Leopard tank over the past 24 hours, the ministry pointed out. "The battlegroup East continued to advance deep into the enemy's defense, defeating the manpower and equipment of five Ukrainian brigades. The enemy lost up to 160 servicemen, two tanks, including a German-made Leopard, three vehicles and two field artillery guns," the statement said. The battlegroups North and Dnepr wipes out up to 125 Ukrainian servicemen over the past 24 hours, the military said. The enemy also lost an armored personnel carrier, six vehicles, two artillery guns and four electronic warfare stations, as well as an ammunition depot. Russian air defenses have downed a Ukrainian Air Force’s MiG-29 jet, two Hammer bombs, two HIMARS rockets and 58 fixed-wing drones over the past 24 hours, the ministry pointed out. According to it, a total of 656 aircraft, 283 helicopters, 44,190 drones, 596 anti-aircraft missile systems, 21,698 tanks and other armored fighting vehicles, 1,521 multiple rocket launchers, 21,948 field artillery guns and mortars, as well as 32,033 special military vehicles have been destroyed since the start of the special military operation.

title: US to present its own UNGA resolution on Ukrainian conflict settlement on February 24
text: WASHINGTON, February 22. /TASS/. The United States will submit to the UN General Assembly on February 24 a draft resolution on the settlement of the conflict in Ukraine, Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on his X page. According to him, on Monday the US "will propose to the United Nations a landmark resolution the entire UN membership should support in order to chart a path to peace." Rubio also pointed out that US President Donald Trump is "committed to ending" the Ukraine conflict. A US State Department statement attached to the post indicated that Trump is committed to "ending the Russia-Ukraine war and to a resolution that leads to a lasting peace, not just a temporary pause." The draft resolution developed by the United States " is consistent with President Trump’s view that the UN must return to its founding purpose, as enshrined in the UN Charter." "If the United Nations is truly committed to its original purpose, we must acknowledge that while challenges may arise, the goal of lasting peace remains achievable," the statement added. The resolution also says that the conflict in Ukraine "is awful," and "the UN can help end it." "We strongly believe that this is the moment to commit to ending [the conflict]. [Adoption of the resolution] is our opportunity to build real momentum toward peace," the statement said.

title: Russia’s Battlegroup South inflicts roughly 230 casualties on Ukrainian army in past day
text: MOSCOW, February 22. /TASS/. Russia’s Battlegroup South inflicted up to 230 casualties on Ukrainian troops and destroyed three field ammunition depots over the past day in the special military operation in Ukraine, Battlegroup South Spokesman Vadim Astafyev told TASS on Saturday. "Enemy’s losses in that frontline area over the past 24 hours amounted to over 230 personnel, three armored combat vehicles, including a US-made M113 armored personnel carrier, three pickup trucks, four field artillery guns (two of them of produced in the West) and three field ammunition depots," Astafyev added. According to him, Battlegroup West units improved their tactical position and inflicted damage on manpower and equipment of Ukrainian army’s three mechanized brigades and an assault brigade near the settlements of Artyom, Predtechino, Seversk, Konstantinovka, Levanevsk, Verkhnekamensk, Chasov Yar and Ulakly.

title: Top defense official awards Gold Star medals to new Heroes of Russia
text: MOSCOW, February 22. /TASS/. Russian Defense Minister Andrey Belousov has awarded Gold Star medals to outstanding participants of the special military operation, the Defense Ministry reported. "Russian Defense Minister Andrey Belousov has awarded Gold Star medals and honorary weapons to Russian servicemen who showed courage and heroism while performing military duty," the statement said. "I can say with confidence that your heroic deeds deserve the deepest respect and are an example for the younger generation and all citizens," Belousov said at the ceremony, adding that he was familiar with the history of each awardee's heroism in detail. The top defense official congratulated the servicemen on the upcoming professional holiday, Defender of the Fatherland Day, and wished them good health and new achievements in military service. After the ceremony, Belousov held a meeting with the decorated servicemen.

title: Russia’s Battlegroup North inflicts up to 50 casualties on Ukrainian army in past day
text: MOSCOW, February 22. /TASS/. Russia’s Battlegroup North inflicted up to 50 casualties on Ukrainian troops over the past day in the special military operation in Ukraine, Battlegroup North Spokesman Yaroslav Yakimkin told TASS on Saturday. "North Battlegroup units in the Kharkov area delivered strikes on manpower and equipment of Ukrainian army’s mechanized brigade, an infantry mechanized brigade and a territorial defense brigade near the communities of Slatino, Svetlichnoye and Volchansk," he said. Enemy’s losses in that frontline area over the past 24 hours amounted to 50 personnel, two motor vehicles and three field artillery guns, Yakimkin added.

title: Russia develops Titan drone jamming system based on artificial intelligence
text: MOSCOW, February 21. /TASS/. The first drone jamming system Titan based on artificial intelligence has been developed in Russia and will be tested in late February. The system is equipped with a laser module with an operating range of 3 km, the press office of the United Russia Party said on Friday. "United Russia Central Executive Committee Head Alexander Sidyakin presented Russia’s first drone jamming system Titan based on AI to United Russia Party Chairman Dmitry Medvedev. As of now, it has already been upgraded not only with a physical impact complex but also with a laser module with an operational range of 3 km. This is one of the patented innovations of the Center of Unmanned Systems and Technologies. The tests will run in late February, after which the Russian army will use the system in the area of the special military operation," the press office said in a statement. The idea of the drone suppression system originated during discussions of the issues of protecting civilian infrastructure from terrorist attacks, Sidyakin added. "The Titan is outfitted with a 24-barrel 12-caliber turret with optical control and automatic reloading, a radio-electronic and optical surveillance system and a radio-electronic suppression capability. Therefore, an operator can promptly spot a drone and take measures for its radio-electronic suppression. If a drone continues its flight, it will be eliminated physically. The system is outfitted with automatic target acquisition based on a neural network. The Titan has been trained to identify a flying drone based on 120 million files," he elaborated.

title: Oreshnik missile warheads withstand heat equal to Sun’s temperature — Putin
text: MOSCOW, February 21. /TASS/. Warheads mounted on Russia’s Oreshnik intermediate-range hypersonic ballistic missiles withstand heat equal to the temperature on the Sun’s surface, Russian President Vladimir Putin said on Friday. "The entire world talks about the Oreshnik. And what are the materials? The temperature on these warheads corresponds to the temperature of the Sun, the temperature on the Sun’s surface," Putin said at a plenary session of the Forum of Future Technologies. "We understand this. We have been developing systems of the boost-glide vehicle that we called Avangard since the 1980s. It features comparable temperatures, slightly less than on the Sun’s surface," the head of state said. Russian scientists thought about creating such systems back in the late 1980s, Putin recalled. "But they could not because there were no materials. That was the problem. It flies and melts like an ice cream. And the control signal goes through," the Russian president explained, stressing that the innovations "are the result of the work on new materials."

title: UTS-800 training plane, Forpost UAV produced by Urals plant evoke interest at IDEX show
text: ABU DHABI /UAE/, February 21. /TASS/. The export versions of the UTS-800 training aircraft and the Forpost-RE medium-range UAV system developed by the Ural Civil Aviation Plant and unveiled at the IDEX 2025 international arms show evoked interest of Middle Eastern and Asian states, Ural Civil Aviation Plant Spokeswoman Yekaterina Zgirovskaya told TASS on Friday. "The products developed by the Ural Civil Aviation Plant have evoked the interest of both representatives of defense, security and law-enforcement agencies and business people. The visitors of the display stand of the integrated Russian exposition that featured the UTS-800 and Forpost-RE mockups inquired in detail about the characteristics of our vehicles," the spokeswoman said. Guests to the Russian exposition asked questions about ejection seats, the status of the UTS-800 program - whether this is a prototype or a flying plane - the advantages of the turboprop engine and the possibility of installing armaments. The Forpost-RE UAV system evoked the interest of visitors from the viewpoint of the use of artificial intelligence, air-launched weapons and possible payloads, she explained. The Forpost medium-range UAV system has proven its efficiency in real combat conditions. In its strike configuration, the system can carry missiles or glide bombs allowing it to deliver strikes on stationary and moving targets, including armor. The Forpost is capable of conducting optical, radio and radar monitoring and employing air-launched weapons. The payload includes replaceable modules with a thermal imaging camera, a color video camera on a gyro-stabilized rotating platform, a photo camera and other equipment. The UTS-800 training plane features high maneuverability and low operational costs. The aircraft trainer is outfitted with a turboprop engine, ejection seats, a standard oxygen system, an air conditioner, an anti-icing system and other equipment. An available G-suit ensures the pilot’s increased tolerance to piloting overloads. The airframe is made of domestic composite materials. The UTS-800 plane’s cockpit is similar to the cockpits of Russian-made combat aircraft.

title: Each Minuteman III launch helps pave way for new US Sentinel missile — expert
text: MOSCOW, February 20. /TASS/. Each launch of the US Minuteman III intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) contributes to the development of the new-generation strategic missile system known as Sentinel, according to Yevgeny Babichev, an expert in military cosmonautics who spoke with TASS. Earlier on Wednesday, the US Air Force Global Strike Command conducted a test launch of the Minuteman III ICBM from the US Space Force's Vandenberg base in California. Babichev noted that there have been approximately 300 such launches to date. While there is nothing particularly novel about this recent launch, the statements made by US officials regarding it warrant attention. "They (American officers - TASS) specify that the data collected and analyzed by the US military is crucial for maintaining the Minuteman III. Simultaneously, they are paving the way for Sentinel - the next-generation US strategic ICBM. It is also important to recognize that each launch, even of a serially built missile, is termed a test launch for a reason; it may test certain technologies and components for advanced weapon systems," the expert explained. He emphasized that the US is actively pursuing the systematic development of a new-generation intercontinental-range missile system. The launch on Wednesday is intended to ensure the combat readiness of the US strategic nuclear capability amid prolonged delays in the development of the new system. The US statement also indicated that Washington had notified Russia in advance of the launch. Babichev pointed out that Russia and the US continue to exchange notifications regarding ICBM and submarine-launched ballistic missile launches. "For the US administration that took office this year, this launch was the first. It is routine work and not particularly significant in terms of international relations. Therefore, it should not be viewed as a destabilizing factor between Russia and the US," the expert stressed. He added that the Minuteman III has been in service since 1970. "This missile is 55 years old and cannot be considered a competitor to our heavy systems currently in operation with the Strategic Missile Forces. Its counterpart is our decommissioned Topol system," Babichev concluded.

title: Russia’s Forpost drone can drop loitering munitions
text: ABU DHABI, February 20. /TASS/. The medium-range Forpost drone displayed for the first time at the IDEX international arms show can receive the function of dropping loitering munitions, Spokeswoman of the Ural Civil Aviation Plant Yekaterina Zgirovskaya said. "The Russian medium-range Forpost reconnaissance-strike complex with unmanned aerial vehicles can receive a possibility to release loitering munitions," she said. At present, the Forpost can carry missiles and glide bombs and strike stationary and moving armor. "The Forpost has been considerably upgraded since the start of batch production. New payloads have radically increased the range of engagement," she said. The drone can conduct optical, radio and radar monitoring and employ air-launched weapons. The plant can add payloads requested by foreign customers, Zgirovskaya said. "The Forpost helped the Defense Ministry master a new line of vehicles - airfield-based medium-range drones - and obtain the necessary experience. The producer mastered a new direction and created a product in demand," the spokeswoman said. Designers of the Forpost drone have created an option with a radar to monitor hostile objects and provide target acquisition, Zgirovskaya said. "Designers have provided new capabilities to the Forpost and equipped it with a radar. The task is to monitor ground and water objects, identify moving targets and provide target acquisition to Russian forces, including the Navy," she said. The Forpost can also operate as a re-transmitter of open and secure communications between military units in the zone of coverage, the spokeswoman said. The Forpost-R is a completely domestic vehicle. All imported components have been replaced. The payload includes replaceable modules with a thermal imaging camera, a color video camera on a gyro-stabilized rotating platform, a photo camera and other equipment. The Forpost-R is an upgrade of the Forpost drone. Only the lines of the hull remained unchanged. The drone is made of Russian materials and carries modern electronic equipment, communications and Russian software. The Forpost-R is powered by a Russian engine. It features increased capabilities thanks to its advanced design and can engage in reconnaissance round-the-clock with optical, electronic and radar means. New radio communications have expanded the engagement range by a hundred kilometers and provided higher protection against foreign electronic warfare. The drone weighs close to 500 kg. The flight duration is 18 hours and the top altitude is 6 km.

title: Belarus continues to exchange information with certain NATO countries — Defense Ministry
text: MINSK, February 20. /TASS/. Belarus continues contacts with some NATO member states for the purpose of information exchange, said Valery Revenko, head of the Department of International Military Cooperation at the Belarusian Defense Ministry. He made the statement at a meeting with representatives of the foreign military and diplomatic corps accredited at the ministry. "The Republic of Belarus is a country that is an integral part of the European geopolitical landscape. We continue to maintain contact with military diplomats of certain NATO member states in order to exchange information on operational and combat training activities both in Belarus and in Europe, clarify the views of the sides on the ongoing military and political developments," a statement from the Defense Ministry quoted Revenko as saying. According to the official, "the country's foreign policy activities, including in the military area, are based on strategic relations with the Russian Federation, comprehensive cooperation with friendly states within integration associations where Belarus is a member, strengthening of strategic, all-weather partnership with the People's Republic of China and multifaceted interaction with other states that respect the views and interests" of Minsk.

title: Active work underway to deploy Oreshnik hypersonic missiles in Belarus — top brass
text: MINSK, February 20. /TASS/. Belarus is working actively on the issue of deploying Russian-made Oreshnik hypersonic ballistic missile systems on its territory, Chief of the Belarusian Defense Ministry’s Department for International Military Cooperation Valery Revenko said on Thursday. "We are working on this issue. However, unfortunately, I can’t tell you details right now. The work proceeds very actively. If the heads of state have decided so, the decision will be implemented," the Belarusian defense official told reporters. Moscow and Minsk signed an interstate agreement on security guarantees of the Russia-Belarus Union State, following a meeting of its Supreme State Council on December 6, 2024. Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko said after the meeting that he had requested Russian leader Vladimir Putin to deploy Oreshnik hypersonic intermediate-range ballistic missiles in the republic. Putin said that Oreshnik missile systems could be deployed in Belarus in the second half of 2025. In late January, Belarusian President Lukashenko announced that the Russian Oreshnik hypersonic missile system would be deployed in the country "any day now." He further said that it could be deployed on the republic’s territory closer to the western Russian city of Smolensk.

title: Russia, Belarus to hold Zapad 2025 strategic drills in September — top brass
text: MINSK, February 20. /TASS/. Russia and Belarus will hold the Zapad 2025 joint strategic drills in mid-September, Chief of the Belarusian Defense Ministry’s Department for International Military Cooperation Valery Revenko said on Thursday, theBelTAnews agency reported. "The exercise is large and observable: it will bring together over 13,000 personnel. As of today, we are elaborating a position on inviting OSCE [Organization for Security and Cooperation in Europe] member states to this exercise," the Belarusian defense official said. Minsk "adheres to the Vienna Document 2011 [on Confidence-and Security-Building Measures]," he stressed. "However, I can’t say right now about inviting some states that have a negative or specific attitude to the fulfillment of this document precisely in relation to our state," he added. Last year, Poland, Estonia and Latvia did not invite Belarus to some drills, he recalled. "But again, we don’t’ say ‘no.’ We say that we will look at further steps that the leaders of those states and the heads of their defense ministries will take," Revenko said. As reported earlier, the General Staffs of the Armed Forces of Russia and Belarus have launched preparations for the Zapad 2025 strategic drills. Belarusian Defense Minister Viktor Khrenin has said that exercises of this scope make it possible "in practice to check the level of the regional grouping’s combat preparedness and the ability of troops to jointly accomplish assigned missions."

title: Foreign customers eye Russian battle-proven air defense systems — Almaz-Antey
text: ABU DHABI, February 20. /TASS/. Russia’s air defense systems feature large experience of their real combat employment that its potential enemy lacks and this evokes great interest of foreign customers, Almaz-Antey Group Deputy CEO for Foreign Economic Activity Vyacheslav Dzirkaln told TASS on the sidelines of the IDEX 2025 international arms show. "Customers display especially great interest in what has already been tested and what is already used. We have huge experience of the combat employment of this equipment in real conditions. This is what our potential enemy lacks. Yes, naturally, they supply their equipment to Ukraine and also try, in the first place, to get the necessary experience of its use. But we have placed this process on a very broad basis. These are not just trials at a proving ground, these are real combat conditions, in which engineers and designers carefully analyze all that is related to the use of equipment," the Almaz-Antey executive stressed. "All closely watch" the combat use of Russian equipment, he added.

title: Russian Uragan MLRS hits Ukrainian troops, weapons in DPR
text: MOSCOW, February 20. /TASS/. Russia’s Battlegroup Center hit Ukrainian manpower and weapons in the Krasnoarmeisk area in the Donetsk People’s Republic with the use of an Uragan multiple launch rocket system (MLRS), the Russian defense ministry said. "The crew of an Uragan multiple launch rocket system of the Battlegroup Center struck an area of the deployment of Ukrainian troops and weapons, as well as a munitions depot in the Kresnoarmeisk area," it said. Uragan is designed to destroy troops and armor, artillery, missile complexes, command posts, communication hubs and infrastructure. The long-range 220mm weapon can fire single shots and a full salvo from 16 tubes at targets on a territory of 40 hectares at a distance of 35 kilometers.

title: Ukrainian troops forcibly evacuating residents from Chasov Yar — expert
text: LUGANSK, February 20. /TASS/. Ukrainian troops are seeking to force residents out of the city of Chasov Yar in the Donetsk People’s Republic (DPR), Vitaly Kiselev, a military expert, told TASS. "People refuse to leave their homes. There were cases when people were evacuated by force. I think that the Russian Investigative Committee and Prosecutor General’s Office will probe into the Ukrainian army’s crimes in Donbass," he said, adding that Russian forces continue advancing toward Chasov Yar.

title: Ukraine’s losses in 2024 amounted to 590,000 troops — top brass
text: MOSCOW, February 20. /TASS/. Ukraine’s lost some 590,000 troops in 2024 alone, and more than one million since the beginning of the special military operation, First Deputy Chief of the General Staff of the Russian Armed Forces and head of the Main Operational Directorate of the General Staff Sergey Rudskoy said. "In 2024 alone, the number of those killed and wounded in the Ukrainian army stood at 590,000. And since the beginning of the special military operation, this figure has exceeded one million," he said in an interview with the Krasnaya Zvezda (Red Star) newspaper.

title: Russian presidential aide calls for balanced Navy development
text: MOSCOW, February 18. /TASS/. Presidential Aide and Chair of the Maritime Board Nikolay Patrushev called for balanced Navy development and updated governance system. "Gigantomania of the fleet or its neglect are unacceptable. Modern situation calls for updating the governance system of the fleet. Therefore, the Russian president decided to withdraw all fleets from the command of military districts and directly subordinate them to the Navy commander-in-chief," he said in an interview with aif.ru. "The development of the fleet needs to consider the national interests, geopolitical priorities, scientific and technical achievements, and economic capabilities of the country," he added. The Navy is receiving new warships and develops according to the tasks set by the national leadership. In particular, the Admiral Isakov frigate armed with Zircon hypersonic missiles was floated last year. "The Russian Navy already has three warships of the project. Another four frigates are at various construction stages," Patrushev said. Besides, the Rzhev small missile ship with Kalibr missiles is undergoing trials. The Knyaz Pozharsky strategic nuclear submarine has been floated and the Provorny corvette with guided missiles is completing the construction. "Minesweepers, ice-class patrol ships, upgraded missile boats, tankers and arms transportation ships are also constructed," he added. The historic experience shows that "Russia created unique warships and fought on them in the direst times. We have to remember that it is easy to lose the fleet but extremely difficult to restore it," Patrushev said.

title: T-90MS tank proves efficiency in Ukraine operation
text: ABU DHABI, February 18. /TASS/. The upgraded export option of T-90MS Proryv tank is causing major foreign interest as it has proved effectiveness in the special military operation in Ukraine, head of the Federal Service for Military-Technical Cooperation Dmitry Shugayev told TASS at IDEX show. "The armor confirms effectiveness in the operation and there is a major interest in T-90MS and its modifications. We display it with all systems that meet modern requirements," he said. Foreign partners are considering modernization of their Russian-made tanks. "There is interest in our reactive armor and drone protection means," he added. "Practically all partners want us to share the experience of combat engagement. The vehicles that have proved effectiveness trigger major interest of foreign customers. The issue of modernization is on the agenda of those customers, who already operate our armor. New antitank weapons appear and better protection against them is necessary," Shugayev said. He also said the Ukraine operation raised the foreign interest in Russian electronic warfare (EW) weapons and confirmed drone effectiveness. "The special military operation showed that drones are a powerful and effective weapon. Jamming means are necessary to protect objects and troops against them. All countries work to improve the quality and capabilities of electronic warfare. We are not behind them. The interest in our designs is high," he said.
mil_orgs: Federal Service for Military-Technical Cooperation Dmitry Shugayev

title: World's largest arms manufacturers show off products at IDEX-2025
text: ABU DHABI, February 17. /TASS/. The world's largest manufacturers of armaments and military equipment presented their products at the IDEX-2025 international show that opened in the Emirati capital on Monday, according to a TASS reporter. The exposition at the Abu Dhabi National Exhibition Centre showcases developments in the defense industry of the US, the UK, Germany, France and other countries that are considered major arms manufacturers. Such international companies as Boeing, Dassault Aviation, General Atomics, Lockheed Martin, Raytheon, Saab and Thales set up their stands at the show. One of the main pavilions belongs to the Emirati state conglomerate EDGE, which unites 25 defense companies. Organizers of the Emirati exposition told TASS that the conglomerate presented 46 new systems and solutions at the exhibition. Among them are the FA-400 patrol vessel, fully designed and built in the UAE, and the HT-750 heavy drone with reconnaissance, surveillance and cargo delivery capabilities. Seven new countries are participating in IDEX-2025: Cyprus, Ethiopia, Hungary, Latvia, Lithuania, Qatar, Romania and Hungary. "This year's exhibition features 41 national pavilions, the largest of which is the UAE pavilion with an area of 25,000 square meters. A total of 213 national companies are taking part in the event," ADNEC Group CEO Humaid Matar Al-Dhaheri told reporters. The Russian display with an area of almost 1,000 square meters is located in a common pavilion and unites more than 40 enterprises of the Russian defense industry. One of the main Russian novelties is the T-90MS tank, which is being demonstrated abroad for the first time in an updated layout. IDEX-2025 will be held in the Emirati capital from February 17 to 21. The previous exhibition, held in 2023, was visited by more than 132,000 people from 65 countries. According to the organizers, IDEX-2023 participants signed contracts worth $6.35 billion.

title: Rosoboronexport's order portfolio exceeds $60 bln
text: ABU DHABI, February 17. /TASS/. Rosoboronexport's order portfolio currently surpasses $60 bln, CEO of Rostec State Corporation Sergey Chemezov told reporters. "Today our order portfolio exceeds $60 bln. This is, to a certain extent, deferred demand," Chemezov said, specifying that he was talking about Rosoboronexport's entire portfolio. According to Chemezov, Russia is striving to fulfill these orders. "For the time being, we are doing this primarily for our own army, and when the opportunity arises, we have some overcapacity, that we use to manufacture products for export," he added.

title: Share of Middle Eastern, African countries in Rosoboronexport order portfolio totals 50%
text: ABU DHABI /UAE/, February 17. /TASS/. The share of countries in the Middle East and North Africa in the order portfolio of Rosoboronexport (part of Rostec) totals around 50%, Chief Executive Officer of Russia’s state arms exporter Alexander Mikheyev told TASS. "Rosoboronexport cooperates fruitfully with almost all countries of the Middle East and North Africa. As of today, their share in the company’s order portfolio is around 50%. We implement and discuss with partners projects on cooperation in the area of arms and military equipment for all types of armed forces," he said at the IDEX 2025 international arms show in Abu Dhabi. Considering the partners’ interest to develop their own defense industry, the company’s offers on implementation of projects in the field of technical cooperation on joint development and production of cutting-edge military products spark particular interest, the chief executive noted. "Now we implement more than 20 such projects with 10 countries and we rank the prospect of a serious growth in coming years as enormous," he said.

title: T-90MS tank enjoys latest technologies
text: ABU DHABI, February 17. /TASS/. The upgraded T-90MS Proryv tank displayed at IDEX show enjoys the latest technologies and new technical solutions, Rosoboronexport CEO Alexander Mikheyev told TASS. "The displayed tank received the latest technologies and new technical solutions. It has reactive armor, bar screens, electronic warfare against drones, and differentiated armor principle. It is therefore protected against FPV drones, antitank missiles and grenades," he said. The tank also has a new fire control system and integrated information-command system and other innovative solutions. T-90MS has a higher firepower due to new armor-piercing subcaliber, fragmentation shells with remote detonation, and guided missiles. "Our partners were excited when they learned that Rosoboronexport will bring T-90MS tank to IDEX 2025. We have numerously heard generals of various armies say it is the best tank," Mikheyev said. He expects major interest in the tank from Middle East and North African partners, who are well aware of the Russian armor and value its reliability and effectiveness. Other friendly countries are also invited to cooperate, he added.
