mil_units: Russian armor and value its reliability

title: Russia discusses fifth-generation aircraft joint design with partners
text: ABU DHABI, February 17. /TASS/. Russia is discussing joint design and production of fifth-generation fighter jets with foreign partners, Rosoboronexport CEO Alexander Mikheyev told TASS at IDEX show. "Projects are discussed for joint design and production of fifth-generation aircraft, adaptation of the latest Russian guided missiles to the aircraft of partner countries. Assistance is planned in the construction of overhaul and maintenance centers for armor and air defense means," he said. All the displayed exhibits meet the expectations of partners in the degree of adaptation to new realities and enjoy good prospects in the Middle East, he added.

title: Russia displays battle-tested weapons at IDEX show
text: ABU DHABI, February 17. /TASS/. Russia is displaying the latest weapons at IDEX show in Abu Dhabi that have been tested in high-intensity combat against western arms, Rosoboronexport CEO Alexander Mikheyev said told TASS. "Our partners in the Middle East are traditionally interested in high-tech weapons that correspond to the latest global trends. We display the biggest number of new weapons at IDEX 2025 that have been tested in high-intensity combat and encountered the latest arms of western countries," he said. The weapons include T-90MS main battle tank upgraded according to combat experience, Pantsir-SMD-E air defense complex with new guided counter-UAV TKB-1055 missiles, Kub-2-1E, Kub-2-2E kamikaze drones, Kornet-EM antitank launcher with remote control and guided Bulat missile, Berezhok combat module for infantry fighting vehicles, Lancet-E loitering munition. Air weapons trigger major interest and include low-signature precision new-generation Kh-69 cruise missile and Kh-58UShKE antiradar missile and Grom-E1 guided missile, which are displayed abroad for the first time, as well as smart K08BE smart bomb and UPAB-1500B-E guided glide bomb. "We are already holding consultations on the displayed samples with many partners also from the Middle East. We look forward to new fruitful projects," Mikheyev said.

title: Russia says Ukraine lost up to 50 troops from operations by battlegroups North, Dnepr
text: MOSCOW, February 16. /TASS/. Ukrainian forces lost up to 50 servicemen and an equipment deport from operations by the battlegroups North and Dnepr over the past day, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "In the Kharkov area, units of the battlegroup North struck manpower and equipment of a Ukrainian airborne assault brigade and a National Guard brigade near the settlement of Volchansk in the Kharkov Region. Ukrainian forces lost up to 30 servicemen, an armored personnel carrier, two motor vehicles and an artillery gun," it said in a statement. According to the statement, units of the battlegroup Dnept struck formations of three brigades of the Ukrainian coastal defense a territorial defense brigade in the areas of the settlements of Kamenskoye in Zaporozhzhye Region; Dniprovskoye, Ponyatovka, Verovka and Kachkarovka in the Kherson Region. Ukrainian forces lost up to 20 servicemen and 5 motor vehicles. Also destroyed was an equipment depot. Ukraine lost more than 165 servicemen in the area of responsibility of the battlegroup West over the past day, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "Ukraine’s losses totaled more than 165 servicemen, 4 armored fighting vehicles, 6 motor vehicles and 3 field artillery guns, including two of foreign-made units," it said in a statement. According to the statement, over the past day, units of the battlegroup West improved their tactical positions and struck the manpower and equipment of six Ukrainian mechanized brigades, a tank brigade, an assault brigade, am air assault brigade, a mountain assault brigade and two territorial defense brigades in the areas of the settlements of Zagoruykovka, Boguslavka, Figolevka, Petropavlovka, Kutkovka, Kupyansk, Monachynovka and Kolodeznoye in the Kharkov Region; and Drobyshevo and Yampolovka in the Donetsk People's Republic. Units of the battlegroup South improved their forward positions, as Ukrainian forces lost more than 180 servicemen over the past day, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "Units of the battlegroup South improved their forward positions and struck formations of two Ukrainian mechanized brigades, one infantry brigade, one airmobile brigade, a Marines brigade and a territorial defense brigade in the areas of the settlements of Serebryanka, Pereyezdnoye, Dolgaya Balka, Kleban-Byk, Konstantinovka, Chasov Yar and Ulakly in the Donetsk People's Republic and Belogorovka in the Lugansk People's Republic. The enemy’s losses amounted to more than 180 servicemen," it said. Ukrainian forces also lost four pickup trucks and an electronic warfare station. Two ammunition and materiel depots were destroyed. Fighters of the battlegroup Center improved their forward positions, as Ukraine lost up to 475 servicemen in the battlegroup’s zone of its responsibility, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "Units of the battlegroup Center improved their forward positions and struck manpower and equipment of three Ukrainian mechanized brigades, one airmobile brigade, one air assault brigade, two jaeger brigades, one assault brigade, an assault regiment and a National Guard brigade in the areas of the settlements of Kotlino, Krasnoarmeysk, Peschanoye, Andreyevka, Uspenovka, Ulyanovka, Zverevo, Udachnoye and Novoalexandrovka in the Donetsk People's Republic. The enemy lost up to 475 servicemen," it said. Ukraine also lost a US-made M113 armored personnel carrier, a UK-made Mastiff armored vehicle, eight motor vehicles and four field artillery guns. Russian air defenses shot down two HIMARS projectiles and 50 fixed-wing drones over the past day, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "Air defense capabilities shot down two rockets, fired with a US-made HIMARS multiple launch rocket system, and 50 unmanned fixed-wing drones," it said.

title: Russia says Ukrainian losses in Kursk Region topped 61,000 troops
text: MOSCOW, February 16. /TASS/. Ukraine’s losses in the Kursk Region have exceeded 61,000 servicemen, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "Since the beginning of hostilities in the Kursk Region, the enemy has lost more than 61,010 troops, 365 tanks, 273 infantry fighting vehicles, 219 armored personnel carriers, 1,886 armored fighting vehicles, 1,994 motor vehicles, 441 artillery units, 48 multiple launch rocket systems, including 13 HIMARS and six US-made MLRS, 21 anti-aircraft missile launchers, 8 reloading vehicles," it said in a statement. Ukraine also lost 109 electronic warfare stations, 15 counter-battery radars, 6 air defense radars, 48 units of engineering and other equipment, including 18 obstacle-clearing vehicles, one UR-77 mine-clearing vehicle, five bridge-laying vehicle, one engineering reconnaissance vehicle, 14 armored repair and recovery vehicles and a command and staff vehicle. Ukraine lost more than 230 servicemen over the past day in the Kursk Region, the Russian Defense Ministry also noted. "Over the past day, Ukraine lost more than 230 servicemen, 2 tanks, 5 infantry fighting vehicles, including one US-made Bradley IFV, 3 armored personnel carriers, 7 armored fighting vehicles, 8 motor vehicles, a self-propelled artillery unit and an artillery gun. Also destroyed were 5 UAV operating stations," it said. Russian forces repulsed three Ukrainian counterattacks in the Kursk Region over the past day, the ministry added. "In the course of offensive actions, units of the battlegroup North struck formations of two Ukrainian tank brigades, one heavy mechanized brigade, five mechanized brigades, an assault brigade, three air assault brigades, a Marines brigade, a Territorial Defense brigade and three assault regiments in the areas of Viktorovka, Guyevo, Kazachya Loknya, Kolmakov, Kurilovka, Lebedevka, Makhnovka, Nikolsky, Rubanshchina and Sverdlikovo. Three enemy counterattacks were repulsed," it said.

title: Russia says its forces struck Ukrainian military airfields, drone assembly workshop
text: MOSCOW, February 16. /TASS/. Russian forces hit Ukrainian military airfields, a drone assembly workshop and an energy facility over the past day, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "Tactical aviation, attack drones, rocket forces and artillery of Russian battlegroups struck the infrastructure of military airfields, a drone assembly workshop, an energy facility that supplies Ukrainian defense enterprises, as well as clusters of enemy manpower and equipment in 141 areas," it said. Russian air defenses shot down two HIMARS projectiles and 50 fixed-wing drones over the past day, the ministry added. "Air defense capabilities shot down two rockets, fired with a US-made HIMARS multiple launch rocket system, and 50 unmanned fixed-wing drones," it said. Ukrainian forces lost up to 50 servicemen and an equipment deport from operations by the battlegroups North and Dnepr over the past day, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "In the Kharkov area, units of the battlegroup North struck manpower and equipment of a Ukrainian airborne assault brigade and a National Guard brigade near the settlement of Volchansk in the Kharkov Region. Ukrainian forces lost up to 30 servicemen, an armored personnel carrier, two motor vehicles and an artillery gun," it said in a statement. According to the statement, units of the battlegroup Dnept struck formations of three brigades of the Ukrainian coastal defense a territorial defense brigade in the areas of the settlements of Kamenskoye in Zaporozhzhye Region; Dniprovskoye, Ponyatovka, Verovka and Kachkarovka in the Kherson Region. Ukrainian forces lost up to 20 servicemen and 5 motor vehicles. Also destroyed was an equipment depot. Ukraine lost more than 165 servicemen in the area of responsibility of the battlegroup West over the past day, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "Ukraine’s losses totaled more than 165 servicemen, 4 armored fighting vehicles, 6 motor vehicles and 3 field artillery guns, including two of foreign-made units," it said in a statement. According to the statement, over the past day, units of the battlegroup West improved their tactical positions and struck the manpower and equipment of six Ukrainian mechanized brigades, a tank brigade, an assault brigade, am air assault brigade, a mountain assault brigade and two territorial defense brigades in the areas of the settlements of Zagoruykovka, Boguslavka, Figolevka, Petropavlovka, Kutkovka, Kupyansk, Monachynovka and Kolodeznoye in the Kharkov Region; and Drobyshevo and Yampolovka in the Donetsk People's Republic. Units of the battlegroup South improved their forward positions, as Ukrainian forces lost more than 180 servicemen over the past day, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "Units of the battlegroup South improved their forward positions and struck formations of two Ukrainian mechanized brigades, one infantry brigade, one airmobile brigade, a Marines brigade and a territorial defense brigade in the areas of the settlements of Serebryanka, Pereyezdnoye, Dolgaya Balka, Kleban-Byk, Konstantinovka, Chasov Yar and Ulakly in the Donetsk People's Republic and Belogorovka in the Lugansk People's Republic. The enemy’s losses amounted to more than 180 servicemen," it said. Ukrainian forces also lost four pickup trucks and an electronic warfare station. Two ammunition and materiel depots were destroyed. Ukraine lost more than 155 servicemen in the zone of responsibility of the battlegroup East over the past day, the Russian Defense Ministry said in a statement. According to the statement, units of the battlegroup continued advancing deeper into the enemy's defense and struck manpower and equipment of four Ukrainian mechanized brigades, an air assault brigades, a Marines brigade and two territorial defense brigades in the areas of the settlements of Razliv, Burlatskoye, Komar, Novosyolka, and Konstantinopol in the Donetsk People's Republic; and Gulyaypole in the Zaporozhzhye Region. "The enemy lost more than 155 servicemen, a tank, an armored fighting vehicle, a motor vehicle and five field artillery guns," the statement said. Fighters of the battlegroup Center improved their forward positions, as Ukraine lost up to 475 servicemen in the battlegroup’s zone of its responsibility, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "Units of the battlegroup Center improved their forward positions and struck manpower and equipment of three Ukrainian mechanized brigades, one airmobile brigade, one air assault brigade, two jaeger brigades, one assault brigade, an assault regiment and a National Guard brigade in the areas of the settlements of Kotlino, Krasnoarmeysk, Peschanoye, Andreyevka, Uspenovka, Ulyanovka, Zverevo, Udachnoye and Novoalexandrovka in the Donetsk People's Republic. The enemy lost up to 475 servicemen," it said. Ukraine also lost a US-made M113 armored personnel carrier, a UK-made Mastiff armored vehicle, eight motor vehicles and four field artillery guns.

title: Kiev loses over 280 troops in Russia’s borderline Kursk area over past day
text: MOSCOW, February 14. /TASS/. The Ukrainian army lost over 280 troops, four infantry combat vehicles and seven armored combat vehicles in Russia’s borderline Kursk area over the past 24 hours, the Russian Defense Ministry reported on Saturday. "During the last 24-hour period, the Ukrainian army lost over 280 personnel, four infantry combat vehicles, a personnel combat carrier, seven combat armored combat vehicles, 12 motor vehicles, two mortars, an electronic warfare system, two bridge-laying vehicles, four armored recovery vehicles, including two German-made Bergepanzer-2, as well as three UAV control posts," the ministry said in a statement. In all, over the period of fighting in the Kursk frontline area, the enemy has lost more than 60,780 troops, 363 tanks, 268 infantry fighting vehicles, 216 armored personnel carriers 1,879 armored combat vehicles, 1,986 motor vehicles, 439 artillery guns, 48 multiple rocket launchers, including 13 HIMARS and six MLRS of US manufacture, 21 surface-to-air missile launchers, the ministry specified.

title: Russia’s Battlegroup East inflicts roughly 150 casualties on Ukrainian army in past day
text: MOSCOW, February 15. /TASS/. Russia’s Battlegroup East inflicted roughly 150 casualties on Ukrainian troops and destroyed an enemy tank in its area of responsibility over the past day in the special military operation in Ukraine, the Russian Defense Ministry reported on Saturday. "Battlegroup East units advanced deeper into the enemy’s defenses and inflicted casualties on formations of a tank brigade, two mechanized brigades of the Ukrainian army and two territorial defense brigades near the settlements of Konstantinopol and Komar in the Donetsk People’s Republic [DPR], as well as the settlements of Ternovatoye and Gulyai Pole in the Zaporozhye Region," the ministry said in a statement. "The Ukrainian army’s losses in that frontline area over the past 24 hours amounted up to 150 personnel, a tank, three armored combat vehicles, three motor vehicles, four field artillery guns, including two Western-produced artillery guns," it specified. The Russian forces also eliminated two field ammunition depots, the ministry added.

title: Russian Battlegroups North, Dnepr inflict 100 casualties on Ukrainian army in past day
text: MOSCOW, February 15. Russia’s Battlegroups North and Dnepr inflicted roughly 100 casualties on Ukrainian troops in their areas of responsibility over the past day, the Russian Defense Ministry reported on Saturday. "Battlegroup North units operating in the Kharkov direction inflicted casualties on formations of territorial defense brigades of the Ukrainian army near the settlements of Volchansk and Liptsy in the Kharkov Region," the ministry said in a statement. The Ukrainian army’s losses in that frontline area over the past 24 hours amounted up to 50 personnel, an armored personnel carrier, two motor vehicles, three artillery guns and an arms depot," it specified. The ministry added that Battlegroup Dnepr units inflicted damage on manpower and equipment of three coastal defense brigades of the Ukrainian army near the settlements of Sadovoye, Ponyatovka and Veletenskoye in the Kherson Region. "The Ukrainian army lost over 50 personnel, nine motor vehicles and an radio-electronic warfare station," the ministry said.

title: Ukrainian army controls 20% of Chasov Yar in DPR — expert
text: LUGANSK, February 15. /TASS/. Only 20% of Chasov Yar in the Donetsk People’s Republic (DPR) is controlled by units of the Ukrainian armed forces, military expert Andrey Marochko told TASS. "All in all, the enemy only controls around 20% of Chasov Yar, which is also decreasing," he said.

title: Russian forces almost fully control northern part of Chasov Yar — expert
text: LUGANSK, February 15. /TASS/. The northern part of Chasov Yar in the Donetsk People’s Republic (DPR) is almost fully controlled by the Russian forces, military expert Andrey Marochko told TASS, adding that the southern part of the town is half under Russia’s control. "Clashes in Chasov Yar are regular, though despite the fact that the enemy is standing fast, <…> almost the whole northern part of the town is under our control: a small portion of houses remains, which our troops are currently clearing from Ukrainian militants. Speaking about the southern part of the town, we control roughly half of it, with advances, confident though small, also in place," he said.

title: Russian company to display Forpost-RE medium-range drone at IDEX-2025 show
text: MOSCOW, February 14. /TASS/. The Ural Civil Aviation Plant is set to present its Forpost-RE medium-range drone at an international exhibition for the first time and will do so at the IDEX-2025 show in Abu Dhabi. "The Ural Civil Aviation Plant will present the Forpost-RE unmanned aerial vehicle system at an international arms exhibition for the first time. The global debut of the export version of the drone, which passed the test of real combat in the Middle East, is scheduled as part of the united Russian display at the IDEX-2025 international arms exhibition in Abu Dhabi," a company spokesman said. According to the spokesman, the system is designed to perform in a wide range of climatic conditions, such as temperatures ranging from - 40 degrees Celsius to + 50 degrees Celsius, to carry out reconnaissance and attacks over land and sea. It can be equipped with guided and unguided aerial bombs and missiles weighing up to 40 kilograms. The system features an automatic takeoff and landing system. Landing is helped by laser beams and visual sensors to keep track of the runway. The system allows landing in the absence of a connection to the global satellite navigation system. "The practice of the special military operation has shown that this class of drones has limited strike capabilities, and they are not required too much. What is much more important is the capabilities of targeting, along with reconnaissance and patrolling of vast areas: coasts, borders and so on. The competitive advantages of Russia’s Forpost-RE drone are its absolute protection from sanctions, as all of its components are made in Russia, and many years of successful experience in real-combat areas," the spokesman said.

title: Russia to display new UTS-800 trainer at IDEX show
text: MOSCOW, February 14. /TASS/. The Ural Civil Aviation Plant (UZGA) will for the first time display UTS-800 new export option of the trainer at IDEX show in Abu-Dhabi. "The world debut of the export mockup option is scheduled at the Russian display at IDEX 2025 show", the company said. UTS-800 was designed for primary training and selection of pilots. It trains flying the aircraft, navigation, extreme situations, flying by devices and in a group. UTS-800 has two pilots seating in a tandem. The cadet is in the front cockpit and the instructor behind. The jet can be piloted by one man without aircraft alignment. "UTS-800 trainer has good tactical and technical characteristics and is unified in ergonomic components with Russian combat jets. UTS-800 simplifies the training of pilots in countries that order Russian aircraft. The aircraft can be adapted to concrete mechanisms at customer request depending on the acquisition volume," it said. UTS-800 is powered by a turboprop engine and is equipped with ejected seats and an oxygen system, air conditioning, anti-icing system and other onboard equipment. There is an anti-overload suit for the pilot. The aircraft is highly maneuverable, enjoys a high horizontal speed and lifting power, and can operate day and night. The temperature range is minus 30 plus 45 degrees Centigrade. The maximum speed is 460 km/h, the flight range is 1,200 km, maximum altitude is 6.000 meters. The aircraft can take off from runways with artificial and dirt cover.

title: Aero India international aerospace show concludes in Bengaluru
text: BENGALURU /India/, February 14. /TASS/. The Aero India 2025 international aerospace show where Russia demonstrated over 500 military products, including the fifth-generation Su-57E multirole fighter jet as its key exhibit has concluded in Bengaluru. The Aero India air show was held on February 10-14 for the 15th time in its history on the premises of the Yelahanka Air Force base in Bengaluru in southern India. Overall, 150 foreign firms, including more than 10 major Russian defense holding companies participated in the air show. As the Russian state arms seller Rosoboronexport reported, Russia’s arms exports to India stand at about $80 billion. Russia offers India large-scale projects of jointly developing and manufacturing military aircraft, helicopters, air defense systems, armor and ammunition and jointly promoting these products in third countries. The export version of the fifth-generation Su-57 fighter jet that has proven its high efficiency in the special military operation in Ukraine was the main premiere of the air show in Bengaluru. The aircraft was piloted by merited test pilot, Hero of Russia Sergey Bogdan. Russia prepared a new flight program specially for the Aero India air show. The demonstration flights by the Su-57E fighter jet, during which Bogdan showed the skills of aerobatic maneuvers, enjoyed great success among the viewers. In addition, Russia’s state arms exporter demonstrated advanced air-launched missiles intended for the weapons suite of generation 4++ and fifth-generation Russian-and foreign-made fighters during its presentation at the Aero India air show. Russia’s Rosoboronexport demonstrated the latest mobile Pantsyr-S1M multichannel surface-to-air missile/gun system at the Aero India air show. The Pantsyr-S1M features improved performance characteristics, including an extended range and altitude of engaging aerial targets. In addition, Russia unveiled the Lancet-E unmanned aircraft system’s export version. The UAV has proven its high efficiency in the special military operation in Ukraine. Also, Russia’s Almaz-Antey Group unveiled information at the Aero India international air show on a new Gardenia two-container active jamming station designed to protect aircraft from air-to-air and surface-to-air missiles. The Gardenia two-container acting jamming station is designed for individual, individual-mutual and group protection of aircraft by interfering with airborne and ground-based radars and radar stations, the presentation materials said. The new jammer can be adapted to modern aircraft, including foreign-made airplanes. The Gardenia is mounted on two aircraft hardpoints. Its advanced information interaction tools allow the jammer to be adapted to radio-electronic equipment of various frontline aircraft, Almaz-Antey said. The segment of Russia’s air defense systems featured models of the weapons employed in the special military operation in Ukraine and distinguished by their high efficiency. In particular, Russia demonstrated the S-400 Triumf long-range anti-aircraft missile system and the Tor family of short-range air defense systems: the autonomous combat modules of the stationary and mobile Tor-M2KM systems, the Tor-E2 tracked and Tor-M2K wheeled vehicles. These air defense systems are designed to shield vital facilities and troops from air strikes, cruise, anti-radiation and other missiles, air bombs and drones.
weapons: S-400 Triumf long-range anti-aircraft missile system

title: Nuclear technologies in Kiev regime’s hands pose global security threat — Russian diplomat
text: MOSCOW, February 14. /TASS/. Nuclear technologies in the hands of Vladimir Zelensky’s criminal regime pose a grave threat to international peace and security, Russian Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova said in a statement on the Ukrainian army’s attack against the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant. The diplomat pointed out that overnight into February 14, news outlets had reported of another provocation staged by the Kiev regime, targeting the Chernobyl nuclear plant. "We have repeatedly warned that the Kiev regime is preparing such provocations. Unfortunately, our fears turned out to be justified. All this confirms that nuclear technologies in the hands of Zelensky’s criminal regime pose a grave threat to international peace and security," Zakharova pointed out. She stressed that the provocation was yet another one in a series of steps "by the Banderites, aimed at undermining nuclear safety and security." "The series includes a strike on the cooling tower of the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant (ZNPP) on August 11, 2024, the killing of the access control office chief at the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant on October 4, 2024 (the responsibility for which was claimed by the Ukrainians), an attack on cars carrying Russian service members and IAEA Secretariat personnel during a rotation attempt at the ZNPP on December 10, 2024, the disruption of two rotation attempts at the ZNPP on February 5 and 12 (artillery, mortar and drone attacks were carried out on February 12), as well as the shelling of the Zaporozhye Thermal Power Plant’s distribution system late on February 13," the Russian diplomat specified, adding: "All this can in no way be described as accidents or mistakes." According to the diplomat, the provocation against the Shelter at the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant is a continuation of reckless actions by the Kiev regime, which "is well aware of what it is doing." "In the past, Russia took part in international efforts to construct this facility, designed to address the aftermath of the 1986 Chernobyl disaster in Ukraine. However, what the Ukrainian armed forces are doing now is undermining those efforts," Zkharova concluded.

title: Russia, India may partner in drones development and production
text: BANGALORE, February 14. /TASS/. Joint development and production of unmanned aerial vehicles may be an area of the Russian-Indian technology collaboration, a spokesperson of the Russian arms export agency Rosoboronexport told TASS. "Collaborative development and production of unmanned aerial vehicles can be one of the areas of the Russian-Indian technology partnership," the spokesperson said. An obvious competitive advantage of Russian drones offered for export is the fact that their technical development relies on the experience of real use in high-intensity combat warfare, in conditions of being countered by modern air defense systems, the spokesperson noted. Another strong side of Russian export proposals in the drone and drone counteraction sphere is the high level of openness to technological cooperation. "Russia is ready to offer Indian partners collaboration in the drone sphere, which may comprise development of drones, localization of their production, transfer of technologies and creation of joint ventures and service centers, including regional ones," he added.

title: Politician says situation at Zaporozhye TPP ‘critical’ due to Ukrainian targeted shelling·
text: MELITOPOL, February 14. /TASS/. The situation at the Zaporozhye thermal power plant (TPP) remains critical, with significant damage caused by Ukrainian precise and intense artillery shelling, Vladimir Rogov, chairman of a Russian Civic Chamber committee, told TASS. "The shelling was deliberate and very intense, devastating. The enemy launched 15 artillery strikes on the territory of the Zaporozhye thermal power plant, which is situated in close proximity to the Zaporozhye nuclear power plant. It is a single power grid constructed during Soviet times. When we discuss the situation, of course, the condition at Zaporozhye TPP itself is very difficult. There is destruction there," Rogov said on Komsomolskaya Pravda radio. The Ukrainian military launched 15 artillery strikes on the Zaporozhye thermal power plant in the early hours of February 14. Essential power equipment was damaged, leaving more than 50,000 people in the region without electricity. No TPP staff or civilians were injured. In the morning, Energodar's acting mayor Maxim Pukhov said that as a result of emergency measures, power supply had been restored in most parts of Energodar, and work was continuing. The Energy Ministry told TASS that specialists had begun work to evaluate the extent of the damage to the Zaporozhye TPP and assess the possibility of repairs. At the same time, Pukhov noted that the thermal power plant has not produced electricity for more than two years, as has the nearby Zaporozhye nuclear power plant. The thermal power plant only serves as an intermediary switchgear for electricity coming into the city from other regions. However, due to the low voltage in the network, the water supply in Energodar was temporarily disrupted. At the same time, the situation with heating in the city remains under control, as residential buildings and social facilities are heated with the help of gas boilers installed earlier.

title: Russia designs robot turret for assault units
text: MOSCOW, February 14. /TASS/. The designers of Varan tracked platform and of DUP-2 turret joined efforts to create a mobile robot for assault actions. The unmanned vehicle has successfully passed trials close to combat ones, the Popular Front told TASS. "The main task of the vehicle is to drive as close to hostile positions as possible, find the target and destroy it. The control distance is up to 15 kilometers. There are daytime and night monitoring regimes and stabilization in motion. We shall soon send the first samples to the front line for tests in combat conditions," Popular Front representative from Ivanovo region Vladimir Krupin said. The robot has successfully passed the trials in close to combat conditions, the Popular Front said.

title: Russian defense firm to demonstrate shipborne air defense systems at IDEX 2025 arms show
text: MOSCOW, February 14. /TASS/. Russia’s Almaz-Antey Group will demonstrate shipborne air defense systems at the IDEX 2025 international arms show that will run in Abu Dhabi in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) on February 17-21, the Group’s press office said on Friday. "The segment of sea-based air defense systems will feature the Komar turret mount designed for the automatic launch of Igla missiles to protect surface ships in the ultra-short-range area from anti-ship missiles, aircraft and helicopters under natural and radio interference. It is also capable of striking ground and naval targets by Ataka missiles," the press office said in a statement. The same segment will also demonstrate a self-contained combat module of the Tor-M2KM surface-to-air missile system. Its design allows for mounting it on the decks of vessels and the roofs of buildings for combat use. The surface-to-air missile system can be employed for air defense of vital military and government installations against strikes by aircraft, helicopters, cruise, anti-radiation and other missiles, and also against other air attack weapons.

title: Russia’s Almaz-Antey to feature battlefield air defense systems at IDEX arms show
text: MOSCOW, February 14. /TASS/. Russia’s Almaz-Antey Group will feature battlefield air defense systems at the IDEX 2025 international arms show that will run in Abu Dhabi in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) on February 17-21, the Group’s press office said on Friday. "During the exhibition’s work, visitors will be able to see battlefield air defenses systems produced by the Group. In particular, its display stand will demonstrate the S-300V4 Antey-4000 surface-to-air missile system and the Viking anti-aircraft missile system," the Almaz-Antey Group said. The S-300V4 Antey-4000 long-range surface-to-air missile system is designed to engage existing and prospective tactical and strategic aircraft, including those based on Stealth technology, medium-range ballistic missiles, cruise missiles, airborne early warning and control systems, target acquisition and fire control systems and loitering jammers. "The Viking all-weather, multichannel, highly mobile medium-range surface-to-air missile system is designed to provide air defense for troops and various installations against massive strikes by enemy modern and advanced air attack weapons in a contested electronic warfare and fire intensive environment. The Viking’s combat capabilities can be manufactured on both tracked and wheeled cross-country platforms," the press office said. In addition, the product line of point air defense systems will also feature the S-350E Vityaz surface-to-air missile launcher. The Vityaz is capable of simultaneously repelling strikes of various types of air attack weapons from any direction. The S-350E Vityaz system features increased survivability achieved through its ability to disperse combat vehicles over a sufficiently large distance, and also the capability to maneuver and change positions within a short period of time. Also, the S-350E can operate both autonomously and under the control of higher-level command posts.

title: Russia’s Su-57 fighter jet outshines US F-35 aircraft by maneuverability — Rostec
text: MOSCOW, February 13. /TASS/. The Russian fifth-generation Su-57 fighter jet features enhanced maneuverability and outshines the US F-35 aircraft, Russia’s state tech corporation Rostec said on its Telegram channel, uploading a video of flight programs at the Aero India 2025 international air show in Bengaluru. "Our Su-57 outperformed the American F-35 in the sky of Bengaluru," Rostec said. At the Aero India air show, the Russian Su-57 and the American F-35 demonstrated their performance during flight programs. The Su-57’s performance lasted about seven minutes, during which the fifth-generation Russian fighter jet flew at extremely low altitudes of 100 meters and above. Over the designated time, the Su-57 performed more than 20 aerobatic maneuvers, including Pugachev’s Cobra, the Bell, the Barrel Roll and others, Rostec specified. The latest Russian fighter jet features many unique solutions valued by the domestic design school, for example, the integral layout, perfect aerodynamics and vectored thrust engines, it explained. "All these factors enhance maneuverability. The better the maneuverability, the greater the chances to get away from enemy missiles while suppressing its air defense system. This also increases the speed of the combat aircraft’s reaction to a changing environment," Rostec emphasized. The Russian Su-57 and the American F-35 are multirole stealth aircraft but it is not quite correct to compare them, the state tech corporation explained. "The F-35 is largely a light attack aircraft for strikes on ground targets while the Su-57 is a heavy multirole jet for gaining supremacy in the air, strikes on ground targets and command and control of other aircraft," Rostec said in a statement. The Russian Su-57 fighter jet drew major attention both during its flight performance and at the exposition of the state arms exporter Rosoboronexport at the Aero India air show: Indian Air Force pilots stood in line to view the jet’s VR simulator, Rostec said. "Today the Su-57 is the world’s sole fifth-generation fighter that has proven its efficiency in real combat conditions to counter Western-made air defense systems," it said. The Su-57 is capable of employing a broad arsenal of precision weapons and features stealth capability against enemy radars, Rostec said. "The aircraft’s export version evokes great interest of specialists and foreign customers," the Russian state tech corporation said.

title: Russia, Tajikistan work to improve efficiency of Russia's 201st military base — Lavrov
text: MOSCOW, February 13. /TASS/. Russia and Tajikistan are collaborating to enhance the efficiency of Russia's 201st military base, Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov stated at a news conference following discussions with Tajik Foreign Minister Sirojiddin Muhriddin. "Our joint efforts to ensure security are multifaceted. As I mentioned, the 201st military base in Tajikistan has been operational for a considerable time and plays a vital role in safeguarding not only Tajikistan but also the southern borders of the Collective Security Treaty Organization (CSTO). We are working closely with our Tajik partners to improve its efficiency, including the development of the aviation component, which has seen significant success," Lavrov remarked. Lavrov emphasized that the two countries also have a bilateral agreement for the modernization of Tajikistan's Armed Forces. "We have plans to accelerate this program. At the end of last year, the summit of the CSTO Collective Security Council adopted a program aimed at strengthening the border between Tajikistan and Afghanistan. This initiative is now beginning to take shape. In the very near future, we will determine who will be responsible for implementing this multilateral document," he added. "And, of course, concerning the Afghan threat, we have established mechanisms within both the CSTO and the Shanghai Cooperation Organization that are currently operational. Additionally, together with Tajikistan, we participate in several regional groups, including the Moscow platform and other mechanisms involving neighboring countries. Today, we discussed the possibility of expanding the structures through which we can collaborate," Lavrov concluded.

title: Tajik, Russian special services successfully fight terrorism together — Foreign Ministry
text: MOSCOW, February 13. /TASS/. The special services of Tajikistan and Russia are doing good work together in the fight against international terrorism, Tajik Foreign Minister Sirodjiddin Muhriddin said after talks with his Russian counterpart Sergey Lavrov. "Our special services cooperate well in thwarting the activities of accomplices of international terrorist organizations," he said. According to Mukhriddin, Dushanbe and Moscow are actively working together within the framework of the Collective Security Treaty Organization (CSTO), leading to the signing of a targeted interstate program by CSTO states in November 2024 aimed at strengthening Tajikistan's border with Afghanistan. "The SCO (Shanghai Cooperation Organization - TASS) agreed that Dushanbe and Moscow will take steps to strengthen the Central Asian focus of this organization, as well as to establish the SCO Anti-Drug Center in Dushanbe and transform the RATS (Regional Anti-Terrorist Structure - TASS). The SCO has become a universal center," Muhriddin added. Russian Ambassador to Tajikistan Semyon Grigoriyev earlier said in an interview with TASS that the republic's law enforcement agencies were actively involved in investigating the circumstances of the terrorist attack at the Crocus City Hall concert venue near Moscow last March, and cooperation with Russia on this issue continues.

title: Exercise Zapad-2025 does not pose threat to other countries — Belarusian Security Council
text: MINSK, February 13. /TASS/. The Belarus-Russia joint exercise Zapad-2025 is a planned event that poses no threat to any other states, according to Alexander Volfovich, the State Secretary of the Security Council of Belarus. "The accusations from the collective West - that we pose a threat - are completely unfounded. Recently, Western leaders have claimed that we are plotting aggression and that we represent a threat from the East. They express concern over the Belarusian-Russian exercise scheduled for 2025, but there is nothing extraordinary about it," he stated, as reported by the Belarusian Defense Ministry's press service on its Telegram channel. Volfovich noted that these exercises have been held regularly since 2009, occurring every two years in alternating locations in Belarus and Russia. "The joint exercises planned for this year are intended for routine practice and to assess the readiness of regional troop groups to defend the territory of the Union State," he added.

title: Russia to demonstrate Supercam S350 drone at IDEX-2025
text: MOSCOW, February 13. /TASS/. Russia’s Unmanned Systems Group will unveil its dual-use flagship drone Supercam S350 at the IDEX-2025 international arms exhibition in the UAE, an official spokesman of the company told TASS. "The Unmanned Systems Group for the first time takes part in the IDEX. The whole model line of unmanned aerial systems will also be presented in the company’s catalog: the Supercam SX350 tilt-rotor drone, the Supercam S150 small-size fixed-wing type drone, and Supercam X4 and Supercam X4E copters," the spokesperson said. The Supercam S350 project is continuously evolving and acquiring new capabilities along with improvement of its characteristics and the tactics of use. The drone is resistant to challenging weather conditions and high temperatures, and does not require runways and auxiliary infrastructure for operations.

title: Moscow, Dushanbe plan to boost counterterrorism cooperation — MFA
text: MOSCOW, February 13. /TASS/. Russia and Tajikistan plan to work together more closely to combat terrorism and the illegal drug trade, Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said at a press conference following talks with his Tajik counterpart Sirojiddin Muhriddin. "Taking into account the constantly growing global challenges and threats, we reviewed the prospects of enhancing cooperation in the area of counterterrorism and combating illegal drug trafficking," he noted. Russia's top diplomat pointed out that security issues in the Central Asian region persist, and drew attention to the situation in Afghanistan in this regard. "It is clear that the situation in this state remains difficult, and it impacts neighboring nations, the countries of Central Asia, including Tajikistan," he stated. "It is in our interest to continue communication on this complicated topic through multilateral frameworks, which include the Moscow format of consultations on Afghanistan and the mechanism of Afghanistan's neighboring countries. There are related mechanisms in the Collective Security Treaty Organization (CSTO) and the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO). Recently, a format such as regional dialogue through security councils has also been established." Moreover, Lavrov voiced Russia’s support for Tajik President Emomali Rakhmon's initiative to increase the efficiency of efforts in the field of information security. "We also noted positive bilateral cooperation on biological security issues between Russia and Tajikistan and agreed to actively promote this topic in the multilateral format within the CSTO," he added, summarizing the talks.

title: Ukrainian forces terrorize ZNPP, while Russia provides security to IAEA experts — diplomat
text: MOSCOW, February 13. /TASS/. Ukrainian army continues to terrorize the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant (ZNPP) and Energodar, while Russia is committed to ensuring the security of the experts of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) stationed at the plant, the Russian Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova said. "Ukrainian troops continue to terrorize the plant itself and the adjacent city of Energodar," the diplomat noted. "Russia is doing everything in its power to ensure the safe presence of the agency’s secretariat’s specialists at the ZNPP." The spokeswoman emphasized that "the IAEA Secretariat’s experts have seen at first hand that Russia never conducted any ‘attacks’ from the area of the ZNPP or stationed any heavy weapons there, while doing everything necessary to maintain the security and integrity of the plant." The agency’s specialists "confirm that the ZNPP is operating normally and note the high level of professionalism and work of the plant’s employees," she added. "Unfortunately, the most important thing that has not been achieved yet is the cessation of Ukrainian provocations against the ZNPP," Zakharova said. She expressed gratitude to the IAEA experts for "maintaining their presence there despite the constant threat of Ukrainian attacks, significantly contributing to the common efforts with Russia to ensure the security of the ZNPP and to mitigate any possible consequences of Ukrainian attacks." "Russia will continue to cooperate with the IAEA Secretariat to ensure the safety of the agency's representatives’ presence and work at the plant," the diplomat stated. On February 12, Zakharova said that a routine rotation of the IAEA Secretariat’s experts, which had previously been postponed from February 5 because of Ukraine, was once again blocked. According to her, this situation stems from the provocative actions by Ukrainians who refused to let the experts "who had been waiting for a possibility to replace their colleagues at the plant since early February" pass. Yevgenia Yashina, the plant’s spokeswoman, told TASS that the agency’s experts who were forced to return to the ZNPP would continue to observe and assess the safety of the Zaporozhye NPP until the rotation resumes. On February 7, IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi stated after a meeting with Russia’s Rosatom State Atomic Energy Corporation chief Alexey Likhachev that expert rotations at the ZNPP would resume in the coming days. Earlier, the Ukrainian Foreign Ministry claimed that a routine rotation of the international organization’s observers had been postponed allegedly due to Russia’s failure to provide security guarantees. Russian Permanent Representative to International Organizations in Vienna Mikhail Ulyanov reiterated that Ukraine was distorting facts when it spoke about a lack of security guarantees from Russia for the IAEA expert rotation at the Zaporozhye NPP. He clarified that Kiev had tried to impose new requirements that went beyond established practices.

title: FSB prevents terrorist attack at Pskov railway station, eliminates perpetrators
text: MOSCOW, February 13. /TASS/. Russia’s Federal Security Service (FSB) has prevented a terrorist attack at the Pskov railway station and eliminated the members of a cell of an international terrorist organization who were preparing it, the FSB press office said. "The FSB has disrupted in the Pskov Region the activities of a cell of the international terrorist organization banned in Russia, whose members, citizens of one of the Central Asian countries, were planning to carry out a terrorist act," the statement said. "The attackers, on the instructions of a handler of the terrorist organization residing abroad, intended to detonate an explosive device at the Pskov railway station. After the terrorist attack, they planned to flee abroad, to one of the countries of the Middle East," the press office pointed out. "During the operation, the terrorists opened fire on the FSB officers and were killed by retaliatory fire," the FSB said. According to the special service, in preparation for the attack, members of the cell conducted reconnaissance of the area, purchased components for the manufacture of a homemade explosive device, and began to assemble it. During the raid, the FSB found and confiscated firearms, ammunition, and components of the improvised explosive device at their residence. Russian investigators opened a criminal case under Articles 30 and 205 of the Russian Criminal Code ("Preparation to Commit a Terrorist Attack by a Group of Persons by Prior Conspiracy").

title: FACTBOX: Disruption of IAEA experts’ rotation in ZNPP
text: MELITOPOL, February 13. /TASS/. Experts of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) who returned to the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant (ZNPP) after the Kiev regime blocked the IAEA rotation will continue to perform their work, the plant’s spokeswoman, Yevgenia Yashina, told TASS. On February 12, Russia’s Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova reported the disruption of the rotation. TASS has gathered the key updates on the situation. - A routine rotation of the IAEA Secretariat’s experts, which had previously been postponed from February 5 to February 12 because of Ukraine, was once again prevented, the Russian Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova reported. - According to her, the convoy of Russian troops and the IAEA Secretariat’s experts leaving the ZNPP was targeted by drones and mortars. - Experts from the IAEA mission are safe, following a Ukrainian assault, thanks to the efforts of Russian servicemen, according to Yevgeny Balitsky, the Governor of the Zaporozhye Region. - The agency’s experts continue to observe and assess the operational and physical safety of the Zaporozhye NPP until the rotation resumes, the plant’s spokeswoman, Yevgenia Yashina, said. - According to her, the vehicles in which the IAEA experts were during the rotation are intact. - The disruption of the rotation of the IAEA experts at the ZNPP was caused solely by the actions of the Ukrainian side, while Russia met all its obligations to ensure security, the Russian Defense Ministry said. - The disruption of Wednesday’s rotation of the IAEA experts stationed at the ZNPP was completely unacceptable due to security risks, the agency’s Director General Rafael Grossi stated. - On February 7, IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi stated after a meeting with Russia’s Rosatom State Atomic Energy Corporation chief Alexey Likhachev that expert rotations at the ZNPP would resume in the coming days. - Earlier, the Ukrainian Foreign Ministry claimed that a routine rotation of the international organization’s observers had been postponed allegedly due to Russia’s failure to provide security guarantees. - Russian Permanent Representative to International Organizations in Vienna Mikhail Ulyanov reiterated that Ukraine was distorting facts when it spoke about a lack of security guarantees from Russia for the IAEA expert rotation at the Zaporozhye NPP. - He clarified that Kiev had tried to impose new requirements that went beyond established practices.

title: Russian naval base in Sudan to strengthen Russia's presence in region — expert
text: MOSCOW, February 13. /TASS/. The establishment of a logistics support facility for the Russian Navy in Sudan will enhance Russia's geopolitical influence in the region and the Indian Ocean as a whole, while also securing the Arabian coast, military expert Alexander Stepanov, program director of the Academy of Political Sciences and senior researcher at the Institute of Latin America of the Russian Academy of Sciences, told TASS in an interview. "If a positive decision is made, it will lay significant groundwork for projecting our geopolitical influence in the region, providing opportunities to monitor shipping, maintain security in the Suez Canal and the Red Sea, ensure safety along the Arabian coast, and generally assert our military presence in the Indian Ocean," the expert noted. He further emphasized that, in light of the sabotage campaign instigated by the United States against both the Russian civilian fleet and partner organizations that facilitate the logistics of hydrocarbons and other goods, this initiative is vital for Russia to preserve the balance of power. It offers the possibility of controlling maritime trade routes and ensuring the safety of its fleet. Additionally, the expert highlighted that the new logistics center would serve as a base for Russia's collaboration with regional partners, creating more opportunities for joint maritime patrols and military cooperation with China and Iran. The agreement between Russia and Sudan to establish a logistics center for the Russian Navy was announced in early December 2020. The facility will be used for repairs, resupply, and rest and recreation for Russian naval crews. Currently, Sudan and Russia have reached a mutual understanding regarding the agreement on the Russian Navy base. Following talks in Moscow with his Russian counterpart Sergey Lavrov, Sudanese Foreign Minister Ali Youseff Ahmed al-Sharif stated that the parties have "agreed on everything.".

title: Russian Navy base in Sudan to provide access to Indian Ocean — expert
text: MOSCOW, February 13. /TASS/. The creation of a Russian naval base or a logistics center in Sudan will allow the Russian Navy to operate freely in the Indian Ocean, Alexander Ivanov, Director of the Commonwealth of Officers for International Security, told TASS. His comments came after discussions between Sudanese Foreign Minister Ali Yousef Sharif and his Russian counterpart Sergey Lavrov, where Khartoum and Moscow had reached an agreement regarding a Russian naval base and "agreed on everything." "Such a base could also become the first installation of the Russian navy in the Red Sea, which would grant the Russian fleet unrestricted access to the ocean. The most direct route between the Indian Ocean, the Mediterranean Sea, and the Atlantic Ocean passes through the waters of the Red Sea. This allows maritime traffic to flow in both directions between Europe and Asia without bypassing Africa," Ivanov stated. He mentioned that the base would have significant geopolitical value. "The base is located in a strategically beneficial position, through which a major portion of global maritime trade routes pass, including the transport of hydrocarbons from the Persian Gulf countries," he continued. According to Ivanov, the countries are now actively discussing details of the agreement and timeline of the project.
mil_units: major portion of global maritime trade routes

title: IAEA experts to continue their duties until rotation resumes at Zaporozhye NPP
text: MELITOPOL, February 13. /TASS/. Experts of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) who returned to the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant (NPP) after the Kiev regime blocked the IAEA rotation will continue to perform their work, the plant's spokeswoman, Yevgenia Yashina, told TASS. "All the experts have returned to the Zaporozhye NPP after the hostile actions of Ukrainian troops who hindered the IAEA rotation. Everything is fine with them. The vehicles in which the IAEA experts were during the rotation are intact. The agency's experts continue to observe and assess the operational and physical safety of the Zaporozhye NPP until the rotation resumes," Yashina said. On February 12, Russian Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova said that a routine rotation of the IAEA Secretariat’s experts, which was previously postponed from February 5 to February 12 because of Ukraine, had once again been prevented. According to the diplomat, this occurred due to the deliberate actions of the Ukrainians as they refused to allow the passage of the agency experts "who had been waiting for the opportunity to replace their colleagues since the beginning of February." IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi stated after a meeting with Russia’s Rosatom State Atomic Energy Corporation chief Alexey Likhachev on February 7 that expert rotations at the Zaporozhye NPP would resume in the coming days. Earlier, the Ukrainian Foreign Ministry claimed that a routine rotation of the international organization’s observers had been postponed allegedly due to Russia’s failure to provide security guarantees. Russian Permanent Representative to International Organizations in Vienna Mikhail Ulyanov reiterated that Ukraine was distorting facts when it spoke about a lack of security guarantees from Russia for the IAEA expert rotation at the Zaporozhye NPP. He clarified that Kiev had tried to impose new requirements that went beyond established practices.

title: Rosoboronexport signs contracts worth $4.5 billion with 15 countries in 2025
text: MOSCOW, February 13. /TASS/. The Russian complex approach to the design and modernization of weapons helped Rosoboronexport sign contracts with 15 countries worth $4.5 billion in 2025, CEO Alexander Mikheyev said. "Rosoboronexport will display at IDEX 2025 a maximum of new designs of the Russian defense industry. They include the best in the world T-90MS tank with a renewed image, Pantsir-SMD-E air defense complex, Kub new-generation loitering munition, Kornet-EM antitank missile launcher with a remote control and new Bulat missile, Berezhok combat compartment. We want to show the Russian complex approach to the creation and modernization of all types of arms according to modern combat experience. It helped us to sign contracts worth over $4.5 billion with 15 friendly countries in 2025," he said. All the exhibits at IDEX show to be held in Abu Dhabi on February 17-21 have been tested in combat and were upgraded according to the experience of high-intensity hostilities, the arms trader said. The company will hold talks with Middle East and other delegations at the show to present the latest Russian weapons and discuss various cooperation variants.

title: Russian, Israeli senior diplomats talk about Iranian nuclear program
text: MOSCOW, February 12. /TASS/. Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Sergey Ryabkov and Israeli Deputy Foreign Ministry Director General Meirav Eilon Shahar have discussed the prevention of the spread of weapons of mass destruction, including the resolution of the Iranian nuclear issue, the Russian Ministry of Foreign Affairs stated in a communique published on its website following their discussions in Moscow. "A substantive meeting has been held on various matters related to the prevention of weapons of mass destruction and arms control, which are of shared concern, including through the lens of current geopolitical shifts and events in the Middle East," the ministry pointed out. They emphasized that special focus during the consultations was placed on the potential of multilateral initiatives for resolving the Iranian nuclear program. At the same time, the Russian side stressed the "absence of alternatives for achieving a durable peace in the region based on the indivisibility of security, which should not be attained by countries in exchange for or at the expense of the legal rights of other nations."

title: Russian troops weather drone attacks as they wait hours for ZNPP staff rotation — MFA
text: MOSCOW, February 12. /TASS/. The Ukrainian armed forces blocked International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) experts from getting to the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant (ZNPP) for a regular staff rotation, forcing Russian servicemen to wait for them for several hours while under attack by drones, Russia’s Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova said. According to the diplomat, the rotation began as normal on early February 12, but then the movement of the organization’s members was abruptly blocked by the Ukrainian armed forces. "Russian servicemen had waited for the IAEA secretariat experts to arrive at the agreed place, all while being attacked by Ukrainian drones and under the threat of shelling, but the agency’s specialists never came. Since the situation in the meeting place was deteriorating, with risks too high for both Russian servicemen and the agency experts, once they came, a decision was made to move the Russian group tasked with ensuring the rotation out of the area. On their way back, the convoy, which included Russian troops and the IAEA specialists who were supposed to leave the ZNPP, was subjected to drone strikes and a mortar attack," she noted. "At the same time, the Kiev regime has once again attempted to attack the ZNPP with UAVs. The distance between the crash site of one of the Ukrainian drones and the first power unit of the station was no more than 300 meters," the spokeswoman stressed.

title: Kalashnikov to display KUB-10E strike drone at IDEX show
text: MOSCOW, February 12. /TASS/. The Kalashnikov Company said it would display KUB-10E strike drone at IDEX arms show to be held in Abu Dhabi on February 17-21. "The Kalashnikov Concern will for the first time showcase the latest KUB-10E complex with guided munitions at IDEX arms show," it said. The drone destroys unarmored and armored vehicles, command posts, air and missile defense weapons, radio-technical reconnaissance and electronic warfare stations, as well as rear objects. It can be operated day and night at wind speed of 15 m/sec. Guidance is provided by preset coordinates and the global satellite navigation system. The drone is launched by a catapult and develops a cruising speed of 100 km/h. It flies at altitudes of 100 to 2,500 meters.

title: Ukraine strikes parking lot 300 meters from nuclear power reactor
text: MELITOPOL, February 12. /TASS/. Ukraine’s military has targeted a parking lot in the city of Energodar, 300 meters away from the reactor of the Zaporizhia nuclear power station with UAVs, said Zaporozhye region head Yevgeny Balitsky. "Kiev terrorists attacked a parking lot in the city of Energodar and the area close to the nuclear power plant with UAVs. The distance to the first reactor was no more than 300 meters," he wrote in his telegram channel. The governor urged Energodar residents to remain calm and not go out until the situation stabilizes. "Air defense is active," Balitsky added. The Zaporozhye nuclear power station, located in Energodar, is Europe’s largest nuclear power plant. It runs six power units with a total capacity of 6 GW. In February 2022, Russian forces seized control over it. Since then, the Ukrainian army has periodically shelled both Energodar’s residential areas and the territory of the station with drones, heavy artillery, and multiple rocket launchers. All six units are in a state of a cold shutdown and do not generate electricity. Since September 2022, an IAEA mission has been present at the station, with its composition changing through regular rotations.

title: Russia to display Pantsir-SMD-E ADS at IDEX show
text: MOSCOW, February 12. /TASS/. High Precision Complexes Holding said it would display the latest Pantsir-SMD-E air defense missile gun and other latest designs at IDEX arms show in Abu Dhabi. "High Precision Complexes will for the first time display Kornet anti-tank missile launcher with a remote control. Mounted full-size Kornet-E (also known as Berezhok) weapon with fire controls and guided missiles will debut at IDEX 2025. A broad line of Pantsir antiaircraft complexes received another product. A full-size sample of the latest Pantsir-SMD-E anti-aircraft missile gun will feature along with Pantsir-S1M and Pantsir-ME," it said. The display will also feature 9M134 Bulat light guided missile for anti-tank launchers, as well as Iskander-E launcher, Product 305E, Verba manpad, Dzhigit support launching unit, BMP-3 infantry fighting vehicle and other exhibits. Pantsir-SM received high-speed guided missiles with a double range - 40 km instead of previous 20. There are also mini missiles to destroy quadcopters, mortar mines and other small targets, for which an ordinary missile is too expensive. Four mini missiles are installed in a container. Thus, the round of munitions of Pantsir-SM can reach 48 and even 96 missiles depending on the launcher option. The radar doubled the range from 36 to 75 km. There is an option without the radar and guns, but with 24 missiles instead of 12. The launcher is guided by a radar-equipped vehicle or from the command post. Pantsir-SM can engage in netcentric war. A six-vehicle battery can be united into a local network. One command launcher will distribute targets to the others. They can also switch off the radars and fire by the commands of S-400 ADS, which they protect. The upgraded Pantsir kept the eight-wheel all-terrain undercarriage of the Tornado automobile and a pair of antiaircraft 2A38M machineguns. The launcher deploys in five minutes and destroys targets flying at an altitude of up to 15 km at a speed of 2,000 meters per second. The reaction time is five seconds. The vehicle develops a road speed of 100 km/h and has a fuel distance of 1,000 kilometers.

title: Russia presents Pantsir-S1M ADS at Aero India show
text: BANGALORE /India/, February 12. /TASS/. Rosoboronexport presented the latest Russian Pantsir-S1M mobile aid defense missile gun at Aero India show, TASS correspondent reported. It has improved technical and tactical characteristics, including longer distance and higher altitude. Previous Pantsir-S1 option is a self-propelled ground air defense missile launcher for close defense of military and civilian facilities, including long-range air defense weapons, from all existing and prospective air attack means. It is carried on a mobile undercarriage and is armed with two 30mm guns with a rate of fire of 40 shots per second and 12 missiles. It destroys air targets flying at a speed of a thousand meters per second at a distance of 200 meters to 20 km.

title: Russian forces containing Ukrainian troops near Volchansk in Kharkov Region
text: MOSCOW, February 11. /TASS/. Russian forces are containing Ukrainian troops near Volchansk in the Kharkov Region not to let them stage a counterattack, head of the regional military-civilian administration Vitaly Ganchev told TASS. "The Battlegroup North is taking steps to block Ukrainian troops who are attacking our positions in the Volchansk area from various sides, including from the Kupyansk area. Tactical actions are being taken to contain a serious counteroffensive by Ukrainian armed units," he said. According to Ganchev, "no serious changes" are observed in the Volchansk area, with Russian forces continuing to hold their positions and not letting Ukrainian troops "to advance deeper into our defenses.".

title: Russian company supplied 18 engines to India’s HAL
text: MOSCOW, February 11. /TASS/. The United Engine Corporation (UEC) delivered eighteen AL-55E engines to India’s HAL for certification tests as part of new HJT-36 trainer aircraft, UEC said. "The successful completion of certification tests will make it possible to progress to organizing licensed production of the AL-55E at HAL corporation plants," the company informed. UEC plans to broaden its cooperation with India, it added. The AL-55E engine was developed specifically for the Indian single-engine trainer aircraft HJT-36, with its upgraded version known as the Yashas. The modern double-channel digital engine control system provides for flight safety.

title: Russia’s Su-57E fighter jet steals the show at Aero India
text: BANGALORE /India/, February 11. /TASS/. Spectators leaned in when the export version of Russia’s advanced Su-57E fifth-generation fighter jet was presented to the foreign media during the Aero India exhibition show, a TASS correspondent reported. The event was organized by state-run Russian arms exporter Rosoboronexport. At least a hundred journalists from Russian and foreign media outlets were present at the outdoor exhibition site featuring the Russian plane. Earlier in the day, Russian test pilot Sergey Bogdan performed a demonstration flight as part of the show. The Su-57 jet is the world's only fifth-generation fighter that has proven its effectiveness in real combat conditions against Western air defense systems. The aircraft is capable of carrying a wide range of precision-guided weapons and features low visibility for enemy detection systems. The Su-57 multirole fifth-generation fighter jet was designed to destroy all air, ground, and water surface targets, monitor the airspace at major distances from deployment bases, make adversary headquarters, command posts and control systems inoperative. The jet can engage a broad range of guided and unguided weapons, including air-to-air short, medium and long-range missiles, smart 250, 500 and 1500 kg bombs (the latter from outside suspension). Maximum combat payload is 10 tons. The aircraft stealth characteristics are boosted by the construction shape, as well as reflecting and absorbing coating materials and electronic warfare means. Some weapons are hidden inside the aircraft to decrease signature. The measures weaken the reflected signal and deviate it from the source. Adversary radar does not receive the information on the location and speed of the aircraft. Low signature in the optical band is ensured by digital (pixel) camouflage painting of the aircraft. Aero India has been held at the Yelahanka Air Force base since 1996. The 15th edition of the air show is being held on February 10-14, 2025.

title: All Russian planes are reliable, no problems with them — Indian pilot
text: BANGALORE /India/, February 11. /TASS/. All Russian planes are reliable, and they fulfill their purposes, so there are no complaints regarding them, Indian chief test pilot Ajay Menon, who flies Su-30MKI fighter jets, has told TASS on the sidelines of the Aero India defense show. "I have started learning fighter flying in the Russian MiG-21s. So, I am a big fan of Russian aircraft. In fact, all [Russian] planes are good," the pilot said. "It is only when it performs what it is supposed to do that you really like the aircraft. So, no complaints," he added. The Sukhoi Su-30MKI is a two-seater, twinjet multirole air superiority fighter developed by Russian aircraft maker Sukhoi and built under license by India's Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) for the Indian Air Force (IAF). It forms the backbone of India’s tactical aviation. The deputy head of the Russian Federal Service for Military-Technical Cooperation, Mikhail Babich, earlier told TASS on the sidelines of the Aero India show that the production localization of Su-30MKI fighter jets produced in India by a Russian license has reached 60%.
mil_orgs: Russian Federal Service for Military-Technical Cooperation

title: Top Russian security official arrives in Kazakhstan to meet with country’s leadership
text: ASTANA, February 11. /TASS/. Russian Security Council Secretary Sergey Shoigu has arrived in Astana to discuss Russia-Kazakhstan bilateral relations, security issues, and the situation in Syria, the organization’s press office reported on Tuesday. This is Shoigu’s first foreign trip in 2025, the press office said. During the working visit, the Russian top security official will take part in bilateral consultations of Russia and Kazakhstan’s security councils, as well as hold a meeting with his Kazakh counterpart Gizat Nurdauletov. The parties are to discuss bilateral relations in various spheres, current challenges and threats to regional and global security, the situation in Central Asia, and developments in both Syria and Afghanistan," the Russian Security Council noted. Shoigu is expected to be received by Kazakh President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev. Shoigu last visited Kazakhstan in late November 2024.

title: No other aircraft can surpass Su-57 — Indian pilot
text: BANGALORE /India/, February 11. /TASS/. The capabilities of Russia's newest Su-57 fifth-generation multifunctional fighter jet contradict the laws of physics, no other aircraft can surpass it, India’s senior test pilot Ajay Menon told TASS after a demonstration flight at the Aero India Su-57 air show. "We know what the Russian fly-by-wire can do. It really defies the laws of physics. So that means I don't think anybody can beat the demonstration that the Su-57 does," said Menon, who pilots Su-30MKI fighters manufactured in India under Russian license. "'About the maneuvers which the pilot fulfilled. Oh, it's very impressive. Definitely very impressive. Especially the high alpha maneuvering of the aircraft," the Indian pilot said. Russia's newest multifunctional fifth-generation Su-57E fighter made its first demonstration flight at the Aero India-2025 international aerospace exhibition. The fighter was piloted by honored test pilot, Hero of Russia Sergey Bogdan, who demonstrated aerobatics skills. The airplane was in the air for 15 minutes. Visitors to the air show watched the flight with admiration.

title: Warfare engines undergo successful trials in Russia, ready for deployment to special op
text: ST. PETERSBURG, February 11. /TASS/. The Kingisepp Machine Building Plant (KMZ) military and industrial holding has successfully tested a large batch of warfare engines intended for use in the special military operation, a representative of the holding’s press service told TASS. "The Kingisepp Machine Building Plant military and industrial holding has tested the UTD-20, B-84, B-46, and D-12 warfare diesel engines. A large batch of engines has been tested successfully. They are ready to go. The engines reached full speed rpm during trials," the press service said. These engines are used in such special equipment as infantry fighting vehicles, airborne assault vehicles, the Osa anti-aircraft weapon system, 2S7M Malka motorized artillery system, Msta-S artillery system, T-72B tank, as a traction engine for the S-300 anti-aircraft missile system, and in the MAZ-543, among others. The Kingisepp Machine Building Plant provides a range of services to ensure guaranteed and uninterrupted operation of diesel systems designed for civilian and military use. The enterprise is involved in repair and maintenance works on diesel engines. It is the head executor of a number of state defense contracts on basic repair of diesel engines for engineering equipment.

title: Russia’s Su-35 fighter provides air cover for aircraft in Kursk Region — ministry
text: MOSCOW, February 11. /TASS/. Russia’s Su-35 highly maneuverable air superiority fighter jet escorted other aircraft delivering strikes on Ukrainian military sites in bordering areas of Russia’s Kursk Region, the Russian defense ministry said. "The goal of the mission was to ensure air cover for bomber and ground attack aircraft, as well as Army Aviation helicopters that were delivering air strikes on Ukrainian military sites and targets," the ministry said in a statement. While on the mission, one of the fighter jets detected signals emitted by the enemy’s air defense radar, the ministry added.

title: Ukrainian special services plan to attack foreign ship in Baltic Sea, blame Russia — SVR
text: MOSCOW, February 11. /TASS/. Ukrainian special services are planning to blow up a foreign ship in the Baltic Sea using Russian-made naval mines and then blame Russia for it, with the goal of getting it banned from entering the Baltic Sea, Russia’s Foreign Intelligence Service (SVR) said. "The plan is to use Russian-made naval mines to blow up a foreign ship in the Baltic Sea," it said. "Moscow will subsequently be blamed for this. Ukrainian special services assume that such an operation will prompt NATO’s leadership to ban Russia from entering the Baltic Sea under the pretext of ensuring safe navigation." The SVR also said that it has received intelligence that Ukrainian special services, backed by their Western sponsors, are plotting a series of high-profile anti-Russian provocations. "Kiev’s goal is to drag NATO into a direct armed conflict with Russia," it underscored.

title: Counterterrorism body reveals number of attacks thwarted in Russia so far in 2025
text: MOSCOW, February 11. /TASS/. Since the start of 2025, Russia’s special services and law enforcement agencies have thwarted 23 terrorist attacks and foiled the terrorist activity of two gang leaders, 97 militants and more than 100 of their accomplices in Russia, the National Antiterrorism Committee (NAC) said following a meeting presided over by its Chairman Alexander Bortnikov, who is also director of the Federal Security Service (FSB). "Since early 2025, 23 terrorist attacks have been thwarted, most of which were plotted on instructions from Ukrainian curators and recruiters for international terrorist organizations. Eleven caches with weapons, munitions and improvised explosive devices have been detected and liquidated, with the terrorist activity of 224 criminals, including two gang leaders, 97 militants, and over 100 accomplices, being foiled," the NAC specified. According to Bortnikov, as the Kiev regime intensifies its terrorist activity, federal government bodies take additional measures to prevent terrorist threats. Also, conditions have been created to protect critical infrastructure, primarily from drone attacks, he added. However, the current pace and scale of threats necessitate efforts to further improve federal government work toward formulating and putting into practice state policy in the field of counteracting terrorism, the FSB chief concluded.

title: Russia expands Su-57 production capacity
text: BANGALORE /India/, February 11. /TASS/. The United Aircraft Corporation (UAC) will continue increasing the capacity for batch production of fifth-generation Su-57 fighter jets, CEO Vadim Badekha told TASS at Aero India show. "The UAC will continue expanding the capacity for batch production of Su-57 this year. Last year the deliveries considerably increased. The fighter jet has immensely evolved and continues to improve. Its arms and systems are increased. The expansion of production capacities will increase the deliveries of the jet to the troops and offer Su-57 export option to foreign customers. Together with Rosoboronexport we shall pay specific attention to the guideline," he said. The aircraft will get new features as batch production expands. "Su-57 platform has been created for a minimum of 40-50 years. It has an open architecture and can use a broad range of technologies with minimal changes of the basic solutions. Su-57 integration with unmanned aerial vehicles will be the central element of future combat systems. In the coming years, new features will be introduced into serial Su-57 in the framework of modernization program," he said. Su-57 can rapidly react to any challenges due to a quick integration of technologies. Badekha said the onboard equipment, arms, the power plant, the information control system, as well as artificial intelligence will be developed. India welcomed Su-57 at the show. "I can say for sure that India has been waiting for our aircraft. The interest is high. There is a warm welcome," Badekha said. Su-57 multirole fifth-generation fighter jet was designed to destroy all air, ground, and water surface targets, monitor the airspace at major distances from deployment bases, make adversary headquarters, command posts and control systems inoperative. The jet can engage a broad range of guided and unguided weapons, including air-to-air short, medium and long-range missiles, smart 250, 500 and 1500 kg bombs (the latter from outside suspension). Maximum combat payload is 10 tons. The aircraft stealth characteristics are boosted by the construction shape, as well as reflecting and absorbing coating materials and electronic warfare means. Some weapons are hidden inside the aircraft to decrease signature. The measures weaken the reflected signal and deviate it from the source. Adversary radar does not receive the information on the location and speed of the aircraft. Low signature in the optical band is ensured by digital (pixel) camouflage painting of the aircraft.

title: Su-57 surpasses US, Chinese competitors in key characteristics
text: BANGALORE /India/. February 11. /TASS/.
