Earlier, a Russian naval group consisting of the corvettes Gromky, Rezky and Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhapov made a business call at the Chinese port of Qingdao. Built at the Admiralty Shipyard in St. Petersburg in Russia’s northwest, the diesel-electric submarine Ufa is making an inter-fleet transit from the Baltic Sea to the Pacific Fleet through the southern maritime route. The Ufa was accepted for service with the Russian Navy on November 16, 2022. The sub is the fourth in a series of six Project 636.3 submarines being built for Russia’s Pacific Fleet. The submarines of this Project optimally combine acoustic stealth, target detection range and powerful torpedo and missile armament. They carry the Kalibr high-precision missile system as their basic strike weapon.

title: Seven Ukrainian drones taken down and jammed in Sevastopol — governor
text: SEVASTOPOL, December 11. /TASS/. The Russian military has repelled an attack of Ukrainian unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) on Sevastopol, taking down and jamming seven drones, Governor Mikhail Razvozhayev said. "Our military repelled the attack of the Ukrainian armed forces. By now, air defenses and Black Sea fleet units have taken down five drones, while two more have been jammed by electronic warfare systems," he wrote on Telegram. According to the city’s rescue service, no damage was reported in the city, and its airspace is being monitored for threats. All city services have been put on high alert. An air raid warning was issued in Sevastopol on Wednesday evening. Public transport services and maritime traffic were briefly halted.

title: Two aerial targets shot down in Sevastopol — governor
text: SEVASTOPOL, December 11. /TASS/. Russian air defenses shot down two Ukrainian aerial targets in Sevastopol, Governor Mikhail Razvozhayev said. "In Sevastopol, the military are repelling a Ukrainian attack. Air defenses are at work. Two more aerial targets were shot down in the area of Balaklava and Inkerman. According to the Sevastopol rescue service, as of this hour no civilian sites in the city have been damaged," he wrote on Telegram. The governor earlier said two fixed-wing drones were destroyed over the Black Sea. All city services have been put on combat alert, according to the official.

title: Ukrainian troops attack DPR’s Yenakiyevo with long-range weapons — DPR head
text: DONETSK, December 10. /TASS/. Ukrainian troops attacked the city of Yenakiyevo in the Donetsk People’s Republic (DPR) with high-precision long-range weapons, residents are being evacuated from the impact zone, DPR’s head, Denis Pushilin, said. "The enemy attacked Yenakiyevo’s suburbs with long-range high-precision weapons. The city’s authorities announced evacuation of civilians from the zone adjacent to the attacked site. No casualties were reported," he wrote on his Telegram channel. According to Pushilin, Ukrainian forces staged six shelling attacks on the DPR, using long-range weapons, 155mm artillery systems and a combat drone. Earlier, Gorlovka’s mayor, Ivan Prikhodko, said that a civilian was wounded after Ukraine’s drone attack.

title: Drone attacks IAEA vehicle en route to ZNPP
text: BERLIN, December 10. /TASS/. A vehicle of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) was attacked by a drone while on its way to the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant (ZNPP), the agency said in a statement on the X social network. According to the statement "a drone hit and severely damaged an official vehicle of the IAEA on the road to" the Zaporozhye nuclear power plant. None of the agency’s staffers were harmed. IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi strongly condemned the incident, describing it as "unacceptable." "Attacking those who care for the nuclear safety and security of these plants is also absolutely unacceptable," he said. IAEA experts have been present at the ZNPP since September 1, 2022, after IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi’s first visit to the facility, the biggest nuclear plant in Europe.

title: Russian military ensures safe rotation of IAEA experts at ZNPP — Defense Ministry
text: MOSCOW, December 10. /TASS/. The Russian military has ensured the safe regular rotation of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) experts at the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant (ZNPP), the Russian Defense Ministry said. "The Russian armed forces jointly with units of the National Guard Service ensured security during the 25th regular rotation of IAEA experts at the ZNPP. A ceasefire was declared from seven in the morning to seven in the evening along the entire route of the IAEA experts’ movement, from the designated starting point at the destroyed bridge near the settlement of Kamenskoye in the Zaporozhye Region. The ceasefire was strictly observed by the Russian military," it said. Before the IAEA experts began their movement, bomb specialists surveyed the area to make sure it was free from mines or the like. After the sweep, the Russian military escorted the three IAEA experts right to the ZNPP. IAEA experts have been present at the ZNPP since September 1, 2022, after IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi’s first visit to the facility, the biggest nuclear plant in Europe.

title: Russian-Indian military cooperation has vast growth potential — Russian defense minister
text: MOSCOW, December 10. /TASS/. Defense cooperation between Russia and India has vast potential, Russian Defense Minister Andrey Belousov said. "India is one of Russia’s key partners, with the two countries maintaining privileged strategic relations. The unprecedented level of trust between our countries can be seen in the extent and closeness of military and military-technical cooperation, which has a vast potential for further growth," he said at the 21st meeting of the Russian-Indian intergovernmental commission on military and military-technical cooperation. Welcoming the Indian delegation, Belousov congratulated the Indian Navy on receiving the Tushil frigate. "The flag-raising ceremony was held yesterday in Kaliningrad with your participation," he said addressing Indian Defense Minister Rajnath Singh. According to Belousov, who co-chairs the commission, today’s meeting is expected to sum up the results of joint work in the outgoing year and outline priority tasks for the future.

title: Russian defense chief commends growing defense ties with India
text: MOSCOW, December 10. /TASS/. Moscow and New Delhi are effectively developing defense cooperation, Russian Defense Minister Andrey Belousov said. "We are pleased with the robust level of defense cooperation between Russia and India on multilateral platforms, primarily within the Shanghai Cooperation Organization and the ASEAN Defense Ministers' Meeting," he stated during talks with Indian Defense Minister Rajnath Singh. The Russian defense chief stressed that the relationship based on trust between the two countries’ leaders was crucial for strengthening bilateral ties between Russia and India. Belousov thanked his counterpart for visiting Moscow to hold the 21st meeting of the Russian-Indian Intergovernmental Commission on Defense Cooperation. He noted that Moscow and New Delhi "share a strong friendship based on mutual respect, which has stood the test of time." According to Belousov, the outcome of this year’s meetings between Russian President Vladimir Putin and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, which took place in Moscow in July and on the sidelines of the BRICS group’s summit in the Russian city of Kazan in October, "helped strengthen our special and privileged strategic partnership, including in the defense sector." "We are committed to executing all the agreements that were reached at the highest level. I am confident that our meeting will contribute to further boosting Russia-India relations in the field of defense and security," Belousov stressed, adding that the meeting was expected "to thoroughly discuss critical issues related to bilateral defense cooperation."
mil_orgs: Russian-Indian Intergovernmental Commission on Defense Cooperation

title: Russian bomb engineers remove mines in DPR’s Selidovo
text: MOSCOW, December 10. /TASS/. Bomb squads from the battlegroup Center are removing mines the liberated town of Selidovo in the Donetsk People's Republic, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "Servicemen from the bomb clearing unit of the battlegroup Center are on a mine clearing mission in the settlement of Selidovo in the Donetsk People's Republic, which was liberated during the special military operation," it said. The squads remove mines from roads, single-family and multi-storey buildings. The work is performed in several shifts in groups of 3-4 specialists. According to the ministry, Ukrainian military formations that were driven out of Selidovo continue to remotely mine roads and access pathways by dropping anti-tank and anti-personnel mines on the city from industrial-type drones at night. It has been confirmed that Ukrainian forces rigged with mines water wells and approaches to water wells before retreating, so that to inflict the greatest possible damage on the civilian population of the town. According to the ministry, when an explosive object is found, sappers assess the possibility of removing it for neutralization. If a removal is impossible, they destroy it at the scene by the method of overhead charge. If the object does not represent immediate danger, demolitions specialists take it to a prepared location for subsequent destruction.

title: India’s share in Russia’s weapons exports up by 16% in six months — official
text: MOSCOW, December 9. /TASS/. Russia and India continue to ramp up defense ties, with India’s share in Russia’s exports of weapons and combat vehicles going up by 15% in the past six months, a high-ranking Russian presidential administration official said. "India’s share in the export of Russian weapons and hardware has increased by 15% in the past six months alone," Viktor Yevtukhov, head of the presidential directorate for state policy in the defense sector, said on the occasion of transferring a project 11356 frigate to the Indian Navy. According to Yevtukhov, the construction of project 11356 multi-role frigates is a cornerstone of Russian-Indian military-technical cooperation. "These deals have demonstrated the Russian defense sector’s research and industrial potential and facilitated the development of the Russian school of surface military ship-building," he stressed. He recalled that six frigates of this project were supplied to the Indian side from 2007 to 2013. The seventh such frigate was transferred today at the Kaliningrad-based Yantar plant of the United Shipbuilding Corporation and the eighth one will be built at this plant and supplied to India next year. The ninth and tenth frigates will be built in India, with Russia’s technical support. "This complies with the key trends of Russia-Indian military-technical cooperation, which is developing on the basis of long-term contracts and with a focus on localizing the production of Russian weapons and vehicles at Indian enterprises. Overall, Russia continues to be India’s leading partner in the defense sector. And not only because we have a vast potential inherited from the former Soviet Union but also thanks to joint work today. Our countries have more than 200 joint defense projects," Yevtukhov added.
weapons: Kaliningrad-based Yantar plant of the United Shipbuilding Corporation

title: Tamal frigate to be delivered to Indian Navy in spring 2025 — shipyard
text: KALININGRAD, December 9. /TASS/. The Project 11356 Tamal frigate under construction of the Indian Navy at the Yantar shipyard is planned to be delivered in spring 2025, chief executive of the shipyard Ilya Samarin said. "Construction of warships is the core business of the Yantar shipyard. Irrespective of whether it is a foreign or a domestic customer, we do our job similarly in good faith and responsibly. It is not accidental that this is already the fourth frigate built at our shipyard. We plan to deliver the fifth one next spring," Samarin said at the ceremony of delivering the Project 11356 Tushil frigate to the Indian Navy. The specific feature of ship construction is installation of foreign customer’s equipment onboard, its integration with Russian systems and the joint effort of Russian and foreign counterparties during trials and at closing stages of construction, the chief executive said. "Further to our construction of frigates, we also educate the Indian crew and specialists, transfer the experience and knowledge to them for the forthcoming service on this frigate and for further construction of ships in India," he added.

title: Series 11356 frigate custom-developed for Indian Navy
text: KALININGRAD, December 9. /TASS/. The Project 11356 frigate series was specifically designed and built for the Indian Navy, CEO of the Russian United Shipbuilding Corporation (USC) Andrey Puchkov said. "Our military products remain in demand primarily owing to the corporation’s expertise in ship design and construction. It is worth noting that our Indian partners, believing in USC’s professionalism, entrusted us with constructing the Series 11356 frigates. This series is custom-built for the Indian Navy," Puchkov said. Project 11356 "integrates the best of Russian engineering, delivering a versatile and reliable warship, which has advanced combat capabilities against above-water, surface, underwater and aerial targets, while the displacement is relatively small," he added. The USC’s Yantar Shipyard Delivered the Project 11356 Tushil missile frigate to the Indian Navy earlier today.

title: Yantar shipyard builds state-of-the-art ships for Russia, India
text: MOSCOW, December 9. /TASS/. The Yantar Shipyard of the United Shipbuilding Corporation (USC) is giving the Russian and Indian Navies a lot of extra firepower with its state-of-the-art ships, the USC press service said on the occasion of the Project 11356 Tuship frigate transfer to the Indian Navy. The Yantar Shipyard has the wherewithal to build the most advanced warships. Notably, BrahMos strike missile systems, the product of successful integration of Russia and India, were installed on the first three Project 11356 frigates," the corporation said. Project 11356 ships are actively employed by the Indian Navy, USC said. These versatile ships boast advanced combat capabilities to fight above-water, surface, underwater and aerial targets while having a relatively small displacement. "Project 11356 exemplifies modern trends in military shipbuilding," it added.

title: Russian shipyard transfers missile frigate to Indian Navy
text: KALININGRAD, December 9. /TASS/. The Yantar Shipyard of the Russian United Shipbuilding Corporation (USC) has transferred the Project 11356 Tushil missile frigate to the Indian Navy, TASS reporters at the scene said. "On November 9, 2024, Rosoboronexport delivered the Project 11356 Tushil frigate to the Indian Navy at the Yantar Shipyard of USC in Kaliningrad. This is the fourth frigate built at the Kaliningrad shipyard of USC for the Indian Navy," the shipyard’s press service reported. Tushil will be the seventh Project 11356 ship in the Indian Navy built at Russian shipyards, the press service noted. This demonstrates the high performance shown by these ships and the high credibility placed in the Russian defense industry by the Indian Navy. The frigate has previously completed successful acceptance trials with the participation of the Indian commission.

title: Rosoboronexport, India implementing joint projects in naval sphere
text: MOSCOW, December 9. /TASS/. Rosoboronexport and India are implementing about ten joint projects in the naval sphere and are discussing several new initiatives as well, CEO of the Russian state's arms export agency Alexander Mikheev said. The company transferred the Project 11356 Tushil frigate to India, which was built at the Yantar Shipyard. The warship transfer ceremony was held today in Kaliningrad and attended by Indian Defense Minister Rajnath Singh. "Raising the Indian flag on board the Tushil ship is a big milestone in Russian-Indian military-technical cooperation, fully aligning with the Make in India initiative. Twenty ship systems made in India are installed on board the Russian-built frigate; it is fitted with the BrahMos ultrasonic missile system. Besides, today Rosoboronexport and India are implementing about ten joint projects and discussing several new ones where the navy is concerned," Mikheev said, cited by the company’s press service.

title: Russia delivering on overseas arms contracts despite restrictions, industry CEO says
text: KALININGRAD, December 9. /TASS/. To eliminate competition, Moscow’s opponents are trying to stop Russian weapons from reaching the international market, but this strategy is not working, Andrey Puchkov, CEO of Russia’s United Shipbuilding Corporation, told reporters. "As for our foreign economic activities, it’s evident that Russia’s opponents are doing all they can to prevent much sought-after Russia-made weapons and military equipment from reaching the international market, fearing any competition. However, as you can see, we still find ways to make sure that our customers get what they need," Puchkov said at a ceremony to hand the Project 11356 frigate Tushil over to the Indian Navy, which took place in Russia’s westernmost exclave of Kaliningrad. On Monday, Kaliningrad’s Yantar shipbuilding plant hosted a ceremony where Russian state arms exporter Rosoboronexport handed the Tushil frigate over to the Indian Navy. This is the fourth frigate built for India at the Kaliningrad shipbuilding facility. The ship was designed by the United Shipbuilding Corporation’s Severnoye design bureau.
mil_orgs: United Shipbuilding Corporation’s Severnoye design bureau

title: Russian submarine docks in Qingdao, China
text: MOSCOW, December 9. /TASS/. The Project 636.3 Ufa diesel-electric submarine of Russia’s Pacific Fleet and its rescue tug have called at the port in Qingdao in the People's Republic of China, the fleet’s press service reported. "The Pacific Fleet diesel-electric submarine Ufa, escorted by the Alatau rescue tug, has conducted a business call at the Qingdao port of the People's Republic of China. Over several days in the Chinese port, the Russian sailors will hold a number of meetings with representatives of China’s People's Liberation Army [PLA] Navy, visit the Chinese PLA Naval Submarine Academy and local tourist attractions," the statement specified. Earlier, the group of vessels consisting of the corvettes Gromky, Rezky and Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhapov and the tanker Pechenga of Russia’s Pacific Fleet completed its business call at the port of Qingdao. The Russian naval sailors participated in sports and cultural events. The warships’ commanders held meetings with the Qingdao administration and representatives of China’s Northern Theater Command Navy.

title: Russian Pacific Fleet ships wrap up visit to China’s Qingdao port
text: MOSCOW, December 9. /TASS/. A naval group of the Russian Pacific Fleet has wrapped up its visit to the Chinese port of Qingdao, the Fleet’s press office reported on Monday. "The Russian naval group consisting of the corvettes Gromky, Rezky and Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhapov and the tanker Pechenga has completed its business call at the port of Qingdao of the People’s Republic of China," the press office said in a statement. During several days of their stay at the Chinese port, the Russian naval sailors participated in sports and cultural events. The warships’ commanders held meetings with the Qingdao administration and representatives of China’s Northern Theater Command Navy, it specified. After leaving the Chinese port of Qingdao, the Russian naval ships set course for the Pacific Fleet’s main naval base in Vladivostok, it said.

title: Thirteen Ukrainian drones downed over three Russian regions last night
text: MOSCOW, December 9. /TASS/. Overnight, air defense forces destroyed and intercepted 13 Ukrainian unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) over three Russian regions, Russia’s Defense Ministry said. "Last night, an attempt by the Kiev regime to conduct a terrorist attack against facilities on Russian soil using fixed-wing UAVs was thwarted. Air defense forces on duty intercepted and downed 13 Ukrainian unmanned aerial vehicles, including eight UAVs over the Belgorod Region, three over the Rostov Region and two over the Astrakhan Region," the ministry specified. Earlier, acting governor of the Rostov Region, Yury Slyusar, wrote on his Telegram channel that three Ukrainian UAVs were downed in the skies over his region overnight.

title: Russian shipyard on Baltic Sea to hand over guided-missile frigate to Indian Navy
text: KALININGRAD, December 9. /TASS/. The Kaliningrad-based Yantar shipyard, a subsidiary of Russia’s United Shipbuilding Corporation, will officially hand over the INS Tushil, a guided-missile frigate, to the Indian Navy, a shipyard official told TASS. "A handover ceremony for the first in a new series of four frigates being built for the Indian Navy is scheduled to take place on December 9," the official said. The construction of Project 11356 frigates has been underway since 1999. Originally there were plans for six Project 11356 frigates for Russia’s Black Sea Fleet, but those plans were changed after Ukraine refused to supply turbines for the project. As a result, the Black Sea Fleet has commissioned three such frigates. Each Project 11356 frigate built for the Russian Navy carries eight Kalibr-NK and/or Oniks cruise missiles, while frigates built for India carry Russian-Indian made BrahMos anti-ship cruise missiles. Earlier, the Yantar shipyard and the Baltic shipyard built six 11356 project frigates for the Indian Navy.

title: Russian forces improve frontline positions near Orekhov in Zaporozhye region — official
text: MELITOPOL, December 9. /TASS/. Russian troops have advanced near the settlements of Malaya Tokmachka and Belogorye in the Orekhov area in the Zaporozhye Region, Chairman of the Civic Chamber Commission on Sovereignty, Patriotic Projects, and Support of Veterans Vladimir Rogov told TASS. "[Russian forces] have improved their frontline positions near Malaya Tokmachka and Belogorye in the Orekhov direction. Russian troops have launched air and artillery strikes on enemy positions, and our forces are conducting offensive actions," Rogov said.
mil_orgs: Civic Chamber Commission on Sovereignty, Patriotic Projects

title: Ukraine loses over 400 troops, two infantry fighting vehicles in Kursk Region
text: MOSCOW, December 8. /TASS/. The Ukrainian armed forces have lost more than 400 troops and two infantry fighting vehicles in Russia’s borderline Kursk Region over the past 24 hours, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "Over the past 24 hours, Ukrainian losses amounted to more than 400 troops, with two infantry fighting vehicles eliminated, including a US-made Bradley armored fighting vehicle, two combat vehicles, two motor vehicles and a mortar," the ministry said. According to the Russian Defense Ministry, since the onset of combat in the Kursk area, the adversary has lost over 39,260 troops, 232 tanks, 171 infantry fighting vehicles, 123 armored personnel carriers, 1,233 armored combat vehicles, 1,098 motor vehicles, 308 artillery guns, 40 multiple launch rocket systems, including 11 US-made HIMARS and six MLRS, and 13 air defense launchers.

title: Ukrainian troops attack Russia’s borderline Belgorod Region with about 30 drones
text: BELGOROD, December 8. /TASS/. The Ukrainian armed forces have attacked Russia’s borderline Belgorod Region with about 30 unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) over the past 24 hours, regional Governor Vyacheslav Gladkov said on his Telegram channel. According to him, about 30 shells have been fired at the region’s residential areas over this period. The governor reported damage to residential buildings, private vehicles and power lines in some districts, noting that there have been no casualties.

title: Russian army has liberated Blagodatnoye settlement in Donetsk People’s Republic
text: MOSCOW, December 8. /TASS/. The units of Russia’s Battlegroup East have liberated the settlement of Blagodatnoye (Oktyabr collective farm) in the Donetsk People’s Republic, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "The units of Battlegroup East have liberated the settlement of Blagodatnoye (Oktyabr collective farm) in the Donetsk People’s Republic as a result of successive advance. Damage was inflicted on the formations of the Ukrainian army’s mechanized brigade and two territorial defense brigades near the settlements of Konstantinopol, Vremevka, Neskuchnoye and Bogatyr (DPR). Two counterattacks by the enemy’s assault groups were repelled," the report said. The Ukrainian army lost up to 190 troops, two 152mm self-propelled Akatsiya artillery systems, two MaxxPro armored combat vehicles and a US-made 155mm self-propelled Paladin artillery system in 24 hours, the ministry said, adding that "a fuel depot was eliminated." Ukraine’s armed forces lost more than 500 troops as a result of actions by Russia’s Battlegroup Center in 24 hours, the Defense Ministry reported. "Units of Battlegroup Center continued advancing deep into the enemy’s defense, inflicting damage on troops and equipment of three mechanized, infantry, jaeger, mechanized infantry brigades of the Ukrainian army, a marine brigade and an assault brigade of the national guard near the settlements of Dzerzhinsk, Shevchenko, Dimitrov, Tarasovka and Novotoretskoye of the Donetsk People’s Republic. Eight counterattacks by the Ukrainian army’s militant groups were repelled. The enemy lost more than 500 troops, a car, a US-made 155mm М-777 howitzer, a 152mm Msta-B howitzer, a 152mm D-20 weapon and two 122mm D-30 howitzers," the ministry said. Units of Ukraine’s armed forces lost up to 85 troops in 24 hours as a result of actions by Russia’s North and Dnepr battlegroups, the ministry reported. "Damage was inflicted on groups of the territorial defense brigade and the border detachment of Ukraine’s border guard by units of Battlegroup North in the Kharkov direction near the settlements of Tikhoye, Volchansk and Kazachya Lopan of the Kharkov Region. The enemy lost up to 40 troops, a car and two D-30 howitzers," the report said. Units of Battlegroup Dnepr inflicted damage on troops and equipment of two mechanized brigades of the Ukrainian army and a territorial defense brigade near the settlements of Otradokamenka and Antonovka of the Kherson Region. The Ukrainian armed forces lost up to 45 troops, nine cars, a 152mm D-20 weapon, two 122mm D-30 howitzers and three Olkha multiple launch rocket systems, the ministry added. Ukraine’s armed forces lost up to 225 troops in 24 hours as a result of actions by Russia’s Battlegroup South, the report said. "Units of Battlegroup South have improved their tactical position, having inflicted damage on formations of a tank, three mechanized, mountain assault, air assault, two airmobile infantry brigades of the Ukrainian army and a marine brigade near settlements of Ulakly, Ostrovskoye, Grigorovka, Verkhnekamskoye, Privolye, Kurakhovo, Yantarnoye and Dachnoye of the Donetsk People’s Republic. Two counterattacks by the enemy’s formations were repelled. The Ukrainian army lost up to 225 troops, an infantry fighting vehicle, two armored combat vehicles, a pickup and a 122mm D-30 howitzer," the ministry said. Units of Ukraine’s armed forces lost over 460 troops as a result of actions by Russia’s Battlegroup West over the past 24 hours, the Defense Ministry reported. "Units of Battlegroup West took more advantageous positions, inflicted damage on troops and equipment of a tank, infantry, two mechanized brigades of the Ukrainian army and a territorial defense brigade near settlements of Monachinovka, Dvurechnaya, Kondrashovka, Petropavlovka, Zagryzovo, Kupyansk of the Kharkov Region and Torskoye of the Donetsk People’s Republic. Eleven counterattacks by assault groups of the Ukrainian army’s units were repelled," the report said. The enemy lost over 460 troops, a tank, an infantry fighting vehicle, four cars, a UK-made 155mm FH-70 howitzer, a US-produced 155mm М198 howitzer, a 152mm self-propelled Akatsiya artillery system, two 122mm Gvozdika self-propelled systems, a 122mm D-30 howitzer, a 120mm Nona self-propelled artillery weapon, three US-made 105mm M119 weapons and an 85mm D-44 gun, the ministry said, adding that two weapons depots were eliminated. The Russian army infliсted damage on infrastructure of military aerodromes, energy facilities ensuring the work of Ukraine’s military and industrial complex, as well as troops and military equipment of Ukraine in 130 regions, the Russian ministry noted. Russia’s air defense forces downed 12 Czech-made Vampire multiple rocket launchers over the past 24 hours, according to the report. "Air defense forces downed 12 Czech-made Vampire multiple rocket launchers and 88 fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles," the report said.

title: Some 46 drones downed over five Russian regions overnight into Sunday
text: MOSCOW, December 8. /TASS/. Russian air defenses eliminated and intercepted 46 Ukrainian drones over five Russian regions in the early hours of Sunday, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "During the past night, the Kiev regime’s attempted terrorist attacks on facilities in Russia with the use of fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles were thwarted. On-duty air defenses intercepted and destroyed 46 Ukrainian UAVs, including 17 over the Bryansk Region, 12 over the Kursk Region, six over the Voronezh Region, seven over the Rostov Region, and four over the Astrakhan Region," the ministry said.

title: Russian forces liberate Berestki in Donetsk People’s Republic — Defense Ministry
text: MOSCOW, December 7. /TASS/. Russian servicemen liberated the settlement of Berestki in the Donetsk People's Republic (DPR), the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "Units of Russia’s Center battlegroup continued to advance deep into the enemy's defenses and liberated the settlement of Berestki in the Donetsk People's Republic," the ministry reported. Russia hit a temporary deployment site of units of a brigade of the Armed Forces of Ukraine using TOS-1A Solntsepek heavy flamethrower, the Ministry of Defense reported earlier "The Russian Ministry of Defense published the footage of the destruction of a temporary deployment point of units of the brigade of the Ukrainian Armed Forces by a platoon of the TOS-1A Solntsepyok in the area of the settlement Razliv in the Volnovakha district. As a result of the strike, up to 45 militants were destroyed," the ministry said. Units of Russia’s South battlegroup improved their tactical position, the Ukrainian Armed Forces lost up to 325 servicemen in one day during the actions of the Russian group, the Ministry reported. "The units of Russia’s South battlegroup of forces improved their tactical position, defeated the formations of two mechanized, airmobile and airborne assault brigades of the Ukrainian Armed Forces in the areas of the settlements of Ulakly, Kurakhovo, Nikolaevka, and Dachnoye of the Donetsk People's Republic. Two counter-attacks of the enemy assault units were repulsed. The losses of the Ukrainian Armed Forces amounted to 325 servicemen," the ministry said. Units of the Armed Forces of Ukraine lost about 120 servicemen in one day as a result of the actions of Russia’s Dnepr and North battlegroups, the Ministry added. "Units of Russia’s North battlegroup inflicted losses on formations of the territorial defense brigade and the border detachment of the Ukrainian border service in the areas of the settlements of Tikhoe, Volchansk, and Liptsy in the Kharkov region in the Kharkov direction. Two counter-attacks of the Ukrainian Armed Forces assault groups were repulsed. The enemy lost up to 60 servicemen, one armored personnel carrier, two cars and three D-30 howitzers," the Defense Ministry reported. Units of the Armed Forces of Ukraine lost up to 490 servicemen in one day as a result of the actions of Russia’s West battlegroup, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "The units of the Western group of forces occupied more advantageous positions, defeated the personnel and equipment of two mechanized airborne brigades of the Ukrainian Armed Forces and three territorial defense brigades in the areas of the settlements of Grigorovka, Zagryzovo, Kupyansk in the Kharkov region, Novoegorovka in the Lugansk People's Republic and Torskoye in the Donetsk People's Republic," the ministry said. The Armed Forces of Ukraine lost up to 525 servicemen in the zone of responsibility of Russia’s Center battlegroup in the past 24 hours, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "The manpower and equipment of two brigades of the Ukrainian Armed Forces - mechanized, motorized infantry - and a marine brigade were defeated in the areas of the settlements of Dzerzhinsk, Shcherbinovka, Petrovka, and Novoalekseevka of the Donetsk People's Republic. Ten counterattacks by armed formations of the Ukrainian Armed Forces were repelled. The enemy lost up to 525 servicemen, 5 combat armored vehicles, including a US-made M113 armored personnel carrier, 3 cars, 3 152-mm D-20 guns and 2 122-mm D-30 howitzers," the ministry reported. The ministry also noted that Russian troops have damaged energy facilities supporting the operation of Ukraine's military-industrial complex, as well as concentrations of manpower and military equipment of the Ukrainian Armed Forces in 142 districts. "Operational-tactical aviation, strike unmanned aerial vehicles, missile forces and artillery of the Russian Armed Forces' troop groups have damaged energy facilities supporting the operation of Ukraine's military-industrial complex, as well as concentrations of enemy manpower and military equipment in 142 districts," the ministry said. At the same time, air defense forces shot down 26 fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) in the past 24 hours, the Ministry reported. "Air defense assets shot down 26 fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles," the ministry said. According to the Defense Ministry, since the beginning of the special military operation a total of 649 aircraft, 283 helicopters, 37,380 unmanned aerial vehicles, 586 anti-aircraft missile systems, 19,698 tanks and other armored combat vehicles, 1,497 multiple launch rocket systems combat vehicles, 19,145 field artillery pieces and mortars, and 29,080 units of special military vehicles have been destroyed.
weapons: multiple launch rocket systems combat vehicles

title: Ukrainian forces lost over 38,800 servicemen in Kursk direction — Defense Ministry
text: MOSCOW, December 7. /TASS/. The Ukrainian Armed Forces lost more than 38,800 servicemen during the fighting in the Kursk region, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "In total, during the military operations in the Kursk direction, the enemy lost more than 38,865 servicemen, 232 tanks, 169 infantry fighting vehicles, 123 armored personnel carriers, 1,231 armored combat vehicles, 1,096 vehicles, 308 artillery pieces, 40 multiple launch rocket system launchers, including 11 HIMARS and 6 MLRS, 13 anti-aircraft missile system launchers, 7 transport and loading vehicles, 72 electronic warfare stations, 13 anti-battery radars, 4 air defense radars," the Defense Ministry said. At the same time, units of Russia’s North battlegroup defeated formations of brigades of the Ukrainian Armed Forces near 11 settlements in Kursk Oblast, including near Plekhov, the Ministry said. "Units of Russia’s North battlegroup defeated formations of three mechanized, heavy mechanized, tank, three airborne assault brigades, a marine brigade, a security brigade, as well as three territorial defense brigades of the Ukrainian Armed Forces in the areas of the settlements of Agronom, Viktorovka, Kurilovka, Lebedevka, Leonidovo, Martynovka, Nikolayevo-Daryino, Nikolsky, Novoivanovka, Plekhovo, and Sverdlikovo," the ministry said. The daily losses of the Ukrainian Armed Forces in the Kursk region amounted to over 380 servicemen, while three surrendered, the Ministry added. "Over 24 hours, the Ukrainian Armed Forces lost over 380 servicemen. One armored fighting vehicle, four vehicles, and three mortars were destroyed. Three servicemen of the Ukrainian Armed Forces surrendered," it said.

title: Russia ready to deploy Oreshnik in Belarus in 2025 — Putin
text: MINSK, December 6. /TASS/. Russia's Oreshnik missile system may be deployed in Belarus in the second half of 2025, President Vladimir Putin has said. "As far the possibility of deploying such, let's say, formidable weapons as the Oreshnik in Belarus is concerned, since we have signed today an agreement on security guarantees with the use of all available forces and means, I consider the deployment of such systems as the Oreshnik in Belarus possible," Putin said after a meeting of the Supreme State Council of the Union State. He added that this would become possible in the second half of next year as the serial production of these systems in Russia increased and the missile systems entered service with the Russian strategic missile forces. Putin warned of the need for addressing some technicalities before the deployment of the Oreshnik system in Belarus. "Of course, there are a number of technical issues here for the specialists to address, namely, the determination of the minimum range with due regard for the security priorities of Belarus," he said. "It takes specialists to look into this matter." The Russian president remarked that deploying the Oreshnik in Belarus would not require heavy spending. "I reckon that there are such opportunities (for the deployment of the Oreshnik in Belarus - TASS) in the light of the fact that it will also require minimal costs. Belarus has retained certain infrastructural capabilities, inherited from the Soviet era, so the placement of such systems in Belarus will involve minimal spending to have infrastructure up and running," Putin explained. "Of course, despite the fact that they (Oreshnik systems - TASS) will be part and parcel of the Russian Strategic Missile Forces, nevertheless, the selection of targets on the territory of a probable enemy will certainly be a prerogative of the military and political leadership of Belarus. We will discuss everything in the working mode," Putin promised. "For now, I can only say - the experts understand it well: the shorter the range, the greater the strike power of the warhead," he stated. "We will discuss the technicalities later." Putin earlier cited some technical aspects as he compared the Oreshnik and nuclear weapons: "Of course, such cutting-edge systems as the Oreshnik have no analogues in the world. In the case of group use, they are similar in effects to nuclear weapons, but they are not a weapon of mass destruction." He underscored two distinctive features of the Oreshnik. "First, unlike weapons of mass destruction, it is a high accuracy weapon, it does not hit large areas and achieves results not due to its power, but due to accuracy. Second, in the case of group use of one, two, or three systems its effects are akin to those of nuclear weapons, but they do not contaminate the terrain or entail any radiation consequences. There are no nuclear components in the warhead of these missiles." On November 21, Putin made a statement to declare that in response to the use of Western long-range weapons against Russia the newest non-nuclear Oreshnik medium-range ballistic missiles launched for the first time against Ukraine's defense industry facility, the Yuzhmash plant.
mil_orgs: Supreme State Council of the Union State

title: Putin says hopes to chop it up with Lukashenko at informal CIS summit in late December
text: MINSK, December 6. /TASS/. Russian President Vladimir Putin has said that he hopes to see his Belarusian counterpart, Alexander Lukashenko at December’s year-end informal meeting of CIS leaders in St. Petersburg. "Naturally, I hope to see Alexander Grigoryevich [Lukashenko] in St. Petersburg on December 25 and 26, at the traditional informal meeting of CIS leaders and the session of the Supreme Eurasian Economic Council," Putin said at a meeting of the Supreme State Council of the Union State of Russia and Belarus. Putin has been a regular guest at these informal CIS meetings since he first became president in 2000. In recent years, the meetings have been hosted by Russia’s second largest city, St. Petersburg, in late December. These meetings, as a rule, have no strict and coordinated agenda, so, the leaders informally exchange views on whatever is on their mind.
mil_orgs: Union State of Russia and Belarus

title: Shoigu arrives in Minsk to attend Supreme State Council of Union State
text: MINSK, December 6. /TASS/. Russian Security Council Secretary Sergey Shoigu has arrived in Minsk to take part in a meeting of the Supreme State Council of the Union State, a TASS correspondent reported. At the airport, he was welcomed by Belarusian Security Council State Secretary Alexander Volfovich. The Russian Security Council press office specified that Shoigu is to brief attendees of the meeting on the concept for the security of the Union State. "This is an important document, which outlines common strategic goals and objectives and defines the Union State's security priorities. It has been developed in accordance with the requirements stemming from the difficult international situation in the face of significantly expanded threats from the West," the press service noted.
mil_orgs: Supreme State Council of the Union State

title: Shipbuilders to deliver latest nuclear-powered sub to Russian Navy next summer
text: MOSCOW, December 6. /TASS/. The latest Project 885M (Yasen-M) multi-purpose nuclear-powered submarine Arkhangelsk built at the Sevmash Shipyard (part of the United Shipbuilding Corporation) will be delivered to the Russian Navy next summer, Vladimir Maltsev, head of the All-Russian Navy Support Movement, told TASS on Friday. "The Arkhangelsk is currently undergoing state trials. The submarine is planned to be delivered to the Russian Navy in the summer of 2025," he said. The nuclear-powered submarine Arkhangelsk deployed to the sea for the first time in June 2024 for shipbuilders’ sea trials. The Arkhangelsk submarine was rolled out from the slipway of the Sevmash Shipyard on November 29, 2023 for completing its construction afloat. The Sevmash Shipyard in Russia’s northwest is constructing five Project 885M submarines. The Project 885/885M nuclear-powered subs Severodvinsk, Kazan, Novosibirsk and Krasnoyarsk have been delivered to the Russian Navy. Project 885M nuclear-powered submarines carry Oniks and/or Kalibr-PL cruise missiles as their basic strike weapons.

title: Russian gunners carry out over 100 strikes on Kiev forces near Kupyansk in 24 hours
text: LUGANSK, December 6. /TASS/. Russian artillerymen have carried out over 100 artillery strikes on units of the Ukrainian armed forces deployed near Kupyansk in the Kharkov region in the past 24 hours, military expert Andrey Marochko told TASS. "According to intercepted enemy data, it became known that in the Kupyansk area, over 100 fire strikes were carried out by artillery units of the Russian armed forces in the past 24 hours," he said, citing his own sources. The expert noted that the "hottest spots" in terms of the number of strikes carried out by Russian artillerymen were the areas of the settlements of Kucherovka, Petrovpavlovka, Lozovaya and Glushkovka.

title: Versatile system for various scenarios: foreign media about close-up shots of Tsirkon
text: MOSCOW, December 5. /TASS/. A recent video released by the Russian Defense Ministry has given an up-close look at the design of the hypersonic anti-ship missile Tsirkon (Zircon). Previously, all launches were shown only from a considerable distance. The event drew the attention of foreign online media outlets, whose verdict was unanimous: the Russian Armed Forces now have a versatile weapon which can be deployed from different platforms. The launch in question took place on December 3, 2024, during joint exercises by the Russian Navy and Aerospace Forces in the eastern Mediterranean Sea. The Defense Ministry said in a news release that the hypersonic Tsirkon missiles were launched by the frigates Admiral Gorshkov and Admiral Golovko, which were operating as part of a task force. The cameras showed launches from different angles, letting experts draw some conclusions about the Tsirkon's design. First of all, the specialists took note of the missile’s nose, which was covered, according to one account, by a "cylindrical casing," the purpose of which "is anyone’s guess." According to another, it is a special unit that stabilizes the missile immediately after its launch and is jettisoned afterwards. The latter version, in particular, is confirmed by video footage of one of the launches from afar. On these video clips it was well seen that the fore section of the missile, after the vertical aligning of the projectile, detached from it, while the missile itself went up, gaining speed. The Tsirkon’s upper stage also attracted the experts’ attention, but it was hardly possible to examine it in detail. Experts of the Army Recognition portal believe that the Tsirkon’s propulsion system consists of two stages: a solid propellant upper stage and a direct-flow air-jet engine that maintains hypersonic speed and provides maneuverability on the main part of the flight path. The specialized portal The War Zone (TWZ) suspects that the shroud in the nose of the missile conceals a ring-shaped air intake that provides cooling for the Tsirkon's direct-flow air-jet engine. The TWZ noted that the Tsirkon’s launch in the recent Defense Ministry video was carried out from the 3C14 vertical launch unit aboard a Project 22350 frigate, to which both The Admiral Gorshkov and The Admiral Golovko belong. The experts also compared the latest video available from the Russian Defense Ministry with previous footage published in October 2020 and May 2022, stating that all three videos probably showed the same type of missile. Besides, they recalled the speculations that the hypersonic Tsirkon was from the same family as the supersonic cruise missile Oniks, discussed in the West back in 2020. Both missiles can be fired with the 3C14 ship-borne launcher. After scrutinizing the recent video, TWZ specialists ruled out the Tsirkon’s similarity to the experimental US X-51 Waverider missile. Images of the latter had previously been used by many media outlets to illustrate the presumed shape of the Russian missile. The development of Russia’s sea-launched system armed with a hypersonic missile - the future Tsirkon - was announced back in February 2011. By 2016, as a source in the defense-industrial complex told TASS, the program had entered the flight tests stage. In the summer of 2019, Boris Obnosov, the CEO of the Tactical Missiles Corporation (Tsirkon was created by its affiliate, NPO Mashinostroyenia), announced the missile’s intensive testing. In January 2020, the first launch from The Admiral Gorshkov frigate took place. In October 2021, the nuclear submarine Severodvinsk carried out a test launch of the Tsirkon from the Barents Sea. On December 24, 2021, Russian President Vladimir Putin announced a salvo launch of Tsirkon missiles. The tests, he said, were "successful, flawless." In a February 2024 address to the Federal Assembly, Putin said that serial production of such missiles had begun. By that time they had already been tested in the special military operation. The Tsirkon is capable of reaching Mach 9 speeds (according to some reports, Mach 10 - TASS). The missile can hit surface and ground targets at a range of over 1,000 kilometers. The commander of The Admiral Gorshkov, Captain 1st class Igor Krokhmal, said that the range of the ship-based Tsirkon was 1,500 kilometers. Obnosov himself pointed out in an interview that "it takes a very effective missile defense system and air defense system" to intercept such a missile. Georgy Roslyakov, the commander of The Admiral Gorshkov's missile-artillery combat unit, noted that in addition to high speeds the project also possessed stealth features. The Washington Times cited the conclusions of a report by the US Congressional Research Service, whose experts noted that the existing US ground and space-based missile detection means were incapable of detecting and tracking the flight of hypersonic missiles. While the main carriers of the Tsirkon missiles are surface ships and submarines, there has been evidence of the development of a coastal missile defense system (CDS) for these hypersonic missiles. A TASS source close to the defense Ministry said in November 2022 that one launcher for them had already been created. It can also fire Oniks supersonic missiles. According to Army Recognition, one of the features of the Tsirkon that is of greatest concern is its versatility. According to the portal, "the missile offers a serious strategic advantage" because it can carry both conventional and nuclear warheads. It also noted that the missile can be launched from various carriers, including surface ships and coastal systems, which provides Russia with the ability to deploy and use it in various combat scenarios.

title: Roscosmos ready to make Oreshnik missile as powerful as Russian Defense Ministry wants
text: MOSCOW, December 5. /TASS/. Roscosmos chief Yury Borisov said the company is ready to increase the power and improve the specifications of Russia’s newest intermediate-range ballistic missile the Oreshnik to the extent required by the country’s Defense Ministry. "It will do as much as the Defense Ministry needs," he said when asked by TASS whether there are plans to increase the power and improve the specifications of the missile. Russian President Vladimir Putin said on November 21 that the US and its NATO allies had announced authorizing the use of longer-range weapons. Following the announcement, US and UK missiles attacked Russian targets in the Kursk and Bryansk regions, according to the president. Russia responded by firing its newest Oreshnik intermediate-range ballistic missile with a non-nuclear warhead at a Yuzhmash Ukrainian defense industry plant in the city of Dnepr, Putin said. The Russian president said the West could bring upon itself heavy consequences, should its inflammatory policies prompt further escalation of the conflict.

title: Russian naval group arrives at China’s Qingdao port on visit — Defense Ministry
text: MOSCOW, December 5. /TASS/. A group of Russian naval ships has arrived at the Chinese port of Qingdao on a visit, Russia’s Defense Ministry reported on Thursday. "A group of the Russian Navy’s combat ships consisting of the latest corvettes Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhapov, Rezky and Gromky and the support vessel Pechenga has arrived at the port of Qingdao today, December 5," the ministry said in a statement. The Russian naval ships have arrived in China in their long-distance deployment on a visit that will last through December 9. The visit envisages scheduled meetings of the Russian naval group’s command staff with the military command of the Chinese naval base and mutual ship attendance, it said. In addition, some cultural events have been scheduled for the crews of the Russian combat ships, in particular, sightseeing tours of Qingdao and sports competitions with Chinese naval sailors, it specified. The visit by the Russian naval ships to China will serve as yet another practical step towards strengthening cooperation between the navies of both countries, the ministry stressed.

title: Teenager wounded as Ukraine shells Novaya Kakhovka
text: GENICHESK, December 5. /TASS/. A teenager was wounded when Ukrainian troops shelled the town of Novaya Kakhovka, the Kherson Region governor’s press secretary, Vladimir Vasilenko, has told TASS. "A teenager born in 2009 was wounded in a shelling attack on Novaya Kakhovka at around 11:00 p.m. Moscow time [8:00 p.m. GMT] on Wednesday," he said. The victim was hospitalized with multiple fragmentation wounds to legs. The spokesman added that an apartment building sustained minor damage to its walls and roof. Also, its windows were smashed as a result of the attack.

title: Russian airstrikes aid Syrian army in taking out over 100 terrorists in past day
text: MOSCOW, December 4. /TASS/. The Syrian military, backed by the Russian Aerospace Forces, attacked numerous militant hideouts and fortifications in the past 24 hours, killing up to 120 members of illegal armed groups, the Russian reconciliation center’s deputy head, Capt. 1st Rank Oleg Ignasyuk, said. "The Russian Aerospace Forces continue to support the Syrian Arab Army in its operation to counter terrorist aggression in the provinces of Idlib, Hama and Aleppo. Over the past 24 hours, groups of militants, as well as their command and control centers and hideouts, came under multiple missile strikes and bombings. Up to 120 terrorists were killed. Besides, 24 vehicles, a tank and an ammunition depot were destroyed," Ignasyuk said. In a separate development, Russian military police teams carried out patrol missions in the provinces of Aleppo, Raqqa and Hasakah.

title: CSTO secretary general calls current situation in Afghanistan encouraging
text: MOSCOW, December 4. /TASS/. The current situation in Afghanistan looks encouraging, but a number of risks remain, CSTO Secretary General Imangali Tasmagambetov said during a press conference. "The situation in Afghanistan is stabilizing. This is, of course, very encouraging. But still, the risks related to terrorist organizations and drug trafficking persist," he said. In this regard, the CSTO Secretary General pointed out the program on strengthening the Tajikistan-Afghanistan border, which will take 5 years to implement. At the same time, he noted that, if something does not work out during this time span, the Collective Security Council is entitled to prolong the implementation of this program. Answering the question whether the organization holds negotiations with Uzbekistan, which also has a border with Afghanistan, he noted: "As for Uzbekistan, we hold no negotiations with the colleagues." "The length of their border [with Afghanistan] is insignificant - only about 140 km. Back in the day, Uzbekistan set up the so-called barrier on that stretch. This is a very thoroughly protected border. In terms of equipment and in terms of security, it could be compared only to the 37th parallel between North and South Korea," he added.

title: NATO infrastructure near Russia’s borders could lead to any scenario, says CSTO chief
text: MOSCOW, December 4. /TASS/. The NATO infrastructure near the borders of Russia and Belarus, along with the situation in the Eastern European region, does not rule out any scenario, Secretary General of the Collective Security Treaty Organization (CSTO) Imangali Tasmagambetov said. "Undoubtedly, our organization is closely monitoring the situation in the Eastern European part of the CSTO's zone of responsibility, and we see NATO's offensive infrastructure approaching the western borders of Belarus and Russia. The intensification of military activity and the geopolitical turbulence that we see could lead to any scenario," he said. At the same time, the CSTO chief pointed out that NATO’s attempt to invade Russia and Belarus would be sheer foolishness. "We all know very well that Russia has updated its nuclear doctrine, making significant amendments, as well as testing its newest Oreshnik hypersonic intermediate-range ballistic missile in combat conditions. This weapon has so far no equivalents in the world, which is something that potential adversaries cannot ignore. That is why, hypothetically speaking, a NATO intervention on the western borders of Belarus and Russia would be sheer foolishness," Tasmagambetov emphasized.

title: CSTO to hold drills in Belarus, Kazakhstan, Tajikistan, Kyrgyzstan in 2025 — bloc’s chief
text: MOSCOW, December 4. /TASS/. The Collective Security Treaty Organization (CSTO) will hold drills in Belarus, Kazakhstan, Tajikistan and Kyrgyzstan next year, CSTO Secretary General Imangali Tasmagambetov told a press conference on Wednesday. "Each year, we adopt an operational and combat training plan, under which we hold drills on various themes and in various directions. The 2025 operational and combat training plan has also been adopted. We will conduct drills in Belarus, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan," the bloc’s chief said. On the territory of Kyrgyzstan, the post-Soviet security bloc will hold the Rubezh 2025 (Frontier 2025) command and staff exercise and an operational gathering of the military command of the Collective Rapid Deployment Forces of the Central Asian collective security region as part of the Combat Brotherhood 2025 operational and strategic drills, he specified. "I should also note that the Kyrgyz Defense Ministry takes a very responsible approach towards holding such drills and I am deeply convinced that the exercises which will run on the territory of Kyrgyzstan in 2025 will also be held at a very high organizational level," the CSTO chief said.

title: Russian MiG-31I jets with Kinzhal missiles take part in Mediterranean drills — top brass
text: MOSCOW, December 3. /TASS/. Over 1,000 troops, 10 ships and 24 aircraft, including MiG-31I jets carrying Kinzhal hypersonic missiles are taking part in the joint drills of the Russian Navy and Aerospace Forces in the eastern Mediterranean, Russia’s Defense Ministry reported on Tuesday. "The exercise involves over 1,000 personnel, 10 ships and support vessels, 24 aircraft, including MiG-31I fighter jets of the Russian Aerospace Forces carrying Kinzhal hypersonic missiles, and a Bastion coastal defense missile system crew," the ministry said in a statement. The Russian Navy and Aerospace Forces are conducting their joint drills with the observance of international law and agreements with foreign states on the prevention of incidents at sea beyond the territorial waters and airspace above them, it said. The drills are running on December 1-3 under the direction of Navy Commander-in-Chief Admiral Alexander Moiseyev in accordance with the combat training plan of the Russian Armed Forces, it said. During the drills, the Russian taskforce built up its capabilities in the eastern Mediterranean. The combined-arms strike and anti-submarine warfare forces established as a result of this buildup are conducting missile, artillery and torpedo firings and practicing bombing. Some combat exercises involve the comprehensive use of precision weapons and the latest armaments accepted for service with the Russian Navy and Aerospace Forces recently, the ministry reported.

title: Russian Navy, Aerospace Forces launch precision missiles in Mediterranean drills
text: MOSCOW, December 3. /TASS/. Russian Navy ships and aircraft of the Aerospace Forces conducted joint launches of sea-and airborne precision missiles during drills in the eastern Mediterranean, Russia’s Defense Ministry reported on Tuesday. "During an exercise held on December 3 to practice the techniques of joint operations, the taskforces of the Russian Navy and Aerospace Forces conducted launches of sea-and airborne precision missiles in the eastern Mediterranean Sea. The frigates Admiral of the Fleet of the Soviet Union Gorshkov and Admiral Golovko operating as part of the Russian Navy’s strike force conducted launches of Tsirkon hypersonic missiles while the diesel-electric submarine Novorossiysk launched a Kalibr cruise missile at a sea target. Meanwhile, a Bastion coastal defense missile system crew conducted a combat launch of an Oniks missile from a designated area on the coast of the Mediterranean Sea," the ministry said in a statement. All the designated targets were engaged by direct hits, it said. The drills are running on December 1-3 under the direction of Navy Commander-in-Chief Admiral Alexander Moiseyev in accordance with the combat training plan of the Russian Armed Forces, it said. During the drills, the Russian taskforce built up its capabilities in the eastern Mediterranean. The combined-arms strike and anti-submarine warfare forces established as a result of this buildup are conducting missile, artillery and torpedo firings and practicing bombing. Some combat exercises involve the comprehensive use of precision weapons and the latest armaments accepted for service with the Russian Navy and Aerospace Forces recently, the ministry reported.
weapons: Admiral of the Fleet of the Soviet Union Gorshkov

title: BRICS’ role in establishment of new world order must grow — CSTO ex-Secretary General
text: MOSCOW, December 3. /TASS/. The role that BRICS plays in establishing a new world order must grow, says Nikolay Bordyuzha, Chairman of the Coordination Council of the Eurasian Information and Analysis Consortium and former Secretary General of the Collective Security Treaty Organization (CSTO). "I believe that BRICS should play a more prominent role. In certain situations, BRICS may assume the functions that the United Nations fulfills - or, rather, should fulfill, but doesn’t, because there is no consensus," he said during the TASS round table on Eurasian security following the 8th Practical Scientific Conference Forum of Russian analysts. According to the former CSTO Secretary General, the political influence of BRICS must increase. "This organization must affect not only the economic situation in its member states, but the world order in general, and the stability of the entire world," he noted. In addition, Bordyuzha pointed out that the Conference on Interaction and Confidence-Building Measures in Asia was rather successful. "This international organization, […] these confidence-building measures were discussed on this platform, and I believe they achieved a lot," he emphasized. "In my opinion, there is a need to expand the work of this conference, including with participation of hostile states, European states, and this very platform. The already established platform could be used for discussions of confidence-building measures, without which there will never be any peace in the Eurasian space."
mil_orgs: Coordination Council of the Eurasian Information and Analysis Consortium
Conference on Interaction and Confidence-Building Measures

title: Russia to be pillar of future Eurasian security system — ex-CSTO chief
text: MOSCOW, December 3. /TASS/. Russia will be pivotal in the future system of Eurasian security, Nikolay Bordyuzha, chairman of the Coordinating Council of the Eurasian Information and Analytical Consortium (EIAK), said. "We can talk about a Eurasian security system only when we guarantee stability in our state. <…> First, we must make sure we are this kind of state. I am deeply convinced <…> that Russia is a pivotal state from the point of view of creating a security system," Bordyuzha, who once was secretary general of the Collective Security Treaty Organization (CSTO), told a roundtable meeting on Eurasian security at TASS. Russia, in his words, should bring together states that can ensure stability in Eurasia and in the entire world along with it. According to Bordyuzha, it will take quite a lot of time to form a new system of European security. He noted the importance of security zones that could be established "by means of the existing international organizations." "They are the Commonwealth of Independent States (CIS), Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO), naturally, the CSTO, and some other international structures tasked to ensure security," he added.
mil_orgs: Coordinating Council of the Eurasian Information and Analytical Consortium

title: German company Helsing launches production of AI-equipped drones for deliveries to Kiev
text: BERLIN, December 2. /TASS/. German company Helsing has begun production of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) equipped with artificial intelligence (AI) technology for deliveries to Ukraine, the new drone is called HX-2. "HX-2 is already in production and the core technology is currently deployed in Ukraine," the company said in a press release. As the company notes, advanced on-board AI enables full electronic warfare and jamming resistance, capabilities developed and tested through Helsing’s extensive experience in Ukraine. In addition to being resistance to jamming, the drones "can assemble into swarms, controlled by single human operators." The HX-2 drones have a flying range of 100 km and can reach speeds of up to 220 km/h. As the Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung newspaper specifies, the payload of one device is 5 kg. Helsing plans to produce 1,000 drones per month from January 2025 and increase production volumes to 10,000 units per month in the medium term. In addition to Germany, drone production will also be located in other European countries. Earlier, the Bild newspaper reported citing sources, that Germany would begin delivering high-tech strike drones from Helsing to Kiev in December. These drones are capable of overcoming electronic warfare and called "mini-Tauruses" (by analogy with Taurus cruise missiles). A total of 4,000 of these drones with on board AI, will be delivered to Kiev. German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius confirmed that Berlin will soon supply Ukraine with 4,000 high-tech attack drones. Germany is the second-largest supplier of weapons to Ukraine after the United States. Russian authorities have repeatedly stated that pumping Ukraine with weapons will not reduce Russia's determination or change the course of the special military operation.

title: Syrian, Russian airstrikes in Aleppo, Idlib eliminate 400 terrorists — command
text: BEIRUT, December 2. /TASS/. The Syrian army, in coordination with Russia's Aerospace Forces, has eliminated over 400 terrorists, some of them foreign, in strikes on the Aleppo and Idlib provinces in recent hours, the Arab republic’s army command reported. "Precision strikes carried out by our armed forces in coordination with allied Russian forces eliminated more than 400 terrorists in the past few hours, with foreign citizens among them," the statement published on the Facebook (banned in Russia; owned by Meta Corporation, recognized as extremist in Russia) account of the Syrian Defense Ministry reads. The command reiterated that rumors of militants taking Syrian territory under control were untrue, saying that the terrorists have already "started to abandon" their positions due to casualties suffered. On the morning of November 27, the Jabhat al-Nusra extremist group (banned in Russia) carried out a large-scale attack in northern Syria. According to a statement by the Syrian Armed Forces’ Command, the terrorists attempted to attack villages and towns under the protection of the Syrian army and military facilities, continuing to target the positions of government forces. The Syrian army launched an operation to repel the raid. On November 30, the country’s armed forces’ command stated that the army was conducting strikes on the positions of terrorists who had managed to infiltrate many neighborhoods of the city of Aleppo. According to the Syrian Defense Ministry’s statement, the army was forced to reorganize its forces to protect the lives of civilians and troops and prepare for a counterattack.

title: Syrian Air Force strikes militants in Aleppo Region — Defense Ministry
text: BEIRUT, December 2. /TASS/. Aircraft of the Syrian Air Force, supported by the Russian Aerospace Forces, carried out strikes on terrorist clusters on the outskirts of the As-Safira settlement, southeast of Aleppo, according to a report published by the republic's Defense Ministry on its Facebook page (banned in Russia and owned by Meta Corporation, which is recognized as extremist in Russia). According to the statement, the strikes eliminated and wounded dozens of militants and destroyed several pieces of military equipment belonging to them. The Syrian military command stated on November 28 that units of the Jabhat al-Nusra extremist group "launched a large-scale attack on a broad front" on the morning of November 27. According to the statement, the terrorists attempted to target "the villages and towns protected by the Syrian army, as well as military installations." On November 30, the Syrian army stated it was forced to regroup its forces while repelling a terrorist attack in Aleppo to protect the lives of civilians and servicemen, and was preparing for a counteroffensive.

title: Two-week NATO drills begin in Estonia — newspaper
text: VILNIUS, December 2. /TASS/. A two-week exercise codenamed Pikne (Lightning) involving NATO troops begins in Estonia on Monday,Postimeesreported. "The aim of the exercise is to practice the rapid deployment of reinforcements and cooperation between French, British, and Estonian forces. The French Armed Forces will carry out a rapid deployment operation to Estonia by air and on land, followed by joint maneuvers with British and Estonian forces in the air and at sea to practice the activities prescribed in our plans," the news portal quoted Estonian Major General Indrek Sirel, who is responsible for training events, as saying. The maneuvers will involve some 2,000 troops from Great Britain, Latvia, the United States, France, and Estonia, mostly taking place in the latter’s northern and northeastern areas. Units from the Estonian land, air, and naval forces and NATO’s battlegroup in the former Soviet republic will participate. The North Atlantic Alliance’s units will practice mechanisms of deploying forces to a potential conflict zone as they will focus on integrating those into the bloc’s battlegroup in Estonia to repel threats from land as well as aerial and naval ones. On November 30, Estonian public broadcaster ERR reported that six warships from Standing NATO Maritime Group One (SNMG1) visited the Estonian capital.

title: Over 300,000 personnel undergo combat training for Ukraine operation — Russia’s top brass
text: MOSCOW, December 2. /TASS/. Over 300,000 personnel have undergone combat training in reserve regiments for deploying to the special military operation area in Ukraine, Head of the Main Combat Training Department of the Russian Armed Forces Ivan Buvaltsev said in an article forthe Krasnaya Zvezda newspaperon Monday. "Serious successes have been achieved in reserve regiments. These are the formations, which train servicemen who have voluntarily signed contracts for their participation in the special military operation. Seven companies of instructors and eight training companies set up for these purposes have trained over 300,000 personnel," the defense official said. Military instruction pays special attention to servicemen’s individual training. The personnel acquire skills in shooting practice, tactical and engineering training, tactical medicine and special instruction depending on military specialty, he said. The stage of single training is completed with comprehensive tactical drills, in which servicemen learn the tactics of operating as part of combat elements and small tactical groups, namely, combat duos, trios and quintets, he said. The personnel training was based on the careful study of the combat experience gained in the special military operation in Ukraine. The techniques and methods elaborated in this process were promptly introduced into combat training. The system of combat training organized in this way has proven its efficiency, the defense official stressed.

title: Over 320 militants killed, over 60 their combat vehicles destroyed in Syria in past day
text: MOSCOW, December 1. /TASS/. Syria’s government army and Russia’s aerospace forces continue repelling terrorists’ aggression in Idlib, Hama, and Aleppo, killing at least 320 militants and destroying over 60 their combat vehicles during the past day, Oleg Ignasyuk, deputy chief of the Russian Center for Reconciliation of the Opposing Parties in Syria (a division of the Russian defense ministry), said. "The Syrian government army, backed by the Russian aerospace forces, continues the operation to rebuff the terrorist aggression in the Idlib, Hama, and Aleppo governorates. During the past day, missile and bomb strikes were delivered on areas of the deployement of militants and their weapons, arms and munitions depots, artillery and air defense positions, and control centers. At least 320 militants were killed and 63 armored and other combat vehicles were destroyed," he said. He also said that aircraft of the so-called international anti-terrorist coalition led by the United States continue to create dangerous situations that may cause air accidents or incidents and escalate the situation in Syria’s airspace. "During the day, three violations of the deconfliction protocols of December 9, 2019 linked with the flights by the US-led coalition unmanned aerial vehicles were reported during the day," he said, adding that there were 54 such violations in November. Apart from that, five pairs of the coalition’s F-15 fighter jets violated Syria’s airspace in the al-Tanf area, across which international air routes run, ten times during the day. In November, as many as 345 such violations were reported.
mil_orgs: Russian Center for Reconciliation of the Opposing Parties in Syria

title: Russia can fully equip its military with Oreshnik missiles — Roscosmos CEO
text: MOSCOW, December 1. /TASS/. Russia has all possibilities to fully equip the Defense Ministry with Oreshnik missiles, Roscosmos CEO Yury Borisov said. "As for the high-precision systems, we have already significantly increased the production of this type of weapon over the past two years or so. As for the Oreshnik [missiles], we have all the necessary capabilities to supply the Defense Ministry, let's say, with the required quantity," he told VGTRK reporter Pavel Zarubin. Russian President Vladimir Putin said on November 21 that the United States and its NATO allies had earlier announced that they would authorize Ukraine’s use of long-range weapons to hit inside Russia, after which American and British missiles struck Russian military facilities in the Kursk and Bryansk regions. He said that Russia responded to those attacks by firing Russia's newest Oreshnik hypersonic intermediate-range ballistic missile with a non-nuclear warhead at a Ukrainian defense industry facility, the Yuzhmash plant in Dnepr (formerly Dnepropetrovsk). The Russian leader emphasized that the West's provocative policies could have dire consequences if they further escalate the conflict.

title: Russian forces destroy Ukrainian temporary deployment site in Nikolayev Region
text: MOSCOW, December 1. /TASS/. The Russian armed forces destroyed a Ukrainian temporary deployment site in the Nikolayev Region with an Iskander missile system, the Defense Ministry said. "The Russian Defense Ministry has published footage of an Iskander missile strike on the Ukrainian temporary deployment site located in the settlement of Rovnoye in the Nikolayev region (15 kilometers north of the town of Ochakov). The temporary deployment site of the neo-Nazis was destroyed by a high-precision preemptive strike," the ministry said.

title: Russian forces liberate DPR’s Petrovka, Ilyinka over past day — top brass
text: MOSCOW, December 1. /TASS/. The Russian armed forces have liberated Petrovka and Ilyinka in the Donetsk People's Republic, the Defense Ministry said. "The battlegroup South liberated the settlement of Ilyinka in the Donetsk People's Republic. The battlegroup Center liberated the settlement of Petrovka in the Donetsk People's Republic," the ministry said. According to it, the Russian servicemen also hit the infrastructure of Ukrainian military airfields, drone assembly shops and warehouses over the day. "The Russian forces struck the infrastructure of military airfields, drone assembly shops and warehouses, as well as enemy manpower and equipment in 136 areas," the ministry said. In addition, Russian air defenses have downed 55 Ukrainian fixed-wing drones over the past 24 hours, the military said. A total of 649 aircraft, 283 helicopters, 37,013 drones, 586 anti-aircraft missile systems, 19,577 tanks and other armored combat vehicles, 1,497 multiple rocket launchers, 18,807 field artillery and mortar guns, 28,905 units of special military vehicles have been destroyed since the start of the special military operation, the ministry added. Russia’s battlegroup East has eliminated more than 100 Ukrainian servicemen over the past 24 hours, the Defense Ministry said. "The battlegroup East continued advancing deep into the enemy's defense and defeated Ukraine’s 32nd mechanized brigade and the 241st territorial defense brigade near Velikaya Novoselka, Razliv and Novy Komar in the Donetsk People's Republic. "The enemy losses amounted to up to 100 servicemen, a US-made HMMWV truck, five vehicles, a 152 mm Akatsiya howitzer and a 122 mm D-20 howitzer," the statement said. The battlegroup West has wipes out up to 500 Ukrainian servicemen over the past 24 hours, the military pointed out. "The battlegroup West has repelled 11 counterattacks by Ukraine’s 14th mechanized, 3rd tank brigades, as well as by the 113th and 117th territorial defense brigades. The Ukrainian losses amounted to up to 500 servicemen, two pickup trucks, an Anclav-N electronic warfare station and four ammunition depots," the statement said. The battlegroup North has wiped up to 85 Ukrainian servicemen and the battlegroup Dnepr has eliminated up to 85 enemy troops over the past 24 hours, the army said. "In the Kharkov area, the battlegroup North defeated Ukraine’s 92nd airborne assault brigade and repelled two counterattacks by the 57th and 58th mechanized brigades. The enemy lost up to 30 servicemen, an armored personnel carrier, two vehicles and two 122 mm D-30 howitzers," the ministry said. It also added that the battlegroup Dnepr eliminated up to 85 Ukrainian servicemen, as well as destroyed three enemy vehicles, an electric warfare station and two ammunition depots.

title: Chinese-Russian patrols not directed against third countries — defense official
text: BEIJING, December 1. /TASS/. Joint patrols of the Russian Aerospace Forces and the Chinese People's Liberation Army Air Force in the Asia-Pacific region are of a routine nature, Defense Ministry Spokesman Zhang Xiaogang said. "The joint Chinese-Russian strategic air patrol is a routine process in the implementation of the annual cooperation plan. It is not directed against third countries and has nothing to do with the current international and regional situation," the Chinese defense official said. Earlier, aircraft of the Russian Aerospace Forces and the People's Liberation Army Air Force conducted joint patrols in the western Pacific. It was the ninth joint strategic air patrol in the Sea of Japan. During the maneuvers, Russian aircraft landed and took off from an airfield in China. The joint flight of the Russian and Chinese aircraft lasted about eight hours. During the flight, the crews of the Tu-95MS strategic missile carriers carried out aerial refueling at night. It is reported that at certain stages of the route the strategic missile carriers were escorted by fighter jets of foreign states.
mil_orgs: Chinese People's Liberation Army Air Force

title: Russian forces liberate 88 settlements during fall
text: MOSCOW, December 1. /TASS/. The Russian army has liberated 88 settlements in the Donetsk and Lugansk People's Republics, the Zaporozhye, Kursk and Kharkov Regions in the fall of 2024, according to TASS calculations based on the Russian Defense Ministry's reports.
