The Aegis Ashore facility in Redzikowo is part of the EPAA (European Phased Adaptive Approach) program for the phased deployment of missile defense system components in Europe. The system also includes Aegis radar-equipped ships based in Rota, Spain, an AN/TPY-2 radar station in Turkey (on alert since 2011), and another Aegis Ashore facility at the Deveselu base in Romania.

title: Kyrgyzstan fully equipped to maintain law and order, Kremlin spokesman says
text: MOSCOW, November 13. /TASS/. Kyrgyzstan’s leadership has all the resources required to maintain law and order in the country, Kremlin Spokesman Dmitry Peskov told reporters. "We are confident that Kyrgyzstan’s leadership has all that is necessary to ensure law and order in the country," he noted, commenting on reports of a group of individuals detained in Kyrgyzstan for plotting to seize power. "Of course, we are always in touch with our Kyrgyz friends. Law enforcement officials maintain constant contact, and our representatives receive information," Peskov pointed out. On November 12, the Kyrgyz Interior Ministry announced the detention of a six-member group led by former Deputy Prosecutor General Adyl uulu Kuban, who was plotting to provoke mass unrest in the country to seize power. According to investigators, the suspects planned to take advantage of nationwide sports games to call for acts of violence and destabilize the domestic situation on November 17, the day of local elections, in order to "seize power by force."

title: FSB foils attempted terrorist attack on power station in Nizhny Tagil
text: MOSCOW, November 13. /TASS/. The Russian Federal Security Service (FSB) has foiled a terrorist attack on an electrical substation that provides energy to a military-industrial facility and the infrastructure of the Sverdlovsk Railway in Nizhny Tagil, the Public Relations Center (PRC) of the FSB reported. "A Russian citizen born in 1997, who was affiliated with a pro-Ukrainian terrorist organization, was arrested during operational and investigative activities when he was planning to carry out a terrorist attack at an electrical substation in the city of Nizhny Tagil. The substation powers one of the region’s military-industrial facilities and the infrastructure of Sverdlovsk Railway," the PRC said. "During the investigation, he pleaded guilty," the FSB added. It was revealed that the detainee had materials for producing an improvised explosive device and had been communicating with Ukrainian security services via Telegram. The Investigative Committee of Russia in the Sverdlovsk Region has initiated a criminal case under part 3 of Article 30 and part 2 of Article 205 of the Russian Criminal Code (attempted act of terrorism), which carries a sentence of up to fifteen years in prison. The suspect has been taken into custody.

title: Russian naval aircraft hit targets by missiles in drills over Barents Sea
text: MOSCOW, November 13. /TASS/. Fighter jets of the Northern Fleet’s composite air regiment hit simulated enemy targets during drills over the Barents Sea, the Fleet’s press office reported on Wednesday. The Northern Fleet’s press office also uploaded a video showing the drills. "Practical missile launches were planned and this is what we have done today at the Timur practice range located in the waters of the Barents Sea. The fighter jets launched air-to-air missiles against targets simulated by illumination air bombs," aviation wing commander Gennady said.

title: Over 3,800 people cross into Syria from Lebanon in past day — Russian military
text: MOSCOW, November 12. /TASS/. More than 3,800 people entered the Syrian territory from Lebanon in the past 24 hours, Oleg Ignasyuk, deputy chief of the Russian Center for Reconciliation of the Opposing Parties in Syria (a division of the Russian defense ministry), said. "Over the past day, as many as 3,849 people crossed Syria’s border from Lebanon via the checkpoints of Al-Arida, Al-Jawasiyeh, Al-Dabousiyah, Jisr Qamar, and Jdaidet Yabous," he said.
mil_orgs: Russian Center for Reconciliation of the Opposing Parties in Syria

title: Mi-171A3 helicopter draws interest from partners in China — Russian Helicopters
text: ZHUHAI /China/, November 12. /TASS /. The Mi-171A3 heavy multipurpose helicopter developed by Russian Helicopters (part of Rostec) is of interest to potential customers in China, who could use it to perform a variety of tasks in the oil and gas production industry, Dmitry Zuikov, the head of the company's marketing department, told TASS on the sidelines of Airshow China in the city of Zhuhai (southern Guangdong province). "We are presenting the 171A3 helicopter at this exhibition in the form of scale models. This is a new helicopter, which we have developed together with companies of Russia’s oil and gas sector for various tasks such as airlifting work shifts and cargoes to offshore drilling platforms and performing search and rescue operations far away from the shore, including in the Arctic regions," he said. This helicopter is capable of carrying up to 24 people in energy-absorbing seats and meets all current requirements for helicopters in the offshore industry, which are very strict in terms of safety and equipment. The helicopter can fly in harsh conditions, particularly those inside the Arctic Circle, where satellite communication coverage is poor. The Mi-171A3 can also be quickly, within a few hours, converted from passenger to search and rescue configuration. "Potential Chinese customers have considerable interest in this helicopter. We have been able to see that at our stand and in general during this exhibition. China is active in the oil and gas sector, including offshore drilling platforms. I believe that the 171A3 will be an excellent means to increase the scope of these projects in China and reduce risks in the development of remote fields," Zuikov said. The company also brought the KA-32A11BC helicopter to the show this year. China already has 27 such aircraft, and the order portfolio keeps growing. In China, they are mainly used for fighting fires. At the exhibition in Zhuhai, Zuikov noted, meetings are held not only with the operators of the company's equipment from China, but also with partners from other countries in the region. At the same time, he noted that among the twin-engine helicopters operating in China more than 30% were models created by the Russian manufacturer he represents. This year's Airshow China in Zhuhai has brought together representatives from 47 countries, including Russia and the United States. Over 1,000 enterprises from China and other countries and about 150 foreign participants will demonstrate their selections - twice as many as at the previous exhibition. The key features of the airshow are military and civilian equipment, space, navigation and radar equipment, airborne armaments, rocket engines, and fire control and air defense systems. Since its founding in 1996, Airshow China has become a major site for showcasing advanced aviation and aerospace technologies and equipment from China and other countries. The show has developed into a major international platform for promoting business cooperation in aviation and aerospace technology and equipment. This year's air show is timed for the 75th anniversary of the Chinese People's Liberation Army Air Force (PLA Air Force), celebrated on November 11.
mil_orgs: Chinese People's Liberation Army Air Force

title: Kalashnikov completes delivery of Chukavin rifles to armed forces under 2024 contract
text: MOSCOW, November 12. /TASS/. Kalashnikov Concern said it has completed executing contract to supply the Chukavin sniper rifle, a firearm used in the special operation area, to the armed forces this year. "Kalashnikov Concern has completed the contract for the manufacture of the Chukavin sniper rifle and shipped the products to the customer in full. The Chukavin rifle represents a new generation of Russian sniper firearms, which is actively used in the special military operation zone," the company said in a statement. According to Kalashnikov Concern President Alan Lushnikov, the rifle has improved usability and firing accuracy, and can integrate any sighting systems of Russian and foreign origin alike. "At the same time, the Chukavin rifle has retained the reliability of the Dragunov sniper rifle, which is widely known in and out of the country and which Kalashnikov Concern continues to produce in large quantities," he was quoted as saying in the company statement. The first volume-produced shipment of Chukavin rifles was delivered to the military in 2023. It is a semi-automatic sniper rifle that comes in the 7.62mm by 54mm caliber version and is compatible with Dragunov rifle magazines, as the new rifle is intended to replace and old one, while inheriting its reliability. Chukavin told TASS that the new rifle outperforms its predecessor in terms of accuracy by 25%-30%. Without a magazine, the Chukavin rifle weighs 4.8 kilograms. With a flame arrester and an unfolded buttstock, it has the length of 1,170 mm, of which the barrel makes up 620 mm.

title: CSTO countries discuss ways to strengthen military personnel training
text: MOSCOW, November 12. /TASS/. Heads of military education management bodies from defense agencies of Collective Security Treaty Organization (CSTO) countries, who gathered in Astana, discussed ways to reinforce the joint military personnel training system, CSTO Joint Staff Spokesman Vladislav Shchegrikovich told TASS. "During the event, officials conducted an in-depth exchange of views on organizing military personnel and specialist training. They discussed proposals to enhance collaboration between military educational institutions and developed several recommendations to improve the joint training system for CSTO member countries," the statement said. Colonel General Andrey Serdyukov, Chief of the CSTO Joint Staff, delivered a video address to the event participants. He highlighted that a network of foundational training, methodological, and research organizations had been established in CSTO countries "to optimize the effectiveness of the educational process and improve research activities for the development of the CSTO’s military." "The status of basic training and methodological organizations has been granted to 12 military educational institutions," Serdyukov emphasized. "Eight specialized research organizations and military educational institutions have been designated as core research centers for scientific and practical work on developing, improving, training, and deploying forces and resources. The Military Academy of the General Staff of the Armed Forces of the Russian Federation is the leading institution among the CSTO’s basic training and research organizations," he added. A meeting of the working group under the CSTO Defense Ministers Council on coordinating joint military personnel training and scientific work was held under the chairmanship of Major General Igor Muravlannikov, Head of the Military Education Department and Deputy Head of the Main Personnel Department of the Russian Defense Ministry. The event brought together heads of military education management bodies from the defense agencies of Belarus, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Russia, and Tajikistan, as well as representatives of training and research organizations, a group of officers from the CSTO Joint Staff led by Lieutenant General Ruslan Shpekbayev, and members of the CSTO Secretariat.

title: Russia to equip 290 landing sites for drone operations by 2030 — regulator
text: MOSCOW, November 12. /TASS/. The Russian Federal Air Transport Agency (Rosaviatsiya) plans to equip 290 landing sites for drone operations in all Russian regions by 2030, the agency’s deputy head Andrey Dobryakov said at the Eurasian Aerospace Congress. According to his presentation, there are plans to create sites at 49 federal airfields and 241 owned by the Russian regions. "We plan to create 290 such sites in all 89 constituent entities of the Russian Federation. We also plan to create 290 specialized regional flights for UAS (unmanned aircraft systems - TASS)," he said. The agency also plans to place landing sites in 16 constituent entities of Russia in 2025. These include the federal cities of Moscow and St. Petersburg, as well as the regions of Kaluga, Smolensk, Novgorod, Arkhangelsk and Orenburg, the Republic of Tatarstan, Khanty-Mansi Autonomous District, the regions of Krasnoyarsk and Irkutsk, the Republic of Sakha (Yakutia) and Chukotka Autonomous District. Subsequently, the landing sites will be equipped with all the necessary infrastructure as part of the federal project "Development of infrastructure, ensuring security and the formation of a specialized UAS certification system." At the moment, the Yamalo-Nenets Autonomous District, Samara and Tomsk regions are equipped with the sites necessary for the operation of UAS. In addition to that, more than 30 airports in Russia will be equipped with an anti-drone system, Dobryakov added. "We are planning, and we will definitely achieve this - 31 first-category airfields of both federal and regional significance will be equipped with protection against unauthorized impact of unmanned aircraft systems," Dobryakov said. According to his presentation, 20 first-category airfields owned by the federal government will be equipped with anti-drone systems in 2025-2028, and 11 regional airports will receive them in 2026-2028. By 2030, it is planned to equip 290 facilities of the unified infrastructure of UAS control and monitoring lines with information security systems.

title: Russia demonstrates Il-76MD-90A(E) airlifter to 11 foreign customers this year
text: ZHUHAI /China/, November 12. /TASS/. Rosoboronexport presented Il-76MD-90A(E) airlifter to eleven foreign customers in 2024, while Il-78MK-90A air tanker was demonstrated to five of them, company CEO Alexander Mikheyev said at Airshow China. "Il-76MD-90A(E) debuted at Dubai Airshow in 2023 followed by the World Defense Show in Saudi Arabia in 2024. It triggered a big attention due to its new technical concept with upgraded onboard equipment and expanded functionalities for transportation and special tasks," he said. Il-76MD-90A is an upgraded Il-76 (Candid by NATO classification). A new navigational complex, automatic controls and communications increase flight safety and airdrop precision. The latest modification is distinguished by new equipment, including a glass cockpit, modern PS-90A-76 engines, a modified wing and reinforced chassis. Il-76MD-90A is used to develop Il-78M-90A air tanker.

title: Combat experience distinguishes Su-57 among fifth-generation fighters
text: ZHUHAI /China/, November 12. /TASS/. Combat experience of Su-57E fighter jet in high-tech hostilities distinguishes it among fifth-generation fighter jets, Rosoboronexport CEO Alexander Mikheyev said at Airshow China in Zhuhai. "Su-57E fully meets all requirements to fifth-generation aircraft. Its major competitive advantage is the real combat engagement experience. The Russian Defense Ministry said it excellently performed in combat. The jet is distinguished by powerful weapons with the latest missiles, as well as a high survivability due to low signature and modern onboard defense," he said. Russia for the first time demonstrates Su-57E and its weapons in Zhuhai. "They include Kh-58UShKE antiradar missile, Grom-E1 guided missile, smart K08BE air bomb and guided UPAB-1500B-E glide bomb. Specialists are mostly interested in the new-generation Kh-69 precision cruise missile," he added. Su-57E will participate in the flight program of Airshow China. Su-57 multirole fifth-generation fighter jet was designed to destroy all air, ground, and water surface targets, monitor the airspace at major distances from deployment bases, make adversary headquarters, command posts and control systems inoperative. The jet can engage a broad range of guided and unguided weapons, including air-to-air short, medium and long-range missiles, smart 250, 500 and 1500 kg bombs (the latter from outside suspension). Maximum combat payload is 10 tons. The aircraft stealth characteristics are boosted by the construction shape, as well as reflecting and absorbing coating materials and electronic warfare means. Some weapons are hidden inside the aircraft to decrease signature. The measures weaken the reflected signal and deviate it from the source. Adversary radar does not receive the information on the location and speed of the aircraft. Low signature in the optical band is ensured by digital (pixel) camouflage painting of the aircraft.

title: Russia’s Su-57 fifth-generation fighter debuts at Airshow China
text: ZHUHAI, China, November 12. /TASS/. Russia’s Su-57 fifth-generation fighter jet soared into the skies on the first day of Airshow China in Zhuhai, marking the world premiere of its export modification and attracting significant interest from airshow attendees, a TASS correspondent reported from the event. The fighter, piloted by merited test pilot and Hero of Russia Sergey Bogdan, demonstrated an aerobatic maneuver during the international airshow in China. Earlier, Sergey Chemezov, CEO of Russia’s state tech corporation Rostec, stated that the Su-57 is the only fifth-generation aircraft globally that has demonstrated its exceptional performance characteristics in all combat scenarios. The fighter also boasts impressive survivability due to its low radar signature and advanced onboard defensive systems. Rostec highlighted that the Su-57E export modification is a key attraction of Russia’s exposition at Airshow China, emphasizing its ability to deploy advanced precision weapons and its minimal radar signature. The Sukhoi Su-57 (NATO reporting name: Felon) is a Russian-made fifth-generation multirole fighter designed to engage air, ground and naval targets. The Su-57 features stealth technology with extensive use of composite materials, can achieve supersonic cruising speeds and is equipped with cutting-edge onboard electronic systems, including a powerful onboard computer (the so-called electronic second pilot), a radar system spread across its body and other innovations, in particular, armament placed inside its fuselage. The Su-57’s AI system assists with some of the pilot’s tasks, including flying the aircraft and preparing weapons for use. On display near an airfield at Airshow China, the Su-57 export version allows attendees to photograph the aircraft and learn about its operational features.

title: Top Russian security official hails strategic cooperation with China
text: BEIJING, November 12. /TASS/. Partnership between Moscow and Beijing can serve as an example of cooperation between countries, Russian Security Council Secretary Sergey Shoigu said. "Comprehensive partnership and strategic interaction [between Russia and China] are an example of cooperation between two powers in today’s world. Without being based on a military and political alliance such as those established during the Cold War, relations between our countries surpass these sorts of intergovernmental ties," Shoigu pointed out at a meeting with Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi. The top Russian security official noted that Russia-China ties were based on the principles of mutual respect, equality, trust, non-interference in each other’s domestic affairs and support for each other on the issues that the parties found important. "Russia-China relations of comprehensive partnership and strategic cooperation have reached the highest level in history. Russia and China share similar approaches to the key international and regional issues," Shoigu added.

title: US-led coalition aircraft violate Syrian airspace 12 times in past day
text: MOSCOW, November 11. /TASS/. The US-led coalition’s aircraft violated Syria’s twelve times during the past day, Oleg Ignasyuk, deputy chief of the Russian Center for Reconciliation of the Opposing Parties in Syria (a division of the Russian defense ministry), said. "Aircraft of the so-called international anti-terrorist coalition led by the United States continue to create dangerous situations that may cause air accidents or incidents and escalate the situation in Syria’s airspace," he said. "Three pairs of the coalition’s F-15 fighter jets (two times each) violated Syria’s airspace in the al-Tanf area, across which international air routes run, twelve times during the day.".
mil_orgs: Russian Center for Reconciliation of the Opposing Parties in Syria

title: EU chief outlines his visit to Ukraine's first line of defense
text: BRUSSELS, November 11. /TASS/. European Union (EU) High Representative Josep Borrell visited the first defense line of the Ukrainian army during his farewell trip to Ukraine, the EU chief wrote on the X social network. "I had the opportunity to visit frontline defense fortifications close to the border, where I witnessed the strength and resilience of the Ukrainian army," he said, adding that "more and faster military support is crucial" for Kiev. Borrell did not specify which of Ukraine’s borders he had actually seen. A photo published as evidence shows Borrell in a tidy wooden room, surrounded by people in camouflage-colored uniforms. Borrell will leave his post on December 1, 2024. He will be succeeded by former Estonian Prime Minister Kaja Kallas, whose appointment will be formally approved by the European Parliament in Brussels on November 12.

title: Presidential aide calls for boosting Russia's role in global maritime affairs
text: MOSCOW, November 11. /TASS/. Russia needs to ramp up cooperation with friendly countries - primarily those in BRICS - in shipbuilding and ensuring maritime security, says Presidential Aide Nikolay Patrushev, Chairman of the Maritime Board of Russia. "We must increase our role in the World Ocean, enhance our own capabilities, and strengthen cooperation with friendly countries," Patrushev said in an interview for the Kommersant newspaper. According to the official, "BRICS member states have big-time maritime potential." "Even for Ethiopia, the only landlocked BRICS country, maritime trade is of undeniable interest," the presidential aide said. "The geographic positioning of BRICS countries suggests that we can’t do without working together in the World Ocean. We are talking about maintaining stability and security with regard to maritime routes, combining efforts in shipbuilding, developing port infrastructure, training maritime personnel and introducing new technologies," Patrushev underscored.

title: Russia's Black Sea Fleet combat-ready to counter threats — presidential aide
text: MOSCOW, November 11. /TASS/. Russia's Black Sea Fleet is combat-ready and prepared to face challenges and counter naval threats, Nikolay Patrushev, Russian presidential aide and chairman of the Maritime Board, has said in an interview with the daily Kommersant. "I have just visited the Black Sea Fleet to look into the combat duty routine of the fleet's forces. I am certain about its combat capability and readiness to confront challenges and naval threats," he said. Patrushev noted that despite Kiev's aggressive actions in the region, coordinated by NATO specialists, there is nothing that might look like "defeat" of Russia's fleet in the Black Sea. The presidential aide pointed out that Russia would not let anyone to weaken its position in the region. "Moreover, we will not tolerate the permanent naval presence of non-Black Sea states in violation of the Montreux Convention," Patrushev added.

title: Rheinmetall announces provision of satellite images to Kiev in cooperation with ICEYE
text: BERLIN, November 11. /TASS/. Germany’s Rheinmetall defense concern announced that it has started providing satellite images to Kiev in cooperation with Finland’s ICEYE aerospace company since October. According to the statement, published on the Rheinmetall website, the intensified cooperation with ICEYE in September, 2014 has allegedly "yielded its first fruits." "In order to satisfy Ukraine’s urgent demand for SAR [Synthetic-aperture radar - TASS] satellite surveillance, Rheinmetall and Ukraine signed an agreement with support from the German government," the company said. The agreement expands the options for provision of data, obtained from SAR micro-satellites, as well as other services, provided by ICEYE. "Since the beginning of October, Ukraine has been receiving new satellite images, obtained via SAR satellites within the cooperation between Rheinmetall and ICEYE," the company continued. It claimed that these images will augment other intelligence used for decision-making and mission planning. According to Rheinmetall, this would allegedly give Ukraine an advantage in surveillance, target detection, and deployment on the battlefield. Rheinmetall is Germany’s largest defense company. It profits from the Ukrainian conflict and predicts a significant increase in its income in the future. In 2023, its turnover increased by 12% to 7.1 billion euros, and its net profit rose by 9%, to 0.6 billion euros.

title: Russia says Su-35S fighter jets provide cover to bombers, attack aircraft in Kursk Region
text: MOSCOW, November 11. /TASS/. Su-35S multi-role super-maneuverable fighter jets escorded bomber and ground-attack aircraft during airstrikes on Ukrainian armored vehicles and troops near the border in the Kursk Region, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "While performing their combat missions, crews of Su-35S multipurpose fighter jets worked to provide air cover in the designated area of operations of bomber and ground-attack aircraft, as well as helicopters of the army aviation as they carried out airstrikes on Ukrainian armored vehicles and servicemen," it said. Also, one aircraft used onboard weapons systems to detect enemy air defense sites, according to the ministry.

title: Russian Knights aerobatic crews arrive in China for international air show
text: MOSCOW, November 11. /TASS/. Crews of the Russian Knights aerobatics team flying their Su-35S and Su-30SM multi-role fighter jets arrived in China for the international Airshow China 2024, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "Crews of the Russian Knights aerobatics team flying their Su-35S and Su-30SM multi-role fighter jets arrived in the People's Republic of China to take part in the international Airshow China 2024," it said. According to the ministry, the Russian Knights crews held qualification flights and aerial training ahead of their aerobatics show, which will be held at Zhuhai airfield in China. At Airshow China, the Russian Knights will show off performance and technical capabilities of the ultra-maneuverable, state-of-the-art Russian Su-35S and Su-30SM fighter jets. The ministry said the Russian Knights pilots will for the first time demonstrate aerobatics by a single Su-35S aircraft and a group of six of them. The audience will be shown such maneuvers as the loop-the-loop, oblique loop, barrel roll, and the bell maneuver.

title: Russia expands production of VK-2500 helicopter engines
text: NOVOSIBIRSK, November 11. /TASS/. The United Engine Corporation (UEC) has ramped up production of VK-2500 engines for Ka-50 and Mi-8 helicopters several-fold this year, Deputy CEO Yury Shmotin said. "In 2024, [production of] VK-2500 engines (for the Ka-50 and Mi-8 helicopters and their variations) has increased manifold," the senior executive said. Meanwhile, the producer is facing greater costs, which have gone up by 20% over the last four years, Shmotin added. "Supplier costs have increased dramatically. Over the last four years, the price of component parts from suppliers in the machinery sector has quadrupled. The delivery cost of raw materials, blanks, disks and shafts has doubled. Producing in-house has become 20% more expensive for us," he noted. Shmotin sees the next two years as paramount to the company’s trajectory. "We need to significanlly boost serial machinery production. These are primarily commercial engines. Today, we are at 50% of our planned production goal," he added. The Klimov VK-2500 engine is a Russian turbofan engine. It has several variations.

title: Ukraine admits to mismanagement of military personnel
text: MOSCOW, November 11. /TASS/. Maria Berlinskaya, the founder of Ukraine’s Center for Aerial Reconnaissance Support, acknowledged that her country has mismanaged its military personnel. "Here, good engineers become bad mortar gunners, and good drivers become bad snipers. That said, we have been misusing personnel," she told the Ukrainskaya Pravda newspaper in an interview. Berlinskaya recounted how Ukraine’s army received tens of thousands of people, including "the smartest managers, the smartest economists, and very good businessmen, for free." "These human resources should be used efficiently," she argued, drawing parallels between brigade commanders and CEOs of major businesses. Ukrainian lawmakers have also hinted at how inefficiently Ukrainian military personnel have been utilized, with large numbers of soldiers missing in the army. On October 30, Verkhovna Rada member Maryana Bezuglaya, who has long criticized Ukrainian senior military officers, said that Ukraine’s commander-in-chief, Alexander Syrsky, had issued instructions to convert technical specialists in military aviation into infantrymen. Ukraine’s military top brass made a similar decision regarding one-fifth of hospital staff, she added. On November 2, the lawmaker said that specialists from other branches of the military, including air defense forces, had been transferred to infantry units amid a shortage of personnel. Later, former spokesman for the Ukrainian Air Force Yuri Ignat confirmed the transfer of a large number of Air Force servicemen and medics to infantry, describing these processes as "very painful."

title: UAC delivers Su-57, Su-35S fighter jets
text: MOSCOW, November 11. /TASS/. The United Aircraft Corporation (UAC) delivered to the Russian Aerospace Forces a batch of Su-57 and Su-35S fighter jets. UAC CEO Vadim Badekha said there would be other batches this year. The fighter jets have passed the whole cycle of factory trials and were accepted. Military pilots tested them at various operational regimes, Rostec said adding the fighter jets flew to the deployment airfield. "UAC enterprises continue rhythmical operation to fulfil the commitments. Other batches of Su-57 fifth-generation and Su-35S multirole fighter jets of generation 4++ will be delivered by the end of the year. The aircraft are at various production and test stages," Badekha said. Intellectual solutions in Su-57 provide excellent flight and combat characteristics, he said adding Su-57 is the best aircraft in its class. Su-57 multirole fifth-generation fighter jet was designed to destroy all air, ground, and water surface targets, monitor the airspace at major distances from deployment bases, make adversary headquarters, command posts and control systems inoperative. The jet can engage a broad range of guided and unguided weapons, including air-to-air short, medium and long-range missiles, smart 250, 500 and 1500 kg bombs (the latter from outside suspension). Maximum combat payload is 10 tons. The aircraft stealth characteristics are boosted by the construction shape, as well as reflecting and absorbing coating materials and electronic warfare means. Some weapons are hidden inside the aircraft to decrease signature. The measures weaken the reflected signal and deviate it from the source. Adversary radar does not receive the information on the location and speed of the aircraft. Low signature in the optical band is ensured by digital (pixel) camouflage painting of the aircraft. Su-35S (Flanker-E+ by NATO classification) is a deeply upgraded super maneuverable multirole fighter jet of generation 4++ designed with fifth-generation technologies. It is distinguished by a new digital avionic complex, a new radar with passive phased antenna array and increased number of simultaneously tracked and attacked targets, plasma-ignition engines of increased capacity and controllable vectoring. The engines correspond to fifth-generation power plants as they develop supersonic speed without a boost. Su-35S is a deep upgrade of Su-27. It has a reinforced glider and no frontal horizontal fins and brake flap. Braking at landing is done by turning rudders to different sides. The jet is armed with a 30mm GSh-30-1 gun and carries short, medium and long-range missiles, antiship Kh-31, Kh-35U or Kh-59M missiles, as well as various precision and unguided munitions.

title: Russia for the first time displays Su-57 jet at China show
text: MOSCOW, November 11. /TASS/. Russia for the first time displays the export option of Su-57 fifth-generation fighter jet abroad at the Airshow China in Zhuhai. Rostec CEO Sergey Chemezov believes it will impress visitors and specialists. "Su-57 is the only fifth-generation fighter jet that has confirmed its breakthrough characteristics in all combat engagement options. The fighter jet is distinguished by a high survivability due a low signature and modern onboard defense complex. The aircraft will continue to be upgraded according to combat engagement experience. Su-57 together with Su-34 and Su-35S are the wings of our victory. I am sure the debut of Su-57 export option in Zhuhai will be worthily assessed by foreign colleagues," he said. Su-57 will demonstrate aerobatics at the show. Su-57 multirole fifth-generation fighter jet was designed to destroy all air, ground, and water surface targets, monitor the airspace at major distances from deployment bases, make adversary headquarters, command posts and control systems inoperative. The jet can engage a broad range of guided and unguided weapons, including air-to-air short, medium and long-range missiles, smart 250, 500 and 1500 kg bombs (the latter from outside suspension). Maximum combat payload is 10 tons. The aircraft stealth characteristics are boosted by the construction shape, as well as reflecting and absorbing coating materials and electronic warfare means. Some weapons are hidden inside the aircraft to decrease signature. The measures weaken the reflected signal and deviate it from the source. Adversary radar does not receive the information on the location and speed of the aircraft. Low signature in the optical band is ensured by digital (pixel) camouflage painting of the aircraft.

title: Russian servicemen raise flag in Makarovka — official
text: MELITOPOL, November 11. /TASS/. Russian troops have raised the Russian flag in Makarovka in the Donetsk People's Republic, Vladimir Rogov, chairman of the Russian Civic Chamber’s commission on sovereignty, patriotic projects and veteran support, reported. "Active and decisive actions of our assault units of the 60th Independent Motorized Rifle Red Banner Brigade, which is part of the 5th Guards All-Volunteer Army of the Eastern Military District, knocked out the Banderovites from Makarovka. Russian soldiers raised the Russian flag in this settlement," he wrote on his Telegram channel. Rogov specified that Makarovka is located in the Vremya direction, slightly north of Staromayorskoye and Urozhaynoye.
mil_units: 60th Independent Motorized Rifle Red Banner Brigade

title: Defense Ministry reports destruction of 96 UAVs over Russian regions
text: MOSCOW, November 11. /TASS/. The Russian Defense Ministry reported the repulsion of a massive strike on the country's civilian infrastructure on Sunday, with 96 Ukrainian drones destroyed and intercepted over the country's subjects. The department emphasized that most of the drones were eliminated over the Moscow Region. The agency reported that 23 Ukrainian UAVs were intercepted and destroyed throughout last night, including 17 over the Bryansk Region, three over the Rostov Region, two over the Belgorod Region, and one over the Kursk Region. In addition, from 7:00 a.m. to 10:00 a.m. Moscow time (4:00-7:00 a.m. GMT), air defenses intercepted and destroyed 70 drones, including 34 over the Moscow Region, 14 over the Bryansk Region, seven each over the Oryol and Kaluga regions, six over the Kursk Region, and two over the Tula Region. In addition, at around 9:25-11:00 p.m. (6:25-8:00 p.m. GMT), two Ukrainian drones were shot down over the Belgorod Region and one over the Kursk Region.

title: Four civilians wounded after Ukraine’s shelling attack on Gorlovka in DPR
text: DONETSK, November 10. /TASS/. Four civilians were injured as a result of Ukraine’s shelling attack on the city of Gorlovka in the Donetsk People’s Republic (DPR), the city’s mayor Ivan Prikhodko said. "The number of Gorlovka residents who were wounded after the shelling attack by Ukraiian terrorist amounts to four," he wrote on his Telegram channel.

title: Ukraine attacks Russian civilian infrastructure facilities with 84 drones — top brass
text: MOSCOW, November 10. /TASS/. Ukrainian troops staged a massive attack on Russian civilian infrastructure facilities with the use of 84 long-range drones, all of the were shot down or jammed, the Russian defense ministry said. "In the nighttime and in the morning on November 10, Ukrainian troops staged a massive attack on civilian facilities in Russia," the ministry said. "In all, Ukraine used 84 long-range fixed-wing drones. Russian air defense and electronic warfare systems operated routinely. All aerial targets were shot down or jammed," it said.

title: Russia’s TOS-1A Solntsepek heavy flamethrower hits Ukrainian troops’ shelter
text: MOSCOW, November 10. /TASS/. The crew of a TOS-1A Solntsepek heavy flamethrower used thermobaric munitions to wipe out a Ukrainian troops’ shelter, the Russian defense ministry said. "The crew of a TOS-1A Solntsepek heavy flamethrower of Russia’s Battlegroup North struck a shelter of Ukrainian militants with the use of thermobaric munitions, eliminating the enemy manpower," it said and posted a relevant video.

title: Russian forces continue offensive in Kursk region, repelling 11 enemy counterattacks
text: MOSCOW, November 10. /TASS/. Russia’s Battlegroup North continued its offensive in the Kursk Region, striking 10 enemy brigades and repelling 11 Ukrainian counterattacks near Daryino and Novaya Sorochina, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. Over the past day, the Ukrainian army sustained over 290 casualties and lost 29 pieces of military hardware, including five tanks, three IVFs, seven armored combat vehicles, three mortars, an electronic warfare system and 10 motor vehicles. Also, three enemy soldiers surrendered. The Russian Defense Ministry put the number of Ukraine’s casualties since its Kursk incursion at over 31,090, adding that the enemy has so far lost 194 tanks, 126 IFVs, 107 APCs and 1,102 armored combat vehicles. The Russian warplanes, artillery and missile troops continued to strike concentrations of Ukrainian troops and military hardware in Russia’s borderline region of Kursk as well as reserve enemy troops in Ukraine’s Sumy Region.

title: What we know about massive Ukrainian drone attack on Russian regions
text: MOSCOW, November 10. /TASS/. As many as 70 drones were downed over six Russian regions on Sunday morning, with almost half of them in the skies over the Moscow Region, Russia’s Defense Ministry reported. TASS has collected the key facts about the situation. - Moscow Mayor Sergey Sobyanin reported the destruction of the first two drones targeting Moscow, in the Ramenskoye municipal district, at 8:37 a.m. Moscow time (5:37 a.m. GMT). - Also, air defenses destroyed drones in the municipalities of Domodedovo and Kolomna in southeast Moscow. - The mayor said as many as 32 drones targeting the Russian capital had been downed. - The Russian Defense Ministry put the number of drones downed over the Moscow Region at 34. - Two cars caught fire from falling debris in Sofyino in the Ramenskoye municipal district. - A 52-year-old woman was injured in the Moscow Region in a drone attack targeting the Russian capital. She has been hospitalized with moderate burns to her face, neck and hands. She is currently in intensive care, Moscow Region Governor Andrey Vorobyev said. - Domodedovo and Zhukovsky airports in southeast Moscow suspended service at 8:03 a.m. Moscow time (5:03 a.m. GMT), the Russian air transport regulator, Rosaviatsiya, said. - From 9:45 a.m. Moscow time (6:45 a.m. GMT), restrictions on the operation of Sheremetyevo Airport in northwest Moscow were imposed for safety reasons, too. - So far, all restrictions to flight departures and arrivals to the three airports have been lifted. - As many as 36 flights were diverted to alternative airports while the operation of the three Moscow airports was suspended. - Between 7:00 a.m. and 10:00 a.m. Moscow time (4:00 a.m.-7 a.m. GMT), 70 Ukrainian unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) were intercepted and destroyed, including 34 over the Moscow Region, 14 over the Bryansk Region, seven over the Oryol Region, seven over the Kaluga Region, six over the Kursk Region, and two over the Tula Region, the Defense Ministry reported. - Overnight, air defenses downed 23 Ukrainian UAVs in the skies over four Russian regions, the ministry said. Namely, 17 drones were downed over the Bryansk Region, three over the Rostov Region, two over the Belgorod Region, and one over the Kursk Region, the ministry specified.

title: Russian Defense Ministry reports liberation of Volchenka in DPR
text: MOSCOW, November 10. /TASS/. Russia’s Armed Forces have liberated the village of Volchenka in the Donetsk People’s Republic (DPR), the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "Units from the Battlegroup Center have liberated the locality of Volchenka in the Donetsk People’s Republic," the ministry said. Russia’s Battlegroup West has inflicted casualties on six Ukrainian brigades in the Kharkov Region and the Donetsk People’s Republic (DPR) in the past day, wiping out roughly 490 enemy soldiers, the Russian Defense Ministry said in a daily report. Also, the enemy lost two armored combat vehicles, three pickup trucks, two US-made howitzers, a D-30 howitzer and an Anklav-N electronic warfare system, the ministry said. Three field ammo depots were destroyed, it added. Ukrainian troops sustained roughly 640 casualties from Russia’s Battlegroup South which pushed deeper into enemy defenses in the past day, the Russian Defense Ministry said. According to it, five Ukrainian brigades in the Donetsk People’s Republic were hit. The enemy lost a US-made M113 APC, a Kozak armored combat vehicle, four motor vehicles, a Gvozdika self-propelled howitzer, a D-30 howitzer, three US-provided howitzers and three ammo depots. Ukrainian casualties in the zone of responsibility of Russia’s battlegroups North and Dnepr totaled almost 100 over the past day, according to the Russian Defense Ministry. "Ukrainian casualties [in the zone of responsibility of the Battlegroup Dnepr] amounted to roughly 50. <…> Ukrainian casualties [in the zone of responsibility of the Battlegroup North] neared 50," the ministry said in a report. The enemy also lost four motor vehicles, a Bogdana self-propelled artillery mount, two US-supplied howitzers, three D-30 howitzers and an ammo depot, the ministry added. Ukrainian armed forces suffered roughly 460 casualties and lost a drone production facility as a result of actions by the Battlegroup Center in the past day, the Russian Defense Ministry said. The Russian battlegroup hit 10 Ukrainian brigades in the Donetsk People’s Republic and repelled 10 counterattacks, with the enemy losing a tank, a US-made M113 APC, three motor vehicles, an UR-67 demining vehicle, a D-20 howitzer, a Gvozdika self-propelled artillery mount, three D-30 howitzers and a Rapira anti-tank gun, the ministry specified. An enemy drone production facility was wiped out, too. Russian air defenses downed three Hammer bombs, one HIMARS rocket and 36 Ukrainian fixed-wing drones in the special military operation zone in the past day, Russia’s Defense Ministry said in a daily report. Overall, the Russian Armed Forces have destroyed 648 Ukrainian warplanes, 283 helicopters, 35,512 unmanned aerial vehicles, 585 surface-to-air missile systems, 19,159 tanks and other armored combat vehicles, 1,487 multiple rocket launchers, 17,568 field artillery guns and mortars and 28,135 special military motor vehicles since the start of the special military operation, the ministry reported. Ukraine sustained roughly 105 casualties in the zone of responsibility of Russia’s Battlegroup East which struck three Ukrainian brigades in the Donetsk People’s Republic (DPR) and the Zaporozhye Region in the past day, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. As Russia repelled four Ukrainian counterattacks, the enemy also lost a Kozak armored combat vehicle, five motor vehicles a US-made HIMARS rocket launcher and two Polish-provided Krab self-propelled howitzers, the ministry added.
weapons: special military motor vehicles since the start of the special military operation

title: Air defenses down 70 drones over six Russian regions on Sunday morning
text: MOSCOW, November 10. /TASS/. As many as 70 drones were downed over six Russian regions this morning, with almost half of them in the skies over the Moscow Region, Russia’s Defense Ministry reported. "Between 7:00 a.m. and 10:00 a.m. Moscow time (4:00 a.m.-7 a.m. GMT) on November 10, an attempt by the Kiev regime to conduct a terrorist attack against facilities on Russian soil using fixed-wing UAVs was thwarted. Air defense forces on duty intercepted and destroyed 70 Ukrainian unmanned aerial vehicles, including 34 over the Moscow Region, 14 over the Bryansk Region, seven over the Oryol Region, seven over the Kaluga Region, six over the Kursk Region, and two over the Tula Region," the ministry specified.

title: Russia downs 23 Ukrainian drones over four regions last night
text: MOSCOW, November 10. /TASS/. Overnight, air defenses downed 23 Ukrainian unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) in the skies over four Russian regions, Russia’s Defense Ministry reported. "Last night, an attempt by the Kiev regime to conduct a terrorist attack against facilities on Russian soil using fixed-wing UAVs was thwarted. Air defense forces on duty intercepted and destroyed 23 Ukrainian UAVs, including 17 over the Bryansk Region, three over the Rostov Region, two over the Belgorod Region, and one over the Kursk Region," the ministry specified.

title: Ukraine loses over 300 troops in borderline Kursk area over past day — top brass
text: MOSCOW, November 9. /TASS/. The Ukrainian armed forces have lost over 300 servicemen in the borderline Kursk area over the past 24 hours, the Russian Defense Ministry reported on Saturday. "Over the past 24 hours, the Ukrainian army’s losses amounted to over 300 personnel, two infantry fighting vehicles, an armored combat vehicle, six mortars, two electronic warfare stations and four motor vehicles," the ministry said. The Ukrainian army has lost more than 30,800 troops since its incursion into Russia’s borderline Kursk Region, including over 300 soldiers in the past 24 hours, the Russian Defense Ministry reported on Saturday. "Over the period of fighting in the Kursk frontline area, the enemy has lost more than 30,800 troops, 189 tanks, 123 infantry fighting vehicles, 107 armored personnel carriers 1,095 armored combat vehicles, 833 motor vehicles and 262 artillery weapons," the ministry said in a statement.

title: Russian air defenses destroy two Ukrainian drones over Bryansk Region
text: BRYANSK, November 9. /TASS/. Russian air defense systems destroyed two Ukrainian unmanned aerial vehicles (UAV) in the Bryansk Region, Regional Governor Alexander Bogomaz said on his Telegram channel on Saturday. "On-duty air defenses intercepted and destroyed two fixed-wing UAVs in the airspace over the Bryansk Region," he stated. There were no casualties or destruction and emergency services are working at the scene, the governor added.

title: Fifty Ukrainian drones downed over Russian regions overnight
text: MOSCOW, November 9. /TASS/. Air defense systems eliminated 50 Ukrainian drones over Russian regions overnight, the Russian Defense Ministry said. "During the night the attempt by the Kiev regime to carry out a terrorist attack using unmanned aerial vehicles on sites on the territory of the Russian Federation were thwarted. On-duty air defense capabilities eliminated and intercepted 50 Ukrainian drones, including 28 drones over the Bryansk Region, 12 drones over the Kursk Region, four drones over the Novgorod Region, two drones over the territory of the Smolensk Region, two drones over the Tula Region, and one drone over each of the Orlov and Tver regions," the ministry said.

title: Air balloon capable of increasing FPV drones’ range to 40 km to be tested in combat zone
text: MOSCOW, November 8. /TASS/. Russian specialists will test in the special operation zone an air balloon carrying a communication system for unmanned aerial vehicles capable of increasing the range of FPV-drones up to 40 km, the product’s engineer who introduced himself to TASS by his call sign Cat Behemoth said. "The balloon is made of low-pressure polyethylene and equipped with a video camera. Tests are scheduled for the next two months and will take place in a rear area of the special operation in cooperation with the Archangel drone operators' school. The balloon is planned to be used to distribute a signal from a repeater so that drone operators can see farther and the vehicles can fly farther. Theoretically, the balloon is capable of increasing the range of drone operation up to 70 kilometers, but practically the distance should be up to 40 kilometers, since our servicemen have no need to fly further," the engineer said. In the future the balloon will operate from a rear area of the special military operation. "The balloon is planned to be used from a rear area of the special operation: it will distribute the signal beyond the zero line. The balloon’s material is invisible to radars. The spherical shape is another feature that makes the balloon hard to detect," the specialist said.

title: EU must achieve unity to survive — Borrell
text: BUDAPEST, November 8. /TASS/. The European Union must achieve unity among its member states to survive, EU High Representative Josep Borrell said upon his arrival at the informal EU summit in Budapest. "The only solution for Europe's survival is unity," he stated. According to Borrell, the EU must develop its own capabilities, including in defense. "We would not be able to do this if we are divided. The EU’s mission is to shape the European Union into a force that is capable of action," he added.

title: Putin, Russian Security Council discuss anti-terrorist transport security
text: MOSCOW, November 8. /TASS/. Russian President Vladimir Putin led a briefing session with permanent members of the Russian Security Council. "We routinely discuss issues related to strengthening protective measures against terrorist threats to various facilities. At this session, we will focus on efforts to ensure anti-terrorism security for transport and transportation infrastructure," the Russian leader said as he opened the meeting. Putin then handed the floor to Minister of Transport Roman Starovoit and Interior Minister Vladimir Kolokoltsev for their reports. Among the participants in the meeting were also the speakers of the Russian Federation Council and the State Duma, Valentina Matviyenko and Vyacheslav Volodin; Russian Security Council Deputy Chairman Dmitry Medvedev; Presidential Administration Head Anton Vaino; Security Council Secretary Sergey Shoigu; Presidential Aide Nikolay Patrushev; Defense Minister Andrey Belousov; Russian Foreign Intelligence Service (SVR) Director Sergey Naryshkin; and Special Presidential Envoy for Environmental Protection, Ecology, and Transport Sergey Ivanov.

title: Stupinets fast attack craft to be handed over to Russian Navy in 2025 — source
text: MOSCOW, November 8. /TASS/. The Project 12418 Stupinets fast attack guided missile vessel constructed at the Vympel Shipyard in Rybinsk is scheduled to be handed over to the Russian Navy in 2025, a source in the shipbuilding industry told TASS. "The first of the two fast attack missile boats being constructed in Rybinsk is set to be delivered to the Russian Navy in 2025," he clarified. TASS has not received an official confirmation of this information. The source noted that the two ships were originally under construction in Rybinsk for a foreign customer. Following the cancellation of the purchase, the Russian Navy expressed interest in the boats. They were promptly reclassified from under construction to under repair in order to install the necessary weapons for the Russian Navy. The main strike weapon of the Project 12418 fast attack missile boat is the Uran anti-ship missile system fitted with Kh-35 missiles. The new ship succeeded the name of the Project 1241, which operated in the Caspian Flotilla until 2023. Stupinets is expected to join the Caspian Flotilla.

title: Organization of Turkic States has no plans to establish joint army yet — Erdogan
text: ANKARA, November 8. /TASS/. At the current stage, the Organization of Turkic States (OTS) has no plans to form a joint armed force, Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan told his pool reporters following his visits to Kyrgyzstan and Hungary. "It is too early to discuss it at this stage. There are no such plans now. There are no initiatives or efforts among the OTS states to establish joint armed forces in the future. The [OTS] agreement on a civil defense mechanism is an important step toward expanding security cooperation in our region. It will strengthen coordination and enhance our countries' response to natural disasters and emergencies. The main purpose of this agreement is to strengthen solidarity among the members of the organization in addressing emergencies," the Anadolu news agency quoted Erdogan as saying. The Turkish leader also noted that "issues of cooperation in education and technology transfer" are under discussion within the OTS. "All of this may contribute to building deeper security ties. These processes take time and depend on various political, economic, and social circumstances. In our era, the importance of alliances, unions, and international organizations has increased significantly. Such steps to enhance solidarity strengthen the power of organizations," Erdogan added.

title: Russia’s latest TOS-3 flamethrower to have firepower exceeding effect of several air bombs
text: MOSCOW, November 8. /TASS/. Russia’s latest TOS-3 Dragon heavy flamethrower will feature firepower exceeding the effect of several ODAB-500P air bombs, the press office of the state tech corporation Rostec said on Friday. Over the period of the special military operation in Ukraine, the Solntsepyok and Tosochka heavy flamethrower systems have evolved in terms of their increased firepower, striking range, protection and automation, the press office said on its Telegram channel. "The Solntsepyok burns out an area of four hectares by a full salvo while the Tosochka already scorches a territory of six hectares. The efficiency can be compared to the effect of Iskanders [tactical missile systems]. A full salvo by the new heavy flamethrower system, which is a follow-up of the weapons that have proven their worth, exceeds the effect of several heavy air bombs, for example, ODAB-500Ps by its striking power. This is also because the new system will make it possible to strike multilayered targets like fortifications spread over a large area more effectively," the press office said in a statement. Rostec Armament, Munition and Special Chemistry Cluster Industrial Director Bekkhan Ozdoyev told TASS earlier that the corporation was developing a new TOS-3 tracked heavy flamethrower system. The first experimental vehicle had already been developed, he said. According to reports, the TOS-3 will feature an increased striking range. The Omsk Plant of Transport Machine-Building (Omsktransmash) filed an application in February for registering the TOS-3 Dragon trademark.

title: No casualties or damage from downed drones in Voronezh Region — official
text: VORONEZH, November 8. /TASS/. No casualties or damage were caused by falling debris from six downed unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) in the Voronezh Region last night, Governor Alexander Gusev reported on his Telegram channel. "According to preliminary data, there were no casualties or destruction in the region," he wrote. The official noted that last night, a Russian air defense system intercepted six UAVs in the skies over the Voronezh Region.

title: Russian tech firm to unveil 5th-generation engine for combat aircraft at Airshow China
text: MOSCOW, November 8. /TASS/. Russia’s United Engine Corporation (UEC, part of the state tech corporation Rostec) will unveil the fifth-generation engine for combat aircraft at Airshow China in Zhuhai, the corporation’s press office reported on Friday. "A full-size mock-up of the latest engine for operational/tactical aircraft will be the main premiere. It noticeably outshines its predecessors in terms of its endurance, fuel efficiency and thrust and is actually the fifth-generation engine. The improved AL-31FN series 5 engine will be shown to foreign partners for the first time. The use of the next-generation powerplant will considerably boost the aircraft’s altitude and flight range capabilities," the press office said in a statement. Russia will also demonstrate a mock-up of the SM-100 engine for advanced unmanned aerial vehicles and combat trainer aircraft at Airshow China in Zhuhai. Visitors to the airshow will also be able to view innovations for the PD-35 high-thrust engine demonstrator with its fan exceeding three meters in dimeter: its blades will be shown in full size, it said. "We offer our strategic partners the best solutions that both boost combat efficiency and cut the life cycle cost of engine operation. For the first time, Rostec will demonstrate at the Zhuhai international airshow a renewed family of AL-31F/Al-31FN engines, including engines of the 5th series, a new SM-100 engine and a completely new fifth-generation engine for the most advanced operational/tactical aircraft," the press office quoted Rostec First Deputy CEO Vladimir Artyakov as saying. Airshow China is the country’s largest international aviation and aerospace exhibition held once every two years in Zhuhai. Russian defense enterprises constantly feature their products at Airshow China. The exhibition focuses on military and civil aircraft, space, navigation and radar equipment, airborne armament, rocket engines, fire control and air defense systems. This year, Airshow China 2024 will run on November 12-17.

title: Russia, India develop defense cooperation – Putin
text: SOCHI, November 8. /TASS/. Russia and India are developing their cooperation in the defense and security sphere, including joint research, Russian President Vladimir Putin told the plenary session of the Valdai Discussion Club. "Our contacts in the security sphere, and in the defense sphere, are developing. Look at how many types of Russian military equipment are in service with the Indian armed forces. There is a great degree of trust in this relationship. We don’t just sell our weapons to India; we design them jointly," he said. As an example, Putin named the BrahMos cruise missile project. "In fact, we made it [the missile] fit for use in three environments - in air, in sea and on land. These projects, conducted for the benefit of India’s security, are ongoing," he said. "This is widely known and no one has issues with that, but these projects demonstrate the high level of mutual trust and cooperation. So this what we will keep doing in the near term, and, I hope, will continue to do in the more distant future.".

title: Russian military blocked two Ukrainian groups of 15,000 troops in total — Putin
text: SOCHI, November 8. /TASS/. Russian President Vladimir Putin said that about 15,000 Ukrainian troops had been blocked by the Russian military in two areas of the Kupyansk direction. "I don’t know whether our military has already announced it or not, but there are two areas in the Kupyansk direction where Kiev troops are blocked. One area is practically in an encirclement. About 10,000 Ukrainian troops are blocked on the bank of a water reservoir. In another area near Kupyansk, about 5,000 troops are blocked," Putin told Thursday’s plenary session of the Valdai Discussion Club. "They are already in encirclement and are trying to create pontoon crossings to evacuate at least some of the troops, but they are destroyed by our artillery almost immediately," he added. "There are also two or three zones where [Kiev troops] are blocked in battlegroup Center’s zone of responsibility," he said. "There certainly are two at this point, and possibly, the third one will emerge soon." "The Ukrainian military is certainly aware of these developments, but decisions that are being made on the political level are not intended to benefit the Ukrainian state, let alone the people of Ukraine," the Russian leader said.

title: Russia ready to supply Su-57E fifth-generation fighter to foreign partners — UAC
text: MOSCOW, November 7. /TASS/. The United Aircraft Corporation (UAC, part of the state tech corporation Rostec) is ready to supply the Su-57E fifth-generation multirole fighter jet to foreign partners, the UAC press office said in the run-up to China Airshow 2024 in Zhuhai that will feature the fighter’s export version. "The UAC is constantly increasing Su-57 production volumes and the Corporation is ready to offer this aircraft to its foreign partners. The presence of a fifth-generation fighter in Russia is an indicator of technological sovereignty and the high level of science and technology of the country. It is symbolic that exactly 10 years ago, the Su-35 was shown at this same exhibition [Airshow China in Zhuhai]. Since then, the aircraft has found its customers in many countries around the world," the press office said in a statement. The Su-57E cutting-edge fifth-generation multirole fighter jet will be presented for the first time abroad. The aircraft will take part in the airshow’s flight demonstration program while visitors will also be able to see the aircraft at the show’s static display, it specified. "The Corporation's stand will also be dedicated to the Su-57. Visitors will be able to ‘fly’ an interactive Su-57E simulator using virtual reality technologies," the press office added. Airshow China is the country’s largest international aviation and aerospace exhibition held once every two years. Russian defense enterprises constantly feature their products at Airshow China. The exhibition focuses on military and civil aircraft, space, navigation and radar equipment, airborne armament, rocket engines, fire control and air defense systems. This year, Airshow China 2024 will run on November 12-17.

title: Russian missile-armed Ufa submarine makes call to Indonesian port
text: MOSCOW, Nov. /TASS/. 7. The Pacific Fleet’s Ufa submarine, armed with Kalibr missiles, is making a call to the Indonesian port of Surabaya, the fleet said in a statement. It is escorted by the rescue tug Alatau. "While at the Indonesian port, Russian sailors will hold a number of formal events such as meetings with representatives of the Indonesian Navy, friendly volleyball matches, and will visit local attractions," the statement said. The Ufa is heading from the Baltic Sea to join the Pacific Fleet. Earlier, a flotilla of Russian naval ships including the corvettes Gromky, Rezky and Hero of the Russian Federation Aldar Tsydenzhapov called at Surabaya for the first joint exercise of Russian and Indonesian warships, which is called Orruda-2024. The Ufa submarine was put into service with the Russian Navy on November 16, 2022. It is the fourth of six Project 636.3 submarines that are planned to be built for the Pacific Fleet. Project 636.3 - or Varshavyanka-class - submarines are the third generation of diesel-electric submarines and are considered to be some of the world’s most silent. The submarines are 74 meters long, with a maximum displacement of more than 3,900 tons. The robust hull allows the submarine to dive to a working depth of 240 meters and a maximum depth of 300 meters. It has a range of up to 7,500 miles. The submarines are armed with the Kalibr-PL missile system.

title: IAEA experts to remain at Zaporozhye nuke plant as long as necessary, chief says
text: VIENNA, November 7. /TASS/. International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) experts will remain at the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant (ZNPP) for as long as necessary, IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi said in a statement. "We will stay <...> for as long as it is needed to help avert the threat of a nuclear accident that could have serious consequences for human health and the environment," the statement reads. According to Grossi, the nuclear safety and security situation remains highly challenging at the ZNPP. The IAEA team "has continued to hear frequent explosions, some distance away from the ZNPP" but no damage to the facility has been reported, he added.

title: CSTO Secretary General warns of risks of political destabilization via Internet
text: MOSCOW, November 7. /TASS/. The Collective Security Treaty Organization (CSTO) points out the risk of Internet being used for destabilization of the situation in various countries, CSTO Secretary General Imangali Tasmagambetov said during the meeting of Security Council Secretaries in Moscow. "The mass production and the absence of clear regulations for the use of deadly autonomous weapon systems, capable of hitting targets on the ground, in the air and in water without human participation," he listed. "The active use of the Internet for disruption of internal political stability, damaging economic, military and other interests of other countries, the intensification of space military activity." The Secretary General also noted that such challenges to international stability as rapidly growing utilization of AI for military purposes continue to intensify. According to the official, the CSTO takes measures to preserve stability in Eurasia and to ensure the security of CSTO member states.

title: Threat of terrorism spreading in Central Asia persists — CSTO chief
text: MOSCOW, November 7. /TASS/. The threat of terrorism spreading in the Central Asian Region remains, but the Collective Security Treaty Organization (CSTO) continues to maintain control, CSTO Secretary-General Imangali Tasmagambetov stated at a meeting of CIS security council secretaries in Moscow. "The threat of terrorist activity, illegal immigration, and the trafficking of weapons and drugs in the Central Asian region remains," he said. "Despite the severity of these issues, we are able to maintain control within this area of CSTO's responsibility," he said. The secretary-general noted that the organization continues to monitor challenges and threats stemming from the Afghan hub and coordinates efforts to address them through the CSTO Foreign Ministers Council's working group on Afghanistan. Representatives from the CIS Anti-Terrorism Center and the CIS Border Troops Commanders Council's coordination service also participate in these efforts.

title: CSTO concerned about situation in Eastern Europe, sees risks in South Caucasus
text: MOSCOW, November 7. /TASS/. The Collective Security Treaty Organization (CSTO) notes the great conflict risks in a number of regions adjacent to the organization's area of responsibility and is concerned about the situation in Eastern Europe, as well as sees tangible risks of destabilization in the South Caucasus, the Secretary-General of the Collective Security Treaty Organization (CSTO) Imangali Tasmagambetov said at a meeting of secretaries the CIS countries’ security councils in Moscow. "The military and political situation along the perimeter of the CSTO's zone of responsibility remains complicated. In a number of regions directly adjacent to the CSTO zone of responsibility, the conflict potential remains at a high level. The situation in Eastern Europe and the Middle East is of serious concern. The internal political processes in Afghanistan remain complicated, and tangible risks linger in the South Caucasus," Tasmagambetov said. He thanked Russian Security Council Secretary Sergey Shoigu for the invitation to the meeting. "The relevance of such a discussion platform is very difficult to overestimate," he added.

title: NATO concentrates information warfare forces on Russia, Belarus — CSTO Secretary-General
text: MOSCOW, November 7. /TASS/. The North Atlantic Alliance is concentrating armed forces and information warfare centers on the border of Russia and Belarus, the Secretary-General of the Collective Security Treaty Organization (CSTO), Imangali Tasmagambetov, said at a meeting of the secretaries of the CIS countries’ security councils in Moscow. "The concentration of NATO armed forces and information warfare centers on the borders of Belarus and the Russian Federation is continuing," he pointed out. "The strategic situation worsened after Finland and Sweden joined the North Atlantic Alliance," Tasmagambetov pointed out. At the same time, the CSTO secretary general noted that the West was increasing its military and financial support for Ukraine in order to continue the conflict with Russia. Tasmagambetov drew attention to the fact that the number of serious incidents at nuclear power plants had surged up, which was fraught with "catastrophic consequences for a vast territory, including outside the combat zone."

title: IAEA chief warns of ongoing danger at Zaporozhye NPP amid continued fighting
text: ROME, November 7. /TASS/. The Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant (NPP) remains at risk due to ongoing fighting and continuous drone attacks, International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Director General Rafael Grossi said in an interview withLa Stampanewspaper. "The Zaporozhye NPP area remains a conflict zone. Many believe that the situation there has stabilized, but unfortunately, it has not. Fighting continues in the area, and drones are still flying, so the highest level of danger alert remains," Grossi said. The IAEA chief is convinced that cooperation for the safety of the Zaporozhye NPP and other NPPs must continue. "I have to maintain dialogue with both the Russians and the Ukrainians," he added. The Zaporozhye NPP, located in Energodar, has six power units with a total capacity of 6 GW. It is Europe’s largest nuclear power generating facility. At the end of February 2022, the facility was taken over by Russian forces. Since then, Ukrainian army units have periodically shelled both residential areas in Energodar and the plant itself using drones, heavy artillery, and multiple rocket launchers. All six units of the plant are in cold shutdown and are not generating electricity. The plant has the necessary personnel to ensure safe operation, with equipment maintained in accordance with all required regulations under strict control of radiation and nuclear safety standards and under the supervision of IAEA experts. In early September 2022, a Grossi-led IAEA team visited the Zaporozhye NPP. Afterwards, several agency staff members remained at the station as observers. The mission serves on a rotational basis, and its task is to observe and assess the operational and physical safety of the Zaporozhye NPP.

title: Over 10 UAVs destroyed over Russia’s borderline Voronezh Region
text: VORONEZH, October 3. /TASS/. Russian air defenses on duty wiped out more than 10 unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) over the borderline Voronezh Region, regional Governor Alexander Gusev said on hisTelegramchannel on Thursday. "Air defenses on duty in the Voronezh Region eliminated over 10 drones in the airspace over three regional districts. According to preliminary information, there have been no casualties or damage," he wrote. The governor wrote that the threat of more UAV attack in the region had remained.

title: US-led coalition aircraft violate Syrian airspace seven times in past day
text: MOSCOW, November 6. /TASS/. The US-led coalition’s aircraft violated Syria’s seven times during the past day, Oleg Ignasyuk, deputy chief of the Russian Center for Reconciliation of the Opposing Parties in Syria (a division of the Russian defense ministry), said. "Aircraft of the so-called international anti-terrorist coalition led by the United States continue to create dangerous situations that may cause air accidents or incidents and escalate the situation in Syria’s airspace," he said. "Three pairs of the coalition’s F-15 and one MQ-1C multi-role unmanned aerial vehicle violated Syria’s airspace in the al-Tanf area, across which international air routes run, seven times during the day.".
mil_orgs: Russian Center for Reconciliation of the Opposing Parties in Syria

title: Over 3,700 people cross into Syria from Lebanon in past day — Russian military
text: MOSCOW, November 6. /TASS/. More than 3,700 people entered the Syrian territory from Lebanon in the past 24 hours, Oleg Ignasyuk, deputy chief of the Russian Center for Reconciliation of the Opposing Parties in Syria (a division of the Russian defense ministry), said. "Over the past day, as many as 3,714 people crossed Syria’s border from Lebanon via the checkpoints of Al-Arida, Al-Jawasiyeh, Al-Dabousiyah, Jisr Qamar, and Jdaidet Yabous," he said. He also said that the Russian reconciliation center conducted one humanitarian operation in the settlement of Hatla in the Deir ez-Zor governorate and distributed 2.9 tons of food among its residents.
mil_orgs: Russian Center for Reconciliation of the Opposing Parties in Syria

title: Mi-28NM helicopter eliminates Ukrainian outpost in Kursk Region with aviation missiles
text: MOSCOW, November 6. /TASS/. A Mi-28NM strike helicopter eliminated Ukrainian personnel and an outpost in the border area of the Kursk Region, the Russian Defense Ministry reported. "The army aviation Mi-28NM helicopter crew, working within a mixed tactical group, hit personnel and a Ukrainian outpost in the border area of the Kursk region with aviation missiles. A forward aviation spotter confirmed the elimination of the target," the ministry said. The Ministry added that the pilots fired missiles at coordinates, provided by the spotter on the ground. After the strike, the pilots fired flares and performed a missile evasion maneuver.

title: Russian troops ensure safe rotation of IAEA observers at Zaporozhye nuke plant — top brass
text: MOSCOW, November 5. /TASS/. Russian troops and the National Guard ensured the safe rotation of the mission of observers from the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) at the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant, the Defense Ministry of Russia reported on Tuesday. "The Russian Armed Forces jointly with units of the National Guard ensured the next safe rotation of the mission of observers from the International Atomic Energy Agency at the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant. A ceasefire strictly observed by all Russian servicemen was declared from 7:00 a.m. to 7:00 p.m. Moscow time along the entire route of travel from the designated place of transit - the destroyed bridge near the settlement of Kamenskoye in the Zaporozhye Region to ensure the safety of IAEA observers," the ministry said in a statement. Meanwhile, Ukraine’s military command attempted to endanger the life and safety of IAE experts and disrupt their rotation at the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant, the ministry said. "Before the movement began, Russian army engineers surveyed the terrain for the presence of unexploded Ukrainian ordnance. On the eve of the next rotation of IAEA observers at the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant, the Ukrainian armed forces heavily shelled the roadway section, along which the mission travels to the station’s territory. The bombardment severely damaged the roadway. By its actions, the Ukrainian military command attempted to endanger the life and safety of experts and disrupt their rotation," the ministry said. Russian troops ensured the safe movement of the column through the damaged section of the roadway and the delivery of experts to the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant, it said. "Four mission inspectors have arrived at the station. Their goals are to monitor and assess the safety of the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant. IAEA experts have been present at the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant since September 1, 2022 after the first visit by IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi to the station," the ministry said. The Zaporozhye NPP is Europe’s largest nuclear power plant.

title: Russian Defense Ministry presents slideshow of Ukrainian plot to capture Kursk nuke plant
text: MOSCOW, November 5. /TASS/. The Russian Defense Ministry has presented a slideshow from a report by the State Service of Ukraine for Emergency Situations plotting to capture the Kursk Nuclear Power Plant (Kursk NPP). The documents were presented during a briefing by Chief of Russia’s Radiation, Chemical and Biological Protection Troops Lieutenant General Igor Kirillov. Ukraine planned to engage 20,000 troops, 27 tanks, 50 units of armored hardware, 30 units of tube artillery and five multiple launch rocket systems. Kirillov also added that Ukrainians planned that only Russian territory would be affected by radiation in the event of an accident at the nuclear facility.
mil_orgs: State Service of Ukraine for Emergency Situations

title: Ukraine’s Security Service carrying out sabotage activities against ZNPP employees
text: MOSCOW, November 5. /TASS/. Ukraine’s Security Service (SBU) is conducting sabotage and terrorist activities targeting employees of the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant (ZNPP) and continues attacks on its satellite city of Energodar, Chief of Russia’s Radiation, Chemical, and Biological Protection Troops Lieutenant General Igor Kirillov said. "Meanwhile, attacks on industrial facilities and the satellite city of Energodar, home to the plant’s personnel, continue. Ukraine’s Security Service is carrying out sabotage and terrorist activities, including against the ZNPP and local officials. The city’s residents are living under psychological pressure and blackmail. Threats have been made against the families of the plant’s employees who reside in Kiev-controlled territories," he said. He recalled that shelling attacks on the nuclear plant by Ukrainian troops had been of little concern for the global community because experts from the University of Cambridge had believed that contamination risks were "relatively low." "But the radiation situation forecast made by our specialists, later confirmed by European sectoral organizations, showed that radioactive emissions in the event of an accident would affect virtually all of Europe. Only after this, and following the deployment of IAEA experts to the plant, did shelling attacks on it practically stop," he stressed. According to Kirillov, Ukrainian troops staged 49 artillery and drone attacks on the Zaporozhye NPP’s facilities in 2022. The attacks targeted nuclear reactors, nuclear fuel and waste storage facilities, as well as other critical infrastructure. The plant was disconnected from external power supplies five times after these attacks, which posed the risk of a man-made disaster.

title: Kiev strikes on Kursk NPP may cause Chernobyl, Fukushima-like disasters — Russian military
text: MOSCOW, November 5. /TASS/.
